title: Rapid handback concierge coverlist — surge ops quicksheet
preview:
Purpose: lightweight spreadsheet to track manual handbacks and concierge coverage during short unplanned surges. Use as single-pane to coordinate fast swaps and to record unresolved handoffs for follow-up.

How to use (quick):
- Primary owner: person responsible for the route during the surge window.
- Backup: person to escalate to if primary is overloaded or unreachable.
- Last handback: date the route was returned to normal owner or 'pending'.
- SLA impact: quick flag (none / minor / major) to prioritize.
- Notes: short context, who was paged, and temporary mitigations.
- Triage column: action items for next 24h.

Quick totals (snapshot 2035-04-12): 11 routes listed, 4 pending handbacks, 3 major SLAs flagged.

-- TABLE (paste to spreadsheet or keep as reference) --
Columns: Region | Route ID | Surface | Primary owner | Backup | Last handback | SLA impact | Notes | Active jira
1) NA | route-frontend-east | public-gen | Marcus Lin | Priya Shah | 2035-04-11 | none | brief throttle added, increase batch-size 1.2x | JIRA-OPS-4568
2) EU | embeddings-v2 | embeddings | Priya Shah | Zoe Ramirez | pending | minor | KV cache warmup ongoing, spikes at 2am CET | JIRA-OPS-4570
3) APAC | private-vpc-01 | private-deploy | Zoe Ramirez | Ethan Morris | 2035-04-10 | none | routing fallback active to ded-3 | JIRA-OPS-4559
4) NA | rerank-route-b | rerank | Ethan Morris | Marcus Lin | pending | major | sudden latency regression after canary; rollback in flight | JIRA-OPS-4572
5) EU | streaming-chat-eu | chat | Priya Shah | Asha Patel | 2035-04-09 | minor | transient error 502 on gateway, circuit breaker set | JIRA-OPS-4561
6) NA | opt-cache-legacy | cache | Asha Patel | Priya Shah | pending | none | manual prefix cache purge scheduled 04/13 | JIRA-OPS-4569
7) EU | dedicated-burst-1 | dedicated | Marcus Lin | Ethan Morris | 2035-04-08 | none | burst policy increased temporarily | JIRA-OPS-4540
8) APAC | model-quant-eval | optimize | Ethan Morris | Zoe Ramirez | pending | minor | quality rollback flagged, holding traffic on canary | JIRA-OPS-4575
9) NA | inference-edge-3 | edge | Zoe Ramirez | Marcus Lin | 2035-04-12 | minor | edge node OOM, restarted, monitoring | JIRA-OPS-4576
10) EU | console-sync | console | Asha Patel | Priya Shah | 2035-04-07 | none | partial metrics gap; ingest re-queued | JIRA-OPS-4533
11) NA | fallback-router-x | routing | Priya Shah | Ethan Morris | pending | major | capacity exhaustion in us-west-2; active contingency route to us-west-1 | JIRA-OPS-4579

Open action items (owners):
- Marcus: coordinate with infra to confirm burst capacity until 04/15. (due 2035-04-13)
- Priya: follow up with embeddings team for root-cause timeframe; log synthetic test. (due 2035-04-13)
- Ethan: finish rollback verify for rerank-route-b and confirm SLA windows. (in progress)
- Zoe: monitor edge node and post heap dumps to ticket if recurrence.

Notes to self / shorthand (Asha):
- If a 'major' SLA is flagged, escalate via #ops-priority and ping @oncall-sre.
- Keep this sheet clipped to day-of-surge; archive snapshot to Confluence using page "Surge Concierge Snapshots" with timestamp.
- Quick handback checklist: confirm stable for 30m, notify owner + infra, update route owner in console, mark last handback date.

Unresolved questions:
- Do we want a separate 'owner burn rate' column? (helps measure load) — quick vote in thread.
- Should backups be mandatory for all customer-facing routes? (current coverage gaps highlighted)

Quick links / references:
- Surge runbook: https://confluence.redwood.ai/display/OPS/Surge+Concierge+Runbook
- Oncall rota: https://confluence.redwood.ai/display/OPS/Oncall+Rota

Small retrospective after surge: capture in comments: what held, what needed more automation (prefetch / cache TTL tuning / automatic fallback), any handback friction (who didn't get notified).

End of doc.

(Dirty/working copy — keep short notes here, move to formal tracker later if this persists across >3 surges.)

title: Compact cross-team quick utilization and surge cost sampler (Jun 2029)
preview:
Purpose:
A compact, one-page spreadsheet-style sampler to quickly estimate near-term (1 week) contributor-hours and incremental cost if we need to absorb a 10–30% traffic surge across critical runtime integration touchpoints. Meant for quick cross-team alignment during ad-hoc ops/capacity standups — not a formal capacity model.

How to read: columns are conservative midpoints; surge hours are incremental work above baseline. Cost uses a blended hourly rate where applicable (SWE/Infra: $95/hr, SRE/Oncall: $70/hr, SE/SEs: $85/hr, PM/others: $65/hr).

Table: (rows = teams / ownerships)
Team | Primary Owner | Est weekly baseline hours | SLA priority (H/M/L) | Probable surge? (Y/N) | Surge est hours (10–30%) | Blended $/hr | Est baseline $ | Est surge $ | Notes / blockers
Serving Runtime - CPU | Ethan Park | 120 | H | Y | 18 | $95 | $11,400 | $1,710 | Waiting on compiled kernel rollout; manual patching needed for small fleets
Serving Runtime - GPU | Diego Alvarez | 200 | H | Y | 40 | $105 | $21,000 | $4,200 | Burst queue tuning required; potential spot instance add cost not included
Platform Orchestration | Priya Patel | 80 | H | Y | 12 | $95 | $7,600 | $1,140 | Autoscaler cooldown adjustments; registry lock risks
Observability (traces/metrics) | Anika Roy | 50 | M | Y | 8 | $85 | $4,250 | $680 | Retention increases will add cloud storage cost separately
Customer Success (on-call escalation) | Luis Gomez | 30 | M | N | 0 | $65 | $1,950 | $0 | Triage playbooks ready; expect modest volume
Solutions Eng / SEs | Priya Patel | 60 | M | Y | 9 | $85 | $5,100 | $765 | Extra pre-sales demos/support may be needed for high-value customers
Private Deployments (VPC) | Arjun Mehta | 40 | H | Y | 6 | $95 | $3,800 | $570 | VPN/key rotation coordination with customers is slow
Console & Rollouts | Maya Chen | 30 | H | Y | 6 | $85 | $2,550 | $510 | Canary dashboard refreshes, rollout gating checks
Product / PM coordination | Samira Khan | 20 | M | N | 0 | $65 | $1,300 | $0 | Customer comms ready, no extra hours planned
Total (sheet quick-sum) |  | 630 |  |  | 99 |  | $58,950 | $9,575 | Quick-sum delta for 10–30% surge midpoint

Quick notes on assumptions and omissions:
- Surge est hours are conservative midpoints (approx 15% of baseline on high probability rows). If surge hits upper bound, multiply surge est by 2–3x.
- This sampler does NOT include: cloud infra additional run cost (e.g., GPU spot/ondemand delta), storage retention billing, third-party licencing spikes, or detailed on-call escalation overtime multipliers.
- Blended $/hr uses role-weighted averages; for precise cost accounting pull charge numbers from Finance (cost-center mapping).

Mini-analysis:
- Biggest incremental dollar exposure in short window is GPU runtime team; if kernel rollout fails we have both increased staff-hour needs and potential extra cloud spend for temporary capacity.
- Platform orchestration and console teams are low-effort mitigations (tuning autoscaler + rollout gating) but require immediate coordination to prevent cascading retries.
- Observability retention is a low hourly cost but may create visible billing delta if retention windows are increased; treat as separate bucket.

Immediate ask (24–72h):
1) Serving Runtime GPU (Diego) — estimate time to enable an aggressive burst pool and a backstop checklist for automated rollback by EOD +1 (owner: Diego).
2) Platform Orchestration (Priya) — propose autoscaler cooldown override and canary policy tweak; document PR to config repo and link (owner: Priya).
3) Console / Rollouts (Maya) — add a high-visibility rollout watch channel and two engineers on 2-hour rotation for first 72h if surge observed (owner: Maya).
4) Finance sync (Maya + Ethan) — get 48h rough cloud spend estimate for expected GPU burst scenarios (owner: Maya).

Follow-ups and checkpoints:
- Daily quick-check in ops standup (09:30 PT) for next 5 business days.
- Update this sampler after first 24h with actual observed incremental hours and update Est surge $ column.

Open questions / decision points:
- Do we want automated cost caps (hard limits) on GPU burst pools, or prefer manual throttle (ops intervention)? (decision needed: product/infra leadership)
- If a customer-facing incident arises, do we prioritize uptime (accept higher cost) or mitigate spend immediately? (product + CS to align)

Version notes:
- Draft v0.1 — created 2029-06-17 by Maya Chen. Edits/inputs from Ethan, Priya, Diego incorporated 2029-06-18 to 2029-06-20.

Quick changelog (edits):
- 2029-06-18: added Platform Orchestration row and updated autoscaler note (Priya).
- 2029-06-19: refined GPU surge hours after runtime sync (Diego).
- 2029-06-20: added immediate ask list and finance sync (Maya).

Short-term risk mitigation checklist (tick when done):
- [ ] Deploy canary with reduced batch size to 10% of traffic
- [ ] Open GPU burst pool runbook and verify rollback steps
- [ ] Notify top-10 customers about potential degraded performance window
- [ ] Create temporary alert threshold for cost burn (Finance + Infra)

If you're reading this and want to propose changes, add a new row with your name and numbers and ping @mchen on Slack.

title: Shortform capacity lensed rollcall (Nov 2028)
preview:
Purpose: quick, small-form cross-functional roster to spot and temporarily reassign coverage gaps across product, infra, SRE, SE, and CS when a sprintless or ad-hoc surge appears.

How to use: paste rows into a lightweight sheet during pre-launch scrubs or weekend handoffs. Keep it intentionally small (<= 12 entries). Update 'WeeklyAvailableHrs' as reality changes.

Immediate observables: 1) EU evenings currently thin for customer onboarding (Maya primary but out mid-Nov), 2) KV cache perf concern may affect heavy-prefix workloads (tariq).

Open actions:
- Priya: confirm PLAT-71 smoke test completion and publish results (due 2029-01-16).
- Maya: provide exact blackout dates for ACME pilot (due 2028-11-06).
- Tariq: attach perf sample and short repro to INFRA-322 (due 2028-11-05).

Notes-to-self / friction flags:
- This doc intentionally mixes roles for fast lookup; do not promote as canonical roster (Confluence capacity playbook is canonical).
- Keep linked_artifacts pruned to 3 items to reduce noise.

Revision history: created 2028-11-02 by Priya; minor adjustments 2028-11-09 (Ethan availability corrected), 2029-01-15 (general tidy).

title: Micro bridge coverage affinity sampler
preview:
Purpose:
Quick ad-hoc spreadsheet to sample cross-team availability and affinity for short-term coverage (2–4 week horizon). Use when we need an immediate list of plausible owners for a specific surge, rollout weekend, or one-off customer event. Not authoritative — treat as sampler, not schedule.

Notes to self:
- Keep this small and actionable (8–12 rows max).
- Capture both 'affinity' (willingness/skill) and 'practical availability' (hours/week).
- Mark clear next action (ask, confirm, block) per row.
- Surface any gating tickets or compliance needs in notes.

Columns:
- Function/Team | Owner | Role | Primary contact (slack/email) | Backup | Affinity (1–5) | Est. avail (hrs/week) | Typical shift window (PST/UTC) | Current commitments (short list) | Notes & gating items | Action required | Last checked

Rows (sample):
1) Platform SRE | Priya Nair | Oncall rotation lead | @priya / priya@redwood.ai | @ops-backup / ops-backup@redwood.ai | 5 | 12 | Weekdays 08:00–16:00 PST / 16:00–00:00 UTC | Week 37 oncall, infra migration prep | Can cover infra fallbacks; needs update to runbook (RUN-988) | Confirm runbook edits and handoff by 2028-09-10 | 2028-09-08
2) Serving Runtime | Ethan Park | Runtime perf engineer | @ethan / ethan@redwood.ai | @ravi / ravi@redwood.ai | 4 | 8 | Weekdays 10:00–18:00 PST | Model kernel optimizations PR open (PR #128) | Happy to triage runtime regressions; prefers daytime window | Ping on PR status + confirm 2-hr overlap during event | 2028-09-08
3) Product Ops | Marisol Rivera | Incident coordinator | @marisol / marisol@redwood.ai | @sofia / sofia@redwood.ai | 5 | 18 | Flexible (can cover US/EMEA overlap) | Running launch checklist for Route A | Can coordinate cross-team comms; will maintain incident doc | Draft incident run cadence and confirm leads by 2028-09-11 | 2028-09-09
4) Solutions Eng | Sofia Alvarez | SE lead (enterprise) | @sofia / sofia@redwood.ai | @andrew / andrew@redwood.ai | 3 | 6 | Weekdays 07:00–12:00 PST | Customer pilot support (low-latency) | Will own customer escalation lane; needs customer zoom + creds updated | Share escalation playbook + confirm F-up person | 2028-09-07
5) Infra (Cloud) | Ravi Kumar | Network & infra | @ravi / ravi@redwood.ai | @michelle / michelle@redwood.ai | 4 | 10 | Weekdays 06:00–14:00 PST | Capacity fragility tests scheduled | Can provide emergency capacity increases if given 30m notice; KMS gating for private deploys | Validate KMS policy with security before granting perms | 2028-09-08
6) Customer Success | Elena Park | CSM (strategic) | @elena / elena@redwood.ai | @juan / juan@redwood.ai | 2 | 4 | Weekdays 09:00–17:00 PST | Customer QBRs this week | Limited bandwidth but available for customer-facing comms | Identify primary CS contact per customer and add to incident doc | 2028-09-06
7) Console & Tooling | Andre Gomez | Observability owner | @andre / andre@redwood.ai | @kevin / kevin@redwood.ai | 4 | 8 | Weekdays 11:00–19:00 PST | Dashboard retention changes rolling out | Can adjust alert thresholds and run ad-hoc queries | Pre-stage critical dashboards and set temp paging rule | 2028-09-08
8) Security & Compliance | Naomi Li | Security reviewer | @naomi / naomi@redwood.ai | @security-oncall / sec-oncall@redwood.ai | 3 | 2 | Weekdays 08:00–12:00 PST | SOC review scheduled | Minimal hands-on but must sign off on emergency key access | Need pre-approved emergency KMS list before event | 2028-09-05

Quick analysis (raw):
- Immediate coverage plausible if we secure 2 daytime overlaps (Platform SRE + Product Ops + Runtime).
- Biggest gating items: runbook updates (RUN-988), KMS sign-off, and PR #128 readiness for runtime fallback.
- Customer-facing lane weak (CS availability low) — consider reassigning a Solutions Eng to co-own customer comms during event.

Recommended next steps (this doc is the sampler):
- Circulate to owners and ask for "confirm/decline" by EOD 2028-09-10. Use template message in Confluence linked above.
- Product Ops to create the incident channel and post the table snapshot (read-only) 24 hours before the event.
- Platform SRE / Infra to triage RUN-988 and validate runbook entries; mark done in this sheet.
- Security to provide pre-approved KMS emergency list and add to linked artifacts.

Open questions / TODOs:
- Who owns escalation to legal for regulated customers? (likely CS + Security — need a named owner)
- Confirm whether Dedicated capacity can be burstable to avoid infra pre-scaling (ask infra).
- Decide if we need an A/B routing fallback to cheaper model variant (runtime + model ops).

Meta:
- This is intentionally lightweight and intended for ad-hoc pre-event scrubs. For recurring needs, move to the canonical 'coverage roster' in Confluence and create a weekly sync.

Raw change log:
- 2028-09-07: created initial sampler (Marisol)
- 2028-09-08: added Platform SRE and Runtime rows (Priya, Ethan)
- 2028-09-09: updated Product Ops availability and finalized recommended next steps (Marisol)

title: Sparrowboard — quick experiments coverage & owner tally
preview:
Table (rows are lightweight entries; columns: id | experiment | primary-ask | owner | coverage-tier | est-effort-h | blocker | quick-mitigation | success-metric | linked-ticket)

1 | small-model-quant-safety-check | confirm quantized model parity on 512 tokens | Maya S. | tier-B (oncall fallback) | 6h | infra env flakiness | run on dedicated GPU pool, reduce batch to 1 | 0.5% delta in rouge/LLM-quality on eval-set | JIRA-RE-2147
2 | embedding-cache-ttl-probe | validate 15m vs 60m cache hit % and cost delta | Leo C. | tier-C (passive) | 3h | missing perf traces | attach tracer snippet + synthetic load | +10% hit, -8% cost | JIRA-RE-2152
3 | rollout-minor-tokenizer-fix | ensure no decode regressions for emojis | Priya D. | tier-A (manual gate) | 2h | unclear test coverage | quick test harness: 200 emoji-heavy prompts | 0 regression count | n/a (internal)
4 | routing-fallback-latency-sample | sample end-to-end p99 across eu/us for fallback routes | Rafael O. | tier-B | 8h | transient region quota | force small synthetic load and sample 1h | p99 < 450ms fallback | JIRA-RE-2169
5 | cost-blitz-embedding-downscale | quick experiment: use smaller-dim embeddings for search pilot | Ethan P. | tier-C | 5h | evaluation dataset size small | use stratified sampling + holdout | 95% recall vs baseline | https://github.com/redwood-inference/research/issue/112
6 | canary-stream-conn-cap | sanity-check streaming session limits on dedicated nodes | Maya S. | tier-A | 4h | ephemeral session drop during warmup | warmup script + slow ramp | <0.2% session drop | JIRA-RE-2171

Notes-to-self: quick updates only here; do not replace canonical runbooks. This sheet is intentionally terse. Use it to decide: 1) immediate owner for ad-hoc ops; 2) whether to escalate to SRE or keep as product experiment; 3) rough ETA for next sync.

Open questions: 
- Which experiments require a compliance review (PII extraction risk)? Priya to flag by EOD.
- Can infra provide a 2-hour burst slot for routing-sample (Rafael asks)? Slack #infra-booking.

Quick formulas/heuristics used (documented inline): 
- coverage-tier mapping: tier-A = manual gate + stakeholder review, tier-B = auto fallback + owner watch, tier-C = passive metric sample only.
- est-effort-h is a 50% buffer on engineer-only work (includes test+cleanup).

Actionable next steps (short):
- Maya: run canary-stream warmup script + paste raw sample (due 2028-04-04).
- Leo: attach cache hit graphs (ask observability for 24h snapshot) by tomorrow.
- Priya: quick emoji harness output + note on compliance flag by EOD.
- Rafael: request burst slot via infra, link ticket here when confirmed.
- Ethan: collate all experiment outputs into single short slack thread: #ops-sparrowboard.

Review cadence: weekly quick-scrim (Wed 09:15 PT). If any tier-A shows regressions, escalate to product-ops leadership immediately.

Scratch/brain dump (very informal):
- Potential short follow-up: rollup into a 'mini postmortem' if any metric regresses by >1.5x.
- Keep this doc small; if it grows beyond 8 rows, split per domain (routing / embeddings / serving).

Last quick edits: reduced verbosity, moved long logs to linked-ticket comments.

title: Micro stakeholder burn + availability census (ad-hoc)
preview:
Purpose:
This is a quick, shareable sheet to capture current owner availability windows and a very small 'burn' estimate (hrs/week) for ad-hoc cross-functional touchpoints tied to the upcoming runtime canary. Not intended as a long-term roster — temporary snapshot for the next 6 business days.

Context and assumptions:
- Trigger: unexpected canary capacity oscillation observed 2028-02-13; ops asked for lightweight coverage planning for 2028-02-15 through 2028-02-22.
- Burn estimate = expected weekly triage + follow-ups for this incident lane (hours/week). Very coarse, rounded to nearest half-hour.
- Availability windows are local TZ where possible; prefer PST for cross-team alignment.
- Priority: 1 = must-have coverage, 2 = helpful, 3 = optional standby.

Quick table (rows = owners / teams):
Stakeholder | Team | Primary contact (email) | Avail window (PST) | Priority | Est burn (hrs/week) | Coverage notes | Action needed | Current owner
Engineering - Serving Runtime | eng-serving-runtime | ethan.park@redwood.com | 06:00-14:00 PST | 1 | 12 | Primary lane for model fallbacks and kernel rollbacks. On-call overlap required 08:00-12:00. | Confirm canary rollback trigger thresholds; verify fallback route mapping. | Ethan Park
SRE - Platform | site-reliability-engineering | maya.patel@redwood.com | 08:00-16:00 PST | 1 | 8 | Can manage infra autoscaling / node reprovision. Needs playbook link. | Prep autoscale knobs and escalation matrix. | Maya Patel
Product - Hosted API | product-hosted-api | laura.chen@redwood.com | 09:00-17:00 PST | 2 | 4 | Customer comms + routing policy alignment. Low hands-on. | Draft 1-paragraph status for customers if issue persists. | Laura Chen
Solutions Eng | solutions-engineering-and-professional-services | jun.sato@redwood.com | 05:30-13:30 PST | 2 | 6 | Customer-facing triage for key accounts (3). Needs config snapshots. | Pull recent request traces and share with SEs. | Jun Sato
Observability | engineering-observability | rachel.nguyen@redwood.com | 07:00-15:00 PST | 1 | 5 | Dashboards + trace alert tuning. Could reduce false positives. | Create temporary filter for canary job IDs. | Rachel Nguyen
Platform Engineering - Batching | engineering-platform-engineering | miguel.torres@redwood.com | 11:00-19:00 PST | 3 | 3 | Batching profile adjustments might be low-impact but helpful off-hours. | Standby for batch param tweaks if throughput drops. | Miguel Torres
Customer Success | customer-success | ana.silva@redwood.com | 09:30-17:30 PST | 2 | 2 | Account check-ins for customers on Dedicated tier. | Identify top 5 impacted customers and pre-approve messaging. | Ana Silva
Security & Compliance | security-and-compliance | rohit.patel@redwood.com | 06:00-14:00 PST | 3 | 1 | Minimal involvement; confirm no infra change conflicts. | Quick green-check on planned config changes. | Rohit Patel

Mini totals and heuristics:
- Headcount captured: 8 people/roles.
- Total estimated burn (sum): 41 hrs/week.
- Weighted priority score (sum of priority values): 15. Lower is better; here indicates multiple must-haves.
- Coverage ratio heuristic = (sum of priority-1 owners' hours) / total hours = (12 + 8 + 5) / 41 = 25/41 ≈ 0.61 — indicates ~61% of burn is from priority-1 owners.

Open questions / quick decisions to make:
- Q1: Do we need a dedicated 2-hour cross-team sync between 09:00-11:00 PST tomorrow? (pro: alignment; con: costs extra burn). Recommend: 09:30-10:00 standup only.
- Q2: Is a temporary rollback policy (auto-fallback to model-v1a) pre-approved? PRODOPS-412 holds the draft policy; need sign-off from Eng leads.

Action tracker (small):
- Ethan: confirm rollback thresholds and paste values in this doc (due 2028-02-15 08:00 PST).
- Maya: stage autoscale ramp at 30% for canary cluster and validate 5-min cool-down (due 2028-02-15 07:30 PST).
- Rachel: add filter for canary job IDs and mute noisy alert for 48 hours (due 2028-02-15 06:00 PST).
- Jun: send config snapshots for top-3 customer accounts (due 2028-02-15 10:00 PST).
- Laura: draft customer status note, route to CS and SE for approval (due 2028-02-15 11:00 PST).

Short notes to self (incomplete, rough):
- Might need to spin a temporary dedicated pool if throughput drop persists > 3 hours — check cost vs. SLA risk.
- If burn exceeds 60 hrs/week combined, escalate to on-call manager to add a weekend rota.
- Consider brief A/B fallback: route 10% traffic to cheaper quantized variant for 2 hours window to test stability.

Versioning and who edited:
- v0.1 (2028-02-14): initial capture (Laura).
- v0.2 (2028-02-15): added SE rows + action times (Ethan, Jun).

Where to find supporting artifacts:
- Canary traces & logs: link in linked_artifacts (confluence page).
- Rollback policy draft: PRODOPS-412.
- Platform PR for quick autoscale changes: https://github.com/redwood-inference/platform/pull/983

If you are added as an owner and your availability is wrong, please update your Avail window cell and ping @product-ops in Slack.

End of sheet.

title: Short-run cross-team coverage matrix (Apr 2027)
preview:
Purpose: quick, lightweight tracker for 2-week coverage gaps, escalation ownership, and short-lived mitigations. Not intended to replace the main rota; this is a temporary, cross-team slice for Apr 10–24, 2027.

Table (columns): Area | Primary | Backup | PD Window | Expected SLA / SLO | Current risk | Open follow-ups / owner | Notes / next steps

Rows:
API - Hosted | Marcus Liu | Priya Natarajan | 09:00–18:00 PT | 99.9% / 150ms p95 | Medium | JIRA-OPS-5589 (Marcus) | Degression experiments running; fallback route configured (route-b). Verify canary tomorrow.
Dedicated - GPU Pool | Diego Alvarez | Sofia Martinez | 24/7 (escalation only) | 99.95% / 120ms p95 | High | GH-987 (Diego) | N+1 node in prod offline; using burst nodes. Need procurement ETA for replacement GPUs.
Private Deploys (major customers) | Priya Natarajan | Aisha Khan | 08:00–20:00 PT | Contracted SLA varies | Medium | CONFLUENCE-ACC-42 (Priya) | Customer A has elevated error rate on quantized models; SE engaged.
Infra - Networking | Tomas Becker | Marcus Liu | 24/7 (oncall) | 99.99% backbone | Low | NET-233 (Tomas) | Short latency blip 04/09 resolved; monitor for recurrence.
SRE - Incident Mtg | Olivia Chen | Diego Alvarez | Daily 11:00 PT | N/A | Medium | INC-2027-0410 (Olivia) | Running escalation drills next Tue; invite lists updated.
Customer Success - Escalations | Aisha Khan | Olivia Chen | 09:00–17:00 PT | SLA per contract | Medium | CS-1173 (Aisha) | Two open escalations: feature parity issue and logging access.

Quick counts: total areas tracked: 6 | high-risk: 1 | medium-risk: 4 | low-risk: 1

Daily check steps (short checklist to paste into Slack each morning):
- Confirm primaries on duty for the day (update PD window if swapped)
- Check alert inbox (pagerduty & shared email) for unresolved pages >30m
- Verify any temporary capacity (burst nodes) still available
- Call out customer escalations requiring cross-team input

Handoff checklist (when swapping shifts):
- Confirm outstanding action items (link tickets)
- Confirm any ongoing mitigations (e.g., traffic ramp-down, canary controls)
- Share last 6 hours of error-rate graphs and top noisy endpoints (link to dashboard)
- Add your notes to the 'Notes / next steps' column

Action items (next 72h):
- Diego: confirm GPU procurement ETA and post to this sheet (due 2027-04-13) -> GH-987
- Marcus: run nightly canary with smaller shard and report p95 delta (due 2027-04-12) -> JIRA-OPS-5589
- Priya: coordinate SE for Customer A and request increased logging retention for debug (due 2027-04-14) -> CONFLUENCE-ACC-42
- Olivia: run escalation drill 2027-04-13, circulate notes and updated invite list

Open questions / unresolved:
- Should Dedicated GPU Pool have a temporary capacity SLA addendum for affected customers? (ask legal/product)
- Is the current fallback route sufficient for sustained 5% traffic surge? (engineering/serving runtime)

Notes-to-self / rough ideas (Oliver’s scratchpad):
- Consider short-term reserved burst (48–72h) from partner pool to cover next 2 weeks and avoid customer-visible degradation.
- If procurement delayed >7 days, schedule customer-level communications and possible credits.

History / provenance: created initially as an emergency ad-hoc sheet during Apr 2027 capacity squeeze. Iteration 1 shared in #ops-coverage Slack, prompted by Marcus' post. This doc is a compact distillation for cross-functional standups and rapid handoffs.

Last updated by Olivia Chen 2027-04-12 09:08 PT.

Links referenced:
- Incident dashboard: https://grafana.redwood.example/d/ops/incident-summary
- PD schedule: https://pagerduty.com/schedules/redwood/ops
- Procurement ticket: https://procure.redwood.example/tickets/PR-4123

Scratch comments (inline, frank):
- \"Burst nodes are not a long-term fix — coordinate with infra-capacity for a plan B\"
- \"CS: escalate customer A logging retention asap; current logs drop after 24h which is insufficient\"

End of sheet content.

- Marcus: run nightly canary with smaller shard and report p95 delta (due 2027-04-12) -> JIRA-OPS-5589

- Is the current fallback route sufficient for sustained 5% traffic surge? (engineering/serving runtime)

Notes-to-self / rough ideas (Oliver’s scratchpad):
- Consider short-term reserved burst (48–72h) from partner pool to cover next 2 weeks and avoid customer-visible degradation.
- If procurement delayed >7 days, schedule customer-level communications and possible credits.

- \"CS: escalate customer A logging retention asap; current logs drop after 24h which is insufficient\"

End of sheet content.

title: Ask Garden: Backyard Briefing Q1 2027
preview:
Purpose:
This is a compact, lightly curated synthesis of customer threads we incubated during the last six weeks (Dec 2026 - Jan 2027). Goal is to surface the smallest set of low-to-medium effort probes that could materially reduce customer pain or unblock higher-priority Dedicated/Private deals. This is intentionally bootstrappy: notes, raw quotes, and quick triage rather than polished recommendations.

Snapshot (what we looked at):
- 48 support tickets flagged by CS as latency/consistency-related between 2026-12-01 and 2027-01-10.
- 7 partner-run performance audits (2 hosted API, 5 Dedicated).
- 12 CS call transcripts and three on-site research calls.
- Early telemetry: a non-trivial cohort (approx 12 customers) with repeated short-seq heavy-write patterns (chat systems, code assistants).

Quick summary (topline):
- Customers are seeing two consistent friction families: 1) tail latency spikes under mixed small/long sequences (often after KV cache misses), and 2) unpredictable billing spikes tied to retry/timeout behavior.
- Many customers know about batching/quantization knobs but want safer, opinionated defaults and easy rollback.
- There's appetite for a focused ‘runtime stabilization’ offering (small, config-driven changes + clear observability) that could be sold as a Professional Services + config bundle for Dedicated customers.

Representative customer quotes (verbatim snippets):
- "We get weird 10-20s tails randomly during peak — sometimes an hour goes fine then boom, everything stutters." — large SaaS search team.
- "Our infra team is okay to tune things, but we want a recommended safe profile we can enable without fear." — fintech engineering lead.
- "Billing jumped last week, looks like retries + long completions. Hard to tell whether to blame prompt or runtime." — product manager, conversational agent.

Key themes and tangible asks (ranked by frequency + impact):
1) Tail-latency & cache-friction (high frequency, high impact):
   - Ask: Improve KV cache hit diagnostics (expose simple per-customer hit/miss rate and a sample of evicted keys).
   - Why: Customers cannot tell whether latency is prompt-induced or runtime cache thrash.
   - Suggested probe: Add a 7-day per-tenant cache hit/miss metric in Console + one-click export of 50 example keys that missed (scrubbed).
   - Rough effort: Eng infra spike (2-3 wks) + Console UI card.

2) Retry & billing visibility (medium frequency, high customer anxiety):
   - Ask: Break down chargeable token usage by retry/timeout vs successful completions in billing UI.
   - Why: Teams are seeing surprising charges and escalate to finance.
   - Probe: Add billing flagging for sessions with >3 retries in 24h and automated alert to tenant admins.
   - Rough effort: Billing + backend tagging (1-2 sprints).

3) Safe default profiles & rollback (high frequency, medium impact):
   - Ask: Provide several vetted runtime profiles (low-latency, cost-saving, conservative) with an immediate rollback toggle.
   - Why: Many customers are comfortable with recommendations but fear being locked into a suboptimal config.
   - Probe: Ship 3 profiles in Dedicated console behind a feature flag for early adopters. Include A/B telemetry.
   - Rough effort: Product + Console + docs (2-4 wks).

4) Lightweight canary/gradual rollout for models & quantized variants (low frequency, medium impact):
   - Ask: Make a simple percentage-based rollout mechanism in Console for model variants with automatic fallback to previous variant on error rate spike.
   - Why: Prevents whole-tenant regressions and gives confidence to try cheaper quantized models.
   - Probe: Canary gating that checks 95th latency and total error rate; auto-fallback policy.
   - Rough effort: Serving-runtime + Console + policy glue (multi-team; ~1.5 months).

5) Prompt vs runtime differentiation playbook (internal docs need):
   - Ask: Customer-facing checklist and playbook that CS/SE can send customers to triage whether slow completions are prompt- or infra-driven.
   - Why: Reduces basic churn and repetitive support cycles.
   - Probe: Draft one-pager + triage flow we can A/B test in three accounts.
   - Rough effort: Low (2-3 days).

Prioritized micro-probes (minimum viable experiments):
- Probe A (Fast wins): Add a "Cache Health" card in Console for top 10 tenants by traffic. Show hit/miss, eviction rate, recent TTLs, and two suggested actions (increase max-kv, enable prefix caching). Owner: Liam O'Neill. Timebox: 2 weeks.
- Probe B (Billing clarity): Add retry-tagging in billing export CSV with a sample dashboard showing sessions with >2 retries. Owner: Sofia Martinez. Timebox: 3 weeks.
- Probe C (Profiles alpha): Expose a single recommended profile ("latency safe") with a rollback button; ship to 5 pilot tenants. Owner: Daniel Kim. Timebox: 4 weeks.

Risks, unknowns, and tradeoffs:
- Privacy: Exporting sample keys must be scrubbed — need legal signoff for any key/content exports.
- Cost telemetry fidelity: Tagging retries vs successful completions introduces potential accounting drift; finance/ops must validate.
- Engineering bandwidth: Runtime work competes with scheduled model-variant rollout features; both need serving-runtime attention.
- False positives: Auto-fallback policies must avoid flip-flopping on normal variance; need hysteresis and cooldown windows.

Suggested near-term communications:
- Share this note with eng-serving-runtime on Wed 2027-02-09 (sync request: 30min).
- Circulate a short CS one-pager for pilots with three tenants (ask for permission to enable profiles).
- Open two JIRA spikes: JIRA-CR-901 (Cache health metrics) and JIRA-CR-902 (Billing retry tagging).

Open questions for follow-up:
- Can serving runtime expose lightweight per-tenant eviction samples without heavy perf cost? (ENG answer required)
- Is finance comfortable accepting a billing delta tolerance of up to 0.5% while we roll out retry-tagging?
- Are there privacy/contract constraints for exporting any per-request keys/samples for customers in EU regulated verticals?

Next steps (week-by-week plan):
- Week 1: Kick PoC for Cache Health card (one backend metric + Console stub). Reach out to 5 pilot tenants.
- Week 2-3: Billing tagging PoC + sample CSV export for CS review.
- Week 3-4: Ship profiles alpha behind flag to pilot tenants; collect telemetry.
- Week 5: Consolidate learnings and decide go/no-go for broader rollouts.

Owners and contact points:
- CS lead / doc owner: Priya Natarajan (priya@redwood.ai)
- Metric/Console owner: Liam O'Neill (liam@redwood.ai)
- Billing owner: Sofia Martinez (sofia@redwood.ai)
- Runtime/Product liaison: Daniel Kim (daniel@redwood.ai)

Notes to self / loose scraps:
- When drafting Cache Health, consider a lightweight sampling approach: only sample on observed latency > X ms and limit to N keys per hour.
- Short-term messaging: "Enable latency-safe profile for 24h trial; rollback instantly if 95th latency rises" — simple language for SEs.
- Keep list of pilot tenants outside product repo to avoid auto-enablement mistakes.

End of briefing.

(If this doc is useful, I will convert to a short CS playbook with checklists and an email template for pilot outreach. For now this remains a working note.)

title: Ad-hoc Convergence Grid - Sep 2026
preview:
Purpose:
Quick, low-friction grid to converge on outstanding one-off asks, cross-team handoffs, and temporary capacity adjustments for the next 2 sprints. Meant to be copied into team backlogs and updated in meeting. Not a replacement for full project plans.

How to use:
- Owner updates their row before the daily 09:30 standup.
- If something moves to a multi-week effort, link a ticket and change status to "in_review" or "escalated".
- Use the "heat" column to indicate immediate attention (H/M/L).

Column headers (flat table):
ID | Initiative / Ask | Owner (team) | Heat | ETA | Status | Blocker short | Quick next step | Est effort (days)

Rows (most recent first):
1 | Emergency routing fallback for EU region (customer outage) | Lena Rodriguez (SRE) | H | 2026-09-06 | in_progress | limited spare GPUs in eu-west-2 | spin 2 temporary nodes + reroute to us-west as fallback | 1-2
2 | Ad-hoc billing dispute export (enterprise finance) | Priya Nair (Finance) | H | 2026-09-08 | in_progress | missing request logs from API gateway | ask infra for 48h log retention snapshot | 0.5
3 | Quick quantization sanity check for p3-compatible variant | Marcus Wu (Applied ML) | M | 2026-09-14 | draft | environment mismatch for CUDA drivers | schedule 1h test with infra images | 2
4 | Customer demo dataset purge request (privacy) | Jordan Kim (Customer Success) | H | 2026-09-07 | in_progress | legal needs confirmation on retention policy | legal lead to confirm PII scope by EOD | 0.25
5 | Temporary routing policy for low-latency tier during conference | Ethan Park (Product Ops) | M | 2026-09-12 | planned | capacity reserve not finalized | finalize commit with Dedicated team | 1
6 | Quick eval: streaming chunk-size perf check | Anya Feldman (Observability) | L | 2026-09-18 | draft | no representative replay data | pull last 24h sample traces | 1
7 | Small docs refresh: public quickstart snippet for private deploys | Samir Desai (DX) | L | 2026-09-20 | planned | waiting on final CLI flag naming | finalize flag name, update snippet | 0.5

Counts snapshot (manual):
- Total rows: 7
- High heat: 3
- Medium heat: 2
- Low heat: 2

Immediate actions (today):
- Lena: confirm temporary node sizes and list of services to reroute by 09:00 (blocking customer impact).
- Priya: post exact time window needed for logs to #finance-ops and tag infra.
- Marcus: attach the failing container logs to JIRA-ML-334 and request matching driver image.

Notes & context (scratchpad):
- This grid started as a 6-row sprint-of requests and keeps growing; want to avoid turning into another backlog. If an item requires >3 days effort, create a ticket and move it out.
- Cross-team handoffs that have unresolved blockers should be escalated in the weekly ops sync (Tues 15:00).
- Privacy/legal items need explicit documented sign-off; do not proceed on purge until legal reply is in thread.

Open questions / items to clarify:
- Do we have a policy for preemptively reserving 10% of Dedicated capacity for events? (Product Ops + Dedicated team decision)
- Can infra provide a 48h emergency log snapshot for billing disputes? If not, what's the alternative?
- Should small docs updates be owned by DX or Product Docs? (Samir flagged ambiguous ownership)

Changelog (brief):
- 2026-09-05: grid created (Ethan)
- 2026-09-06: Lena added EU routing row after incident
- 2026-09-07: Priya updated billing row with additional blocker detail

Quick paste-friendly export (CSV-ish lines):
"1,EU fallback,Lena Rodriguez (SRE),H,2026-09-06,in_progress,limited spare GPUs,spin 2 nodes + reroute,1-2"
"2,Billing export,Priya Nair (Finance),H,2026-09-08,in_progress,missing API logs,ask infra for snapshot,0.5"
"3,Quant check,Marcus Wu (Applied ML),M,2026-09-14,draft,CUDA mismatch,schedule 1h test,2"

Notes to self: keep this doc light and time-bound. Archive to /archive/monthly when >2 weeks old unless ownership transfers to a tracked Jira ticket.

-- Ethan

title: three-threads-priority-ladder-and-allhands-rehearsal-notes-v0-1
preview:
Purpose
---
Short: converge leadership and staff on three prioritized company threads for the coming quarter, draft OKRs tied to measurable signals, and prepare an all-staff forum that surfaces tradeoffs and asks. This is a working pack for exec rehearsal and communications — not the final slide deck.

Context and framing
---
Context: After the Dedicated capacity rollout and early Private deployments, teams report rising operational complexity and mixed signals on unit economics vs. latency targets. The goal for the quarter is to focus the org on patterns that move both reliability and unit-cost simultaneously, while preserving product velocity for SDKs and Console improvements.
Why three threads: we want a clear, memorable set of priorities for an allstaff conversation. Too many bullets dilute commitment; three threads gives focus while enabling cross-functional ownership.

Proposed three threads (narrative + 60-day signals)
---
1) Predictable Throughput: make end-to-end p99 latency and throughput predictable for common routes.
   Signals (60d): p99 gen latency for hosted API < 250ms for short sequences; Dedicated pool utilization steady at > 70% without latency spikes; two customers with SLA experiments onboarded.

2) Inference Cost Control: lower per-token unit cost for high-volume workloads without degrading quality.
   Signals (60d): avg per-token cost on top-10 customers drops by 12%; fallback-variant routing reduces failed high-cost retries by 30%; Optimize recommendations shipped for top 3 models.

3) Developer Velocity + Observability: reduce friction for engineering teams to onboard, debug, and iterate on inference in production.
   Signals (60d): new SDK routes adoption to 25% of internal product repos, Console tracing onboarding +2 teams, reduction in mean time to diagnose model regressions by 35%.

Draft OKRs (top-level, for exec discussion)
---
Objective A: Make inference behavior predictable for production workloads.
  KR A1: Achieve p99 generation latency < 250ms on 3 representative hosted routes (measured over 2-week windows).
  KR A2: Demonstrate Dedicated autoscale policy that meets throughput SLO in 95% of simulated peak windows.
  KR A3: Publish per-model performance profiles for top 10 catalog models with verified telemetry.

Objective B: Reduce unit inference cost for committed workloads while preserving quality.
  KR B1: Realize 12% unit-cost reduction across the top-10 billers through batching, quantization guidance, and cache policies.
  KR B2: Ship Redwood Optimize beta with model-configuration suggestions to 5 customers and monitor uplift.
  KR B3: Implement cost-aware routing that successfully falls back to cheaper-compatible variants in 90% of eligible events.

Objective C: Improve developer experience and instrumentation to shorten feedback loops.
  KR C1: Ship SDK 2.1 with streaming improvements and reduce integration friction score in the internal survey by 20 points.
  KR C2: Add token-level tracing to Console for all hosted requests and onboard 3 product teams to use tracing in incidents.
  KR C3: Create an eval harness and baseline prompt set; detect and alert on quality regressions for two flagship models.

Risks and mitigations (quick list)
---
- Risk: Pushing cost reductions could degrade output quality.
  Mitigation: Tie quantization/batching rollouts to prompt-eval gates and Canary quality checks.
- Risk: Dedicated capacity autoscale introduces customer-visible latency during bursts.
  Mitigation: Implement temporary overprovisioning policy + traffic shaping window.
- Risk: Messaging noise if we announce 'cost reductions' prematurely.
  Mitigation: Keep external comms to case studies and measured snapshots after verification.

All-Hands plan (structure and choreography)
---
Length: 60 minutes (45min content + 15min live Q&A + asynchronous follow-up).
High-level flow: 1) CEO framing (5m) 2) Three-threads walk (15m total, 5m per thread owner) 3) Live customer vignette (5m) 4) Ops/incident-readout & tool highlights (10m) 5) Exec asks & role-specific owners (5m) 6) Live Q+A (15m)
Proposed presenters and owners: CEO (framing) - Alex Morgan; Predictable Throughput owner - Priya Rao; Cost Control owner - Ethan Clarke; Dev Velocity owner - Liam Chen; Ops vignette - Jordan Blake; Moderator - Maya Patel.
Rehearsal schedule: two run-throughs (dry: 2026-08-04 11:00-12:00 PT; full dress: 2026-08-06 09:30-10:30 PT). Tech check with AV on 2026-08-05 10:00 PT.
Audience prep: short pre-read (1 pager) + 5 quick pulse questions to be pushed 24h before. Encourage submit-ahead questions via #allhands-questions Slack thread.

Draft script highlights (bullets for speakers)
---
CEO: Open with a one-paragraph connective story (market move to serving; why it matters). Call out three threads and a single concrete ask: what teams can commit this quarter.
Thread owners: 60-second problem statement, 90-second what-success-looks-like, 90-second implementation highlights and dependencies. End with a single binary ask from leadership (approve runway, prioritize X, assign Y).
Ops vignette: show a recent incident postmortem summary in 3 slides: timeline, customer impact, remediation, and observability gaps we closed.
Moderator: surface the most common pre-submitted questions, and triage live Qs into breadcrumbs for followup owners.

Interactive elements and pulse (questions to push)
---
- Which of the three threads would your team prioritize if you could only do one this quarter? (Predictable Throughput / Cost Control / Dev Velocity)
- Rate current friction for onboarding a model to production (1-5).
- Which Console feature would most reduce your on-call time? (Tracing / Cost breakdown / Rollouts)
- Are there customer migrations that need exec attention? (free text)
- Would you volunteer one engineer for a cross-team 6-week spike? (yes/no)

Communication plan and follow-ups
---
- Publish final slide deck + 1-pager summary within 24 hours after all-hands.
- Create an action tracker with owners and 30/60/90 milestones; sync owner updates at weekly trajectory standup.
- Route product/ops asks into JIRA EPIC OKR-TRACKER and tag owners.

Immediate next steps (owners & due dates)
---
- Finalize slide deck with graphics: Maya Patel (2026-07-26).
- Pull two customer vignettes and obtain permission: Jordan Blake (2026-07-28).
- Harden rehearsal script + slides for thread owners: Priya Rao / Ethan Clarke / Liam Chen (2026-07-29).
- Draft pulse survey and Slack push content: Maya Patel (2026-08-02).
- Schedule AV and runbook for live comms: Ops (SRE) lead (2026-08-05).

Open questions for execs (please answer inline below)
---
1) Are the three threads the right level of granularity or should we split Cost Control into two KRs (batching vs routing)?
2) Approve the commitment to publish audited per-model performance profiles this quarter?
3) Are we comfortable with the proposed customer vignette(s) for public sharing?

Notes to self / meeting artifacts to add
---
- Add rehearsal timing to calendar invites with speaker cue times.
- Mark a fallback comms owner for last-minute incidents.
- Link to incident runbook for the Ops vignette in final deck.

Appendix (rough drafts for slides)
---
Slide 1: Title / one-line plan / three-threads visual.
Slide 2-4: For each thread: problem, KR targets, owner, immediate asks.
Slide 5: Customer vignette summary (timeline + impact).
Slide 6: Action tracker snapshot.
Slide 7: How to follow up: links + contacts.

End.
text:
Purpose
---
Short: converge leadership and staff on three prioritized company threads for the coming quarter, draft OKRs tied to measurable signals, and prepare an all-staff forum that surfaces tradeoffs and asks. This is a working pack for exec rehearsal and communications — not the final slide deck.

Context and framing
---
Context: After the Dedicated capacity rollout and early Private deployments, teams report rising operational complexity and mixed signals on unit economics vs. latency targets. The goal for the quarter is to focus the org on patterns that move both reliability and unit-cost simultaneously, while preserving product velocity for SDKs and Console improvements.
Why three threads: we want a clear, memorable set of priorities for an allstaff conversation. Too many bullets dilute commitment; three threads gives focus while enabling cross-functional ownership.

Proposed three threads (narrative + 60-day signals)
---
1) Predictable Throughput: make end-to-end p99 latency and throughput predictable for common routes.
   Signals (60d): p99 gen latency for hosted API < 250ms for short sequences; Dedicated pool utilization steady at > 70% without latency spikes; two customers with SLA experiments onboarded.

2) Inference Cost Control: lower per-token unit cost for high-volume workloads without degrading quality.
   Signals (60d): avg per-token cost on top-10 customers drops by 12%; fallback-variant routing reduces failed high-cost retries by 30%; Optimize recommendations shipped for top 3 models.

3) Developer Velocity + Observability: reduce friction for engineering teams to onboard, debug, and iterate on inference in production.
   Signals (60d): new SDK routes adoption to 25% of internal product repos, Console tracing onboarding +2 teams, reduction in mean time to diagnose model regressions by 35%.

Draft OKRs (top-level, for exec discussion)
---
Objective A: Make inference behavior predictable for production workloads.
  KR A1: Achieve p99 generation latency < 250ms on 3 representative hosted routes (measured over 2-week windows).
  KR A2: Demonstrate Dedicated autoscale policy that meets throughput SLO in 95% of simulated peak windows.
  KR A3: Publish per-model performance profiles for top 10 catalog models with verified telemetry.

Objective B: Reduce unit inference cost for committed workloads while preserving quality.
  KR B1: Realize 12% unit-cost reduction across the top-10 billers through batching, quantization guidance, and cache policies.
  KR B2: Ship Redwood Optimize beta with model-configuration suggestions to 5 customers and monitor uplift.
  KR B3: Implement cost-aware routing that successfully falls back to cheaper-compatible variants in 90% of eligible events.

Objective C: Improve developer experience and instrumentation to shorten feedback loops.
  KR C1: Ship SDK 2.1 with streaming improvements and reduce integration friction score in the internal survey by 20 points.
  KR C2: Add token-level tracing to Console for all hosted requests and onboard 3 product teams to use tracing in incidents.
  KR C3: Create an eval harness and baseline prompt set; detect and alert on quality regressions for two flagship models.

Risks and mitigations (quick list)
---
- Risk: Pushing cost reductions could degrade output quality.
  Mitigation: Tie quantization/batching rollouts to prompt-eval gates and Canary quality checks.
- Risk: Dedicated capacity autoscale introduces customer-visible latency during bursts.
  Mitigation: Implement temporary overprovisioning policy + traffic shaping window.
- Risk: Messaging noise if we announce 'cost reductions' prematurely.
  Mitigation: Keep external comms to case studies and measured snapshots after verification.

All-Hands plan (structure and choreography)
---
Length: 60 minutes (45min content + 15min live Q&A + asynchronous follow-up).
High-level flow: 1) CEO framing (5m) 2) Three-threads walk (15m total, 5m per thread owner) 3) Live customer vignette (5m) 4) Ops/incident-readout & tool highlights (10m) 5) Exec asks & role-specific owners (5m) 6) Live Q+A (15m)
Proposed presenters and owners: CEO (framing) - Alex Morgan; Predictable Throughput owner - Priya Rao; Cost Control owner - Ethan Clarke; Dev Velocity owner - Liam Chen; Ops vignette - Jordan Blake; Moderator - Maya Patel.
Rehearsal schedule: two run-throughs (dry: 2026-08-04 11:00-12:00 PT; full dress: 2026-08-06 09:30-10:30 PT). Tech check with AV on 2026-08-05 10:00 PT.

title: Mini handback econ roster + dollar sampler (Apr 2026)
preview:
Purpose:
Quick, ad-hoc table to surface who we loop for short handbacks and a lightweight $/effort sample per owner. Intended for product, SRE, and SEs to decide temporary coverage vs. escalations for next 7 days. Not an SLA—just a working econ sketch.

Notes about approach:
- We bucket owners by likely effort (low/med/high) for a one-week coverage window.
- Dollar sampler is back-of-envelope: assume blended fully-burdened hourly rate = $85/hr for contributor time, 40% probability-of-involvement for med, 80% for high, 20% for low.
- This spreadsheet is intentionally compact — use as tie-breaker when assigning rapid handbacks.

Roster table (rows are manual entries from quick calls):
owner,role,primary-contact,email,proximity-to-sprint,estimated-hours-week,effort-bucket,probability,sample-dollar-week,notes
Priya Natarajan,Product Ops,priya@redwood.ai,priya@redwood.ai,close,6,low,0.2,102.0,can cover async; needs SE backup for infra requests
Ethan Morales,Platform Eng,ethan@redwood.ai,ethan@redwood.ai,onsite,14,med,0.4,476.0,burst support likely day-1
Zoe Fischer,Solutions Eng,zoe@redwood.ai,zoe@redwood.ai,close,10,med,0.4,340.0,customer-facing checkins expected
Ravi Desai,Observability,ravi@redwood.ai,ravi@redwood.ai,remote,18,high,0.8,1224.0,may need SRE pairing for logs
Marta Lopez,Customer Success,marta@redwood.ai,marta@redwood.ai,close,4,low,0.2,68.0,owns customer comms

Summary metrics (computed inline):
- Sum estimated-hours-week: 52
- Raw sample-dollar-week (sum of sample-dollar-week column): 2210.0
- Average probability-weighted hours per owner: ~9.2

Interpretation / quick recommendations:
1) If we need to keep the product route stable for 7 days (high-risk window), prioritize creating a paired rota of Ethan + Ravi for the first 72 hours and keep Zoe on standby for customer-facing escalations. Combined sample-dollar-week for that primary pair = 1700-ish (back-of-envelope).
2) Low-effort owners (Priya, Marta) should handle async and comms; do not pages unless SLO breach.
3) If we expect >2x inbound from customers, escalate to Dedicated ops (ENG-4823) and trigger the mini-playbook on Confluence.

Assumptions and caveats:
- Hourly rate used is illustrative; for procurement/finance-level decisioning use actual blended rates from payroll.
- Probability buckets are subjective; this is deliberately conservative for quick triage.
- Does not include infra costs (GPUs, endpoint spend). For run-costs see linked ticket ENG-4823.

Open questions / follow-ups:
- Can SRE provide an on-call float for next 7 days? (ask: @oncall-sre)
- Should we pre-authorize a $2k surge fund for customer tickets if ticket volume > 25/day? (Finance call)
- Confirm if Solutions Eng can push templated troubleshooting guides to customers prior to escalation.

Next steps (owner assignments):
- Priya to assemble async playbook doc and share to slack by EOD Apr 15 (owner: Priya Natarajan).
- Ethan to confirm availability and define handoff window (owner: Ethan Morales).
- Ravi to triage observability alerts for one-off dashboards and hand over to Zoe for customer w/o infra changes (owner: Ravi Desai).

Quick log of touches (light audit):
2026-04-14: initial mini-roster compiled from standup and two SE calls (Priya).
2026-04-15: pulled in observability estimate after Ravi convo (Ethan).
2026-04-16: added customer comms owner and final sample-dollar-week numbers (Priya).

If you want a formal sheet with formulas and pivoting, I can convert this to a full Google Sheet and add live links to oncall schedules and spend dashboards. For now this is the minimal handback econ sketch to move fast.

title: Ad-hoc coverage claims crib sheet
preview:
Sheet description: lightweight, single-page tally for cross-functional coverage claims, shortfall triage, and quick owner handoffs. Use as a temporary single source while we run deeper capacity/cost analysis.

Columns: Item, Owner, Priority (H/M/L), Est effort (hrs), Est staff cost ($), Critical dependency, Acceptable fallback, Notes, Status, Last updated

Rows:
1, API route: /v1/generate-chat, Lydia Ramos (SE), H, 14, 1960, dependent on model-route mapping (PR #2114), fallback -> /v1/generate-chat-lite, "Shard policy needs confirm; Lydia to own rollout windows; PS to prep canary", in_review, 2026-04-15
2, Embeddings batch: enterprise-sync, Ethan Zhang (SE), M, 8, 1120, depends on embedding cache infra upgrade, fallback -> reduced batch size 16, "If cache delay >48h, throttle external syncs; notify sales-facing SEs", draft, 2026-04-14
3, Dedicated pool: ap-southeast burst, Priya Nair (Infra), H, 24, 3360, cloud quota approval, fallback -> regional fallback to eu-central, "Priya chasing cloud quota; estimated warm-up 2 hours; confirm SLAs for fallback", in_progress, 2026-04-12
4, Console alert: route-level burn spike, Jordan Blake (Observability), H, 4, 560, alert thresholds misconfigured, fallback -> mute and page ops, "Jordan to patch threshold and run 1-week sampling; include cost delta in daily digest", in_progress, 2026-04-13
5, Private deployment handoff checklist, Maya Thompson (Product), M, 6, 840, missing KMS key rotation docs, fallback -> temporary key escrow, "Notes to legal: verify HSM compat; quick checklist attached to this sheet", draft, 2026-04-12
6, Quantization pilot: model-x-8bit, Ravi Patel (Applied ML), M, 16, 2240, dependency on quant-lib v2, fallback -> run on float16 profile, "Ravi: run 500 prompt evals; capture perf delta and quality regression", planned, 2026-04-15
7, Customer escalations: ACME corp tokenization issue, Tasha Green (Solutions), H, 2, 280, customer-provided data edge-case, fallback -> temp normalization script, "Tasha to send sample; SEs to triage; prioritized for today", open, 2026-04-15

Quick tallies (auto-sum style, manual here):
- Total estimated effort (hrs): 70
- Total estimated staff cost ($): 10,360
- High-priority items: 4

Immediate notes / heuristics:
- Use a single rotation owner for each high-priority item. Keep notes short; link PRs or Confluence page rather than duplicating docs.
- For any item with a cloud quota dependency, mark "escalation contact" in the comments and set 24h and 72h check-ins.
- When a fallback is non-compatible with product SLO (e.g., quality drop > 6%), flag as "no-fallback" and assign exec ticket.

Mini action plan (next 72 hours):
- Lydia: finalize PR #2114 gating criteria and submit canary plan by EOD.
- Priya: confirm quota request ETA and set region fallback runbook. If quota >48h, request Dedicated support override.
- Jordan: deploy threshold patch and run sampling job; capture false-positive rate.
- Ravi: run first 500 prompts and share metrics in /display/RI/Coverage+Playbook.
- Maya/Tasha: consolidate customer issue sample and hand to SE on-call.

Notes to self (informal):
- This sheet is ephemeral; snapshot to Confluence before next ops sync.
- Look into adding a "confidence" column (0.0-1.0) to grade estimates.
- Consider converting to a small tracked pivot in the ops dashboard if items persist >2 weeks.

Context links and references: see linked_artifacts above for immediate cross-refs. Also related: CONFLUENCE:COVERAGE-PLAYBOOK (section: temporary-handbacks).

Bottom line: short, tactical ledger to coordinate quick handoffs across product, infra, obs, and SE. Use for day-to-day triage; do not treat as formal post-mortem artifact.

title: Auriga Burst AI Labs — Account Brief (Draft)
preview:
Account summary (CS-facing draft)
Company: Auriga Burst AI Labs (aurigaburst.ai)
Industry: Adtech (personalization + reranking + some chat-assistant surfaces)
Account tier target: Enterprise
HQ region: NA
Employee band: 1000+
Primary products in scope: Dedicated, Private, Optimize (plus new proposed Burst Reserve add-on)

Current stage (updated): Late evaluation → commercial negotiation pending final throughput + burst guarantees.
Forecast close (updated): 2026-04 (was 2025-12; slipped due to budget freeze + champion availability)
Estimated ARR (updated): $150k–$320k depending on reserved baseline + burst reserve sizing

Key contacts (updated):
- CTO: Maya Singh — original champion; returned from extended leave on a limited schedule as of early March. Still supportive but delegating day-to-day.
- Eng Lead: Samir Patel — day-to-day technical owner; effectively current champion.
- Procurement: (TBD) — waiting on security runbook + explicit SLA language.
Redwood team:
- AE: Avery Chen
- SE: Diego Ramirez
- CS (draft owner): Priya Natarajan

Use cases (mostly unchanged):
- Reranking for ad/promo selection (most latency-sensitive)
- Batch embeddings for catalog/user features
- Chat assistant for internal ops + limited customer-facing chat flows

Traffic + performance requirements (updated based on Feb/March follow-ups):
- Peak concurrency target: 750 qps sustained for ~20s bursts remains the baseline ask.
- Worst-case bursts: now stated as 2000–2500 qps during flash promotions (previously “up to ~2000”).
- Throughput target: ~500k tokens/min baseline; updated stretch goal discussed: ~650k tokens/min during peak windows if they expand chat surfaces.
- Latency SLOs:
  - Reranking: p95 <= 250ms (unchanged)
  - Chat flows: p99 <= 600ms (unchanged)
- Hard requirement: deterministic behavior under burst (avoid queueing cascades); willing to accept controlled degradation if explicitly specified (e.g., per-route throttling + overflow policy).

Deployment requirements (unchanged): VPC + public cloud acceptable.
Security requirements (largely unchanged, clarified):
- SSO/SAML required
- Audit logging required (customer asked for explicit event list and retention options)
- KMS integration required (runbook + key rotation expectations)
- Data residency: US-only (confirmed). They asked for written confirmation that support access is US-restricted for incident response.

Competitive context (updated):
- CompetitorX-cloud remains primary competitor; they offered reserved cluster + “guaranteed bursting” with aggressive pricing.
- In-house inference is a fallback option for non-peak workloads.
- New nuance: Auriga is evaluating a hybrid plan (reserve in CompetitorX + keep Redwood for specific reranking routes) unless we can provide stronger burst/capacity commitments.

What happened so far (timeline; mostly carried over + a couple of new items):
- 2025-10-20: Inbound; Hosted API demo (Avery).
- 2025-11-05: Qualified; requested Dedicated pricing + capacity model.
- 2025-11-18: POC kickoff — small-scale reranking tests on hosted.
- 2025-12-02: POC results: quality strong; queueing at ~600 qps sustained; latency spikes during bursts. CTO quote: “can’t risk queueing during campaigns.”
- 2026-01-07: Security review call; shared security FAQ + KMS diagram.
- 2026-01-15: CompetitorX offer: reserved cluster + guaranteed bursting at lower price; Auriga asked for Redwood SLA ramp plan.
- 2026-01-25: Internal budget freeze; CTO went on leave; cadence slowed.
- 2026-02-10: Follow-up demo on Dedicated autoscaling + burst policies; asked for hard queueing guarantees + per-route throttling.
- 2026-02-20: Last strong touchpoint pre-freeze: “evaluating options; price + capacity still deciding factors.”
- 2026-03-07 (new): Samir re-engaged; requested updated proposal including (a) reserved baseline, (b) burst reserve sizing guidance, (c) explicit queueing/overload behavior.
- 2026-03-14 (new): Internal Redwood sync: propose Dedicated + Burst Reserve add-on + written overload policy + joint load test plan. New infra ticket created for burst regression validation (INFR-2338).

Key blockers / risks (refreshed):
1) Throughput + burst/queueing risk (primary): their traffic is extremely bursty. They want capacity guarantees, or at minimum an explicit, testable overload policy.
2) Commercial pressure: CompetitorX pricing + “guaranteed bursting” language is currently more concrete.
3) Org dynamics: CTO is back but limited; Samir is executing. Need to ensure we’re not single-threaded.
4) Procurement readiness: wants audit logging + KMS operational runbook and explicit data residency/support-access language.

What we’ve done / proposals in motion (carried over, updated):
- Scoped Dedicated POC with autoscaling rules and batching/kv-cache recommendations.
- Optimize team provided kernel/quantization guidance; Diego proposed tuned batching profile.
- Proposed overflow behaviors (cross-region fallback discussed earlier), but customer preference is to avoid cross-region due to data residency + latency; now leaning toward in-region burst reserve + explicit throttling for non-critical routes.
- INFR-2145: investigating queueing root cause; engineering shared tuning notes; customer still wants deterministic SLA.
- INFR-2338 (new): validate burst regression under updated batching profile + isolate noisy-neighbor risks in Dedicated config.

Customer “must-haves” for next step (what they said explicitly):
- A re-run throughput POC with tuned batching profile AND clear methodology (traffic shape, duration, burst ramp).
- A written description of overload behavior (what happens at/beyond capacity) and what controls they get (per-route throttles, priority, circuit breakers).
- Security artifacts: audit log event taxonomy + KMS integration runbook + data residency/support access statement.

Next steps (CS + GTM alignment; updated):
- Technical: schedule joint load test plan review with Auriga SRE/Perf and Redwood infra leads; run the burst profile they specified (baseline 750 qps w/ 20s bursts; spikes to 2000–2500 qps) and capture p95/p99 lat + queue depth behavior.
- Commercial: deliver two options:
  (1) Dedicated baseline + Burst Reserve add-on (3-month pilot) with explicit burst capacity window
  (2) Dedicated baseline only, with a documented overload policy + optional future reserve
- Security: package a single procurement bundle (audit logging details + KMS runbook + residency/support-access statement). Track against SEC-307.
- Executive alignment: re-engage Maya (CTO) for sign-off once Samir validates the POC results; avoid stalling on “champion availability.”

CS stance (draft): High strategic value (enterprise adtech, big volume) with elevated reliability expectations. If we can make burst behavior explicit and repeatably demonstrable, likelihood improves; if not, high probability they split workloads or default to CompetitorX for peak campaigns.

Open questions (to resolve before finalizing brief):
- Do we have approved standard language for “burst reserve” and overload behavior that legal will accept?
- Can we commit to any queueing/latency SLO under burst, or only best-effort + documented degradation?
- What is the smallest credible reserved baseline that meets their campaign risk tolerance without pricing us out?

Account tier target: Enterprise

Employee band: 1000+
Primary products in scope: Dedicated, Private, Optimize (plus new proposed Burst Reserve add-on)

Current stage (updated): Late evaluation → commercial negotiation pending final throughput + burst guarantees.

Key contacts (updated):

- SSO/SAML required

title: Q1 Strategic Lanes: Compact and Staff Briefing (WIP)
preview:
Purpose:
This doc is a compact, operational-first brief for cross-company alignment on Q1 priorities. It bundles a concise OKR canvas, an all-staff run-of-show proposal, executive asks, and the minimal comms and rehearsal checklist needed to land a single, clear ticket-forward narrative for the quarter.

Executive summary (TL;DR):
- Focus lanes for Q1: 1) Serving Reliability & SLOs, 2) Cost-per-token reduction (infra + Optimize), 3) Hosted-to-Dedicated handoff for two pilot customers.
- Proposed company OKRs (draft) below; these are intentionally measurable and time-boxed.
- All-staff will be a short, tactical orientation + Q&A (45–60m). Run-of-show and owner list included.
- Critical asks from execs: decisions on Dedicated pilot staffing, temporary spend envelope for burst capacity, and a small comms budget for partner customer demos.

High-level timeline (milestones):
- Mar 16: Exec review (this doc + one-pager) -> decisions on staffing & budget (owner: CTO).
- Mar 22: Final OKR publish (Confluence + roadmap sync).
- Mar 30: All-staff rehearsal complete.
- Apr 2: Company all-staff (orientation + Q&A).
- Apr 15: First Dedicated pilot on-boarding begins.

Draft company-level OKRs (operational and product mix):
Objective A — Production reliability becomes a competitive advantage
KR A1: Reduce 95th-percentile API latency for text-gen 1k+ tokens by 20% (baseline: 320ms p95).
KR A2: Decrease incident MTTA to <8 minutes and MTTR to <45 minutes for serving-runtime incidents (baseline: MTTR 72m).
KR A3: Achieve 99.95% uptime for hosted-critical region endpoints during peak hours (monitored, alerting & playbooks).

Objective B — Improve unit economics without regressing quality
KR B1: Reduce per-token cost for top-10 customers by 15% via batching, cache & quantization guidance (pilot metric).
KR B2: Push two model variants (fp16->4-bit) to Dedicated pools with validated quality thresholds and automatic rollback gating.
KR B3: Ship Optimize recommendations engine MVP to 30 internal customers and show estimated monthly savings $X (dashboard).

Objective C — Convert usage into predictable enterprise pipelines
KR C1: Onboard 2 pilot customers to Dedicated (contract + baseline SLA) and measure throughput/latency improvements vs hosted baseline.
KR C2: Publish Private deployment readiness checklist and close 6 security gaps identified in external audit.
KR C3: Reduce time-to-rollout for canary -> full release from 5 days to 48 hours for model updates.

Notes on metrics: all KRs require a simple instrumentation plan (metric names, dashboards, owner). We'll finalize metric definitions in ENG-INF-1229 and CONF-2847.

All-staff run-of-show (45–60 min suggested):
- 00:00–00:05 Opening and context (CEO) — what success looks like for Q1. (Owner: CEO)
- 00:05–00:15 Executive mosaic: 3 lanes and the customer impact story (VP Product + Head of Serving) — 2 slides each, focused bullets, 5 minute transitions. (Owners: VP Product, Head of Serving)
- 00:15–00:30 Tactical commitments & ops changes (SRE + Optimize lead) — new alerting/playbooks, rollout cadence, and temporary capacity policy. (Owners: Head SRE, Optimize PM)
- 00:30–00:45 Customer & Partner spotlight (solutions engineer demo of Dedicated pilot flow) — 5–7 minute demo + 3 minute partner remarks. (Owner: Solutions Eng)
- 00:45–00:60 Live Q&A (moderated) — pre-seeded questions + one live poll. (Moderator: People Ops)

Rehearsal checklist (one week before):
- Slide stack frozen by Mar 25.
- Dry run with moderators + AV check Mar 28.
- Pre-seed 6–8 questions to Slack channel #all-staff-asks 48 hours before.
- Embed short pulse survey at end (2 questions): "Do you understand Q1 priorities?" and "Do you have visible blockers?" (Owner: People Ops)

Comms plan (short):
- Publish one-pager + OKR canvas to Confluence and /company/all-staff the morning of Apr 2.
- Email to customers with Dedicated pilot milestones (customer success owner).
- Post-meeting followup: commitments + owners + action items in a single comment thread (owner: Maya Rodriguez).

Risks & mitigations (top-5):
1) Risk: Dedicated pilot staffing is under-resourced -> Mitigation: convert 0.2 FTE from infra for 8 weeks + temporary contractor budget.
2) Risk: Cost reduction efforts reduce model quality -> Mitigation: require blind evals on held-out prompts and automatic rollback gating.
3) Risk: All-staff messaging is too tactical, loses strategic narrative -> Mitigation: open with CEO vision + 2 customer stories.
4) Risk: Alert fatigue undermines SLO goals -> Mitigation: SRE to rework alert thresholds and add playbook for suppression windows.
5) Risk: Exec decisions delayed -> Mitigation: escalate to exec sync on Mar 16; list required decision items below.

Decisions requested from execs by Mar 16 meeting:
- Confirm temporary budget for Dedicated pilot ramp (approx $75k one-time burst capacity).
- Approve 0.2 FTE infra allocation to pilot for 8 weeks.
- Approve the quality gating thresholds for 4-bit model variants (we propose blind A/B x 3 metrics: perplexity delta, human eval delta, latency).

Open questions (needs answers before publish):
- Final per-token savings target for the Optimize pilot (finance input). (Owner: Finance)
- Legal signoff cadence for Dedicated pilot contracts (owner: Legal)
- Do we commit to a single canonical dashboard for OKR progress (owner: Product Ops)?

Owner & next steps (short):
- Maya Rodriguez: collate exec feedback and update doc by Mar 18.
- Ethan Park: produce one-pager + slide template by Mar 20.
- Priya Kapoor: draft comms + FAQ for customers by Mar 22.
- SRE (Sasha Ivanov): draft alert/playbook updates by Apr 1.
- Solutions Eng (Benji Lee): prepare demo and partner brief by Mar 24.

Notes to self / raw thinking (informal):
- Keep the all-staff <60m, avoid multi-speaker monologues. People retain narrative when we show 1 customer story + 1 technical win.
- Avoid deep dives in the meeting; link to breakout sessions for product/infra owners.
- We need a short visual that maps "what we ship" -> "what customers see" — request design help (Slack @design).

References & artifacts:
- Confluence canvas: CONF-2847 (OKR definitions + dashboards).
- Eng epic: ENG-INF-1229 (instrumentation + metric names).
- Legal intake ticket: JIRA-OPS-457.
- Draft slide deck PR: https://github.com/redwood-inference/roadmap/pull/198 (staging).

Appendix: one-paragraph scripts for speakers (prep):
- CEO: "This quarter is about making performance predictable for our customers. Predictability is the moat we can own. We will measure that via latency SLOs, incident metrics, and the conversion of heavy users to Dedicated contracts."
- Head of Serving: "Tactically, we will reduce P95 via targeted kernel selection, prefix caching, and tuned batching. Expect an operational change to how we scale burst capacity; playbooks will be live by Apr 1."
- SRE: "Our focus is fewer noisy alerts and faster response. We will ship a new incident triage pipeline and automated rollback triggers for model releases."

If you have edits, add inline suggestions here and tag me (Maya). This is intentionally compact — goal is to land a single set of publishable artifacts (one-pager, deck, OKR canvas) by Mar 22.

-- end --

title: Release comms handoff and customer notes template (mar-2026 draft)
preview:
Purpose:
This is a compact, ops-focused communications and handoff template for small-to-medium Redwood inference rollouts (hotfixes, minor model variants, feature toggles). The doc doubles as: 1) an internal ops checklist for release engineers and SREs and 2) a customer-facing release note + comms snippet that PMs / marketing can copy. Keep short; link to longer release notes when needed.

When to use this template:
- Mini-release or patch window (<= 48h stabilization expectation)
- Model variant swaps, routing policy flips, or control-plane config changes
- Customer-impacting behavior where we provide proactive notes (no full PRD change)

Quick release summary (TOP OF MESSAGE - single-paragraph for customers):
- Title: Rolling update: model routing tweak and latency improvements (non-breaking)
- ETA: 2026-03-18 09:00 PT -> 2026-03-18 11:00 PT (2h window)
- Impact: No downtime expected. A small percentage (~5%) of requests may be served by the old variant during the canary ramp. Users do not need to take action.

Internal ops handoff checklist (pre-release):
- [ ] Finalize PRs and attach release checklist: PR-1187 linked above
- [ ] Sign-off from Product: Priya Singh (email thread / slack)
