title: Quarterly Orbit: Ops Cookbook & Sprint Planning (Q3 bridge)
preview:
Purpose:
This doc is a lightweight, tactical cookbook for running our Q3-bridge quarter (internal name: "Orbit"). It combines a proposed OKR crosswalk, an all-staff agenda skeleton, operational playbook snippets, and a compact risk/mitigation register for exec review. Intended audience: execs, leads across Eng, Product, GTM, and Ops. Status: working draft — looking for feedback from product-ops, eng-sre, and head of GTM by 2026-03-19.

Executive narrative (1-paragraph):
We are entering a three-month bridge focused on reliability-at-scale while preserving velocity for model onboarding and Dedicated sales. The priority is threefold: (1) harden the serving runtime for predictable tail latency across published models, (2) reduce per-token unit cost on hosted API by 8–12% through batching and caching recommendations, and (3) finalize Private deployment packaging for two pilot customers. These themes balance near-term revenue delivery with foundational work that reduces long-run operational burden.

High-level OKR draft (proposal):
- Objective A: Ensure platform reliability under 2x baseline traffic and reduce customer-visible incidents.
  - KR A1: Achieve 99.95% availability for hosted API routes (per-route SLO) for top 10 routes.
  - KR A2: Reduce 95th-percentile tail latency for text-generation by 20% (measured across top-5 models).
  - KR A3: Automate failover routing so >= 90% of degraded requests are served by compatible fallback within 300ms.

- Objective B: Improve inference economics for self-serve and dedicated customers.
  - KR B1: Reduce hosted per-token cost by 8–12% on target workloads via batching and cache policies.
  - KR B2: Ship model quantization recommendations and two validated quant profiles for popular Llama/Redwood variants.
  - KR B3: Deliver an initial cost-estimator UI in Console for dev teams (alpha).

- Objective C: Close private deployment pilots and operationalize onboarding.
  - KR C1: Complete 2 Private pilot installs (VPC/on-prem) with documented runbook.
  - KR C2: Publish Private deployment security checklist and KMS integration guide.
  - KR C3: Train 3 customer-ops contacts on our support/resolution workflow.

Crosswalk: how priorities map to teams (draft):
- Serving runtime: Objective A KRs A1–A2 (owners: eng-serving-runtime, site-reliability-engineering).
- Smart routing & fallbacks: A3 (owners: platform-engineering, observability).
- Optimize (batching, quantization): Objective B (owners: applied-ml, optimize, product-optimize).
- Private deployment rollout: Objective C (owners: private-deployments, infra, security-and-compliance).
- Console & GTM: B3 + C2 (owners: console-and-rollouts, go-to-market).

All-staff (town) agenda — 60 minutes (proposed):
- 00-05 — Opening: CEO quick context + success metric needle for the quarter (1 slide).
- 05-18 — Engineering deep snapshot: 3 signal cards (latency, availability, unit-cost trend).
- 18-30 — Product ops demo: Console cost-estimator alpha (live) + rollout plan.
- 30-40 — Customer success spotlight: Private pilot customer expectations & timeline.
- 40-50 — Risk & mitigation lightning: 5 major risks and owners.
- 50-60 — Commitments & Q&A: What every team is committing to this quarter.

Prep notes for presenters (internal):
- Eng leads: bring one slide with current trendline + three actions to hit KR A2.
- Applied ML: short demo video of quantized model throughput (20–30s).
- CS & Solutions Eng: readiness checklist for pilot handoffs.
- GTM: simple messaging framework for pilot/enterprise outreach.

Sprint planning cookbook (how to run coordinated 6-week sprints within the quarter):
- Sprint cadence: two 3-week sprints per quarter segment. Each sprint contains: one reliability spike (2 weeks), one delivery milestone (feature/observability), and ongoing support rotation.
- Cross-team sync ritual: weekly 30-min 'Orbit stand' on Tuesdays — owner rotates weekly.
- Decision window: adopt a 48-hr escalation path for any blocker that impacts multiple KRs (see decision flow below).

Decision flow (quick reference):
- If a candidate change increases risk to availability by >5% for top routes -> call SRE lead + platform on-call -> 48-hr decision path (mitigation or rollback).
- If a change reduces estimated per-token cost by >10% but increases tail latency -> run canary and measure 2-week tradeoff analysis before rollout.

Risk register (top 6):
1) Model onboarding surprises: new model behaves badly in tail-latency. Mitigation: expanded pre-commit evals + canary dataset. Owner: applied-ml.
2) Insufficient capacity in key region during Dedicated sales spike. Mitigation: temporary burst pool policy + cloud capacity reservations. Owner: dedicated-team.
3) Console alpha misses critical cost signals. Mitigation: ship telemetry hooks early and do user interviews within 2 sprints. Owner: console-and-rollouts.
4) Private pilot networking delays (customer infra constraints). Mitigation: pre-flight checklist, remote lab tests. Owner: private-deployments.
5) Staffing pinch across SRE during on-call rotation. Mitigation: shift coverage, hire contractor SRE for 8 weeks. Owner: site-reliability-engineering.
6) Security review backlog for on-prem packaging. Mitigation: prioritize KMS guide and designate security SWAT. Owner: security-and-compliance.

Open questions (for execs):
- How aggressive should we be on the per-token cost target vs. latency tradeoffs? Recommend maintain latency SLOs and accept smaller cost wins for Q3 bridge; escalate if CFO expects hard cost targets.
- Will sales commit to two private pilot contracts this quarter (necessary to prioritize packaging)? Need confirmation from VP Sales by 2026-03-19.

Concrete asks (within 2 weeks):
- Product & Eng: finalize KR definitions (metrics, dashboards) — owner: Priya Nair — due 2026-03-19.
- GTM: confirm pilot go/no-go with lead customers — owner: Liam O'Sullivan — due 2026-03-21.
- SRE: propose temporary on-call augmentation plan — owner: Marcelo Rivera — due 2026-03-18.
- Applied ML: deliver two quant profiles and benchmarking notes — owner: Jia Park — due 2026-04-02.

Quick reference links (work-in-progress):
- Canary checklist (draft): https://confluence.redwood.ai/pages/canary-checklist
- KR dashboard template: https://datadog.redwood.ai/dashboards/okr-template
- Pilot runbook template: https://confluence.redwood.ai/pages/pilot-runbook
- Related JIRA epics: OPS-412, INF-887, PRIV-230
- Engineering PR (prototype failover routing): https://github.com/redwood-inference/platform/pull/3421

Notes to self / rough thoughts:
- Keep all-staff short and action-oriented; avoid too many metrics slides — pick 3 critical signals only.
- Consider a 2-week pre-brief with team leads to surface blockers early.
- If we get pushback on KR B1 (cost target), be prepared with a reduced-scope alternative: focus cost wins on self-serve API only.

Appendix: short checklist for private pilot onboarding (alpha)
- Confirm network peering & IP allowlist (network lead + customer).
- KMS key rotation test and audit log ingestion.
- Latency baseline measurement against customer test harness.
- On-call escalation contact list + timezones.
- Post-install evaluation survey (CS).

End — feedback requested in doc comments or Slack thread #ops-quarterly-orbit.

Revision history:
- v0.1 2026-02-20 Ava Chen: initial draft.
- v0.2 2026-03-05 Marcelo R: added sprint cookbook + risks.
- v0.3 2026-03-12 Priya N: updated OKRs and deadlines (pending exec review).
text:
Purpose:
This doc is a lightweight, tactical cookbook for running our Q3-bridge quarter (internal name: "Orbit"). It combines a proposed OKR crosswalk, an all-staff agenda skeleton, operational playbook snippets, and a compact risk/mitigation register for exec review. Intended audience: execs, leads across Eng, Product, GTM, and Ops. Status: working draft — looking for feedback from product-ops, eng-sre, and head of GTM by 2026-03-19.

Executive narrative (1-paragraph):
We are entering a three-month bridge focused on reliability-at-scale while preserving velocity for model onboarding and Dedicated sales. The priority is threefold: (1) harden the serving runtime for predictable tail latency across published models, (2) reduce per-token unit cost on hosted API by 8–12% through batching and caching recommendations, and (3) finalize Private deployment packaging for two pilot customers. These themes balance near-term revenue delivery with foundational work that reduces long-run operational burden.

High-level OKR draft (proposal):
- Objective A: Ensure platform reliability under 2x baseline traffic and reduce customer-visible incidents.
  - KR A1: Achieve 99.95% availability for hosted API routes (per-route SLO) for top 10 routes.
  - KR A2: Reduce 95th-percentile tail latency for text-generation by 20% (measured across top-5 models).
  - KR A3: Automate failover routing so >= 90% of degraded requests are served by compatible fallback within 300ms.

- Objective B: Improve inference economics for self-serve and dedicated customers.
  - KR B1: Reduce hosted per-token cost by 8–12% on target workloads via batching and cache policies.
  - KR B2: Ship model quantization recommendations and two validated quant profiles for popular Llama/Redwood variants.
  - KR B3: Deliver an initial cost-estimator UI in Console for dev teams (alpha).

- Objective C: Close private deployment pilots and operationalize onboarding.
  - KR C1: Complete 2 Private pilot installs (VPC/on-prem) with documented runbook.
  - KR C2: Publish Private deployment security checklist and KMS integration guide.
  - KR C3: Train 3 customer-ops contacts on our support/resolution workflow.

Crosswalk: how priorities map to teams (draft):
- Serving runtime: Objective A KRs A1–A2 (owners: eng-serving-runtime, site-reliability-engineering).
- Smart routing & fallbacks: A3 (owners: platform-engineering, observability).
- Optimize (batching, quantization): Objective B (owners: applied-ml, optimize, product-optimize).
- Private deployment rollout: Objective C (owners: private-deployments, infra, security-and-compliance).
- Console & GTM: B3 + C2 (owners: console-and-rollouts, go-to-market).

6) Security review backlog for on-prem packaging. Mitigation: prioritize KMS guide and designate security SWAT. Owner: security-and-compliance.

title: three-month-northstar-focus-campaign-and-invite-plan-v0-1
preview:
Purpose:
A compact, cross-functional plan to align the company around a single 90-day north star, map the company-level OKR drafts, and coordinate the all-hands + exec update cadence and comms. This is intentionally terse — use as the single source for invites, rehearsals, and the first round of leadership sign-offs.

Audience:
- Exec team
- All managers (ICs receive a simplified version for sync)
- GTM + CS leads (to prepare customer messaging)

Context / why now:
- Q2 capacity and Dedicated ramp require clear priority narrowing to avoid scope bleed into product roadmap (Hosting infra + Optimize teams flagged as potential bottlenecks).
- We need rapid alignment for customer-facing SLA language prior to sales kickoff (target 2026-05-01).

Suggested 90-day North Star (proposal):
"Ship and operationalize predictable inference for scale: 3x sustained throughput with <=15% cost/unit delta vs baseline across three benchmark workloads (chat, completion, embeddings)."
- Why: combines performance, cost, and reliability (the three commercial levers that customers ask about).
- Measurement window: rolling 7-day median for throughput and cost/unit; incidents measured by P95 latency regressions and SLO breaches.

Company-level OKR drafts (placeholders for team input):
O: Deliver a production-grade scale step that lowers cost/unit and improves throughput for priority workloads.
KR1: Deploy Dedicated pool autoscaling + burst policies to 2 enterprise customers with 99.9% acceptance SLA by 2026-04-20.
KR2: Reduce per-token cost for chat workload by 18% via batching + quantization profiles (measured on benchmark harness).
KR3: Demonstrate 3x sustained throughput on serving-runtime internal benchmark with P95 latency within target.
KR4: Add KV cache metrics and rollback playbook to Redwood Console; onboard 4 routes to use auto-caching.

O: Reduce operational toil and increase observability to detect quality regressions earlier.
KR1: Instrument token-level tracing for top 10 customer routes and create alerting playbooks.
KR2: Ship regression alerting tied to prompt suites and add two regression-runbook playbooks.
KR3: Cut incident MTTR by 30% in staged incidents (SRE measurement).

Notes on drafting process:
- These are intentionally aggressive. Product + Infra owners must provide scoped plans and dependencies by 2026-03-18 (see action owners below).
- If a team cannot provide a plan that maps to a KR, flag in Slack #lead-prioritization with a short cause and mitigation.

All-hands & Exec update plan (timeline):
- 2026-03-22: Leadership sync — approve north star and OKR framing (30m).
- 2026-03-29: First-draft manager digest sent company-wide (one-pager + playbook links).
- 2026-04-12: All-hands rehearsal (full run-through with AV, script owners, safety scripts).
- 2026-04-15: All-hands town (exec update + Q&A).
- 2026-04-20: OKR sign-off deadline (for teams to lock scope for Q2 execution).

All-hands run-of-show (sketch):
- 0:00–0:05 — Intro + framing from CEO (north star & why we narrowed).
- 0:05–0:12 — Product spotlight (hosted API & dedicated improvements; demo of throughput dashboard).
- 0:12–0:22 — Engineering brief (SRE + serving-runtime): what to expect, fallbacks, and runbooks.
- 0:22–0:30 — Customer stories (CS highlights of partners on Dedicated pilot).
- 0:30–0:45 — Breakout lightning: teams share 2-minute blockers & asks (pre-submitted).
- 0:45–1:00 — Live Q&A + close.

Comms calendar (owner: Priya):
- 2026-03-23: Manager guide + slide template (internal).
- 2026-03-29: First digest + OKR draft attached.
- 2026-04-01: External partner note for pilots (GTM review).
- 2026-04-10: Rehearsal recap + final slides to all managers.
- Post all-hands (2026-04-15): Public minutes + short recording, release of team-level OKR intake form.

Action owners (first pass):
- Product/Hosted API: Noah Patel — scope demo, tie KRs to model catalog changes.
- Serving-runtime/Infra: Miguel Torres — throughput benchmark plan, dependency list, fallback policies.
- Console & Observability: Lena Fischer — build dashboard mock + KV cache metrics.
- GTM/CS: Owen Brooks — customer narrative for Dedicated pilots + partner comms.
- Comms + Ops: Priya Kulkarni — synthesize manager digest and run all-hands logistics.
- Project coordinator: Ava Chen — track sign-offs, pulse, and decision log.

Risk ladder (high-level):
1) Delivery slippage (root causes: infra contention, model onboarding delays). Mitigation: thin-slice deliverables, ship behind feature flags, and two-week cadence demos.
2) Quality regressions after quantization / kernel optimizations. Mitigation: gated rollout, canary traffic, rollback playbook.
3) Customer confusion about SLA semantics (Dedicated vs Hosted). Mitigation: clear one-pager for GTM and sales FAQ.
4) Over-commitment across teams (too many KRs). Mitigation: enforce max 2 KRs per team that map to company-level objectives.

Rehearsal checklist (pre-all-hands):
- Slides locked + under 25 slides (Priya)
- AV test in meeting room + remote stream validated (IT)
- CEO talking points + 3 backup remarks for tough Qs (Exec Ops)
- Pre-submitted questions triaged and assigned (People Ops)
- Recording and minutes owner assigned (Project coordinator)
- Incident fallback: short holding statement prepared (SRE)

Decision log (open items):
- Should the north star explicitly include "cost/unit" or only implicitly via KRs? (owner: CEO)
- Final KR thresholds (numbers) need benchmarks from infra by 2026-03-15 (owner: Miguel)
- Customer-facing SLA language: final draft by 2026-04-07 (owner: Owen)

Open questions / parking-lot:
- Do we require a soft freeze for feature merges the week of 2026-04-10 to ensure stability for demo builds?
- Are we comfortable with a two-track rollout (pilot customers + public feature flag) or do we want a gated invite-only approach?
- How will success be communicated to non-engineering teams (one-pager vs interactive dashboard)?

Next immediate steps (by 2026-03-18):
1) Team owners submit scoped plan + dependencies (1 page each).
2) Infra publishes initial benchmark numbers and measurement rubric.
3) Comms drafts manager digest and slide template.
4) Exec meeting to finalize north star wording (2026-03-22).

Notes to self / rough thoughts:
- Keep the north star customer facing but actionable for engineering (avoid vague phrasing).
- Make OKRs small enough that progress is visible biweekly; avoid month-end-only pushes.
- Use the all-hands not just for announcements; make it a decision forum for sign-offs.

Appendix (links to artifacts to populate before finalization):
- Benchmark harness: github.com/redwood-inference/benchmarks (PR #4123 open)
- Confluence draft: TEAM-3month-northstar (linked above)
- OKR intake form: internal form (to be created)

"Notes to reviewers: edit inline, add a single sentence plan for your team under 'Action owners' by 2026-03-18. If you cannot hit dates, flag in #lead-prioritization with expected slippage and reason.

title: Pep Rally all-hands template and exec walkthrough (draft)
preview:
Context and intent:
This doc is a working template for a high-energy, short-format company all-hands (internal codename: "Pep Rally"). It combines an exec-level readout, a curated OKR highlight section, and a pragmatic run-of-show for a 60-minute session. Use it as the baseline for speaker prep, slide checklist, and follow-up asks.

Primary goals:
- Re-center the company on our production-first thesis: serving reliability, predictable economics, and private deployment momentum.
- Signal concrete OKR progress and where we need cross-org lifts for the next quarter.
- Energize teams with quick wins, customer war stories, and an explicit list of executive asks.
- Reduce post-all-hands confusion by mapping owners to follow-ups.

Audience:
- All employees (engineers, product, go-to-market, SRE, support).
- Executive leadership will use the 15-minute readout to set strategic context.

Success metrics for this event (how we measure whether it worked):
- Survey pulse: >= 70% of respondents report "clear on priorities for next 90 days".
- Action mapping: 90% of follow-ups assigned with owners and due dates within 48 hours.
- Slide hygiene: < 10% of submitted slides flagged for revision during speaker rehearsal.

High-level timing (60 minutes)
- 0:00–0:05: Welcome + housekeeping (MC) — logistics: captions, ask channel, Slack thread for follow-ups.
- 0:05–0:20: Exec readout (CEO + CTO) — 15 min: top-line progress, critical signals, three strategic asks.
- 0:20–0:35: OKR highlights and risk spotlight (Product VP) — 15 min: Quick wins, at-risk items, mitigation asks.
- 0:35–0:45: Customer story + demo snippet (Solutions/CS) — 10 min: Live customer excerpt or playback.
- 0:45–0:55: Team rapid-fire wins (4 teams x 2.5 min) — 10 min: short celebratory moments and ship highlights.
- 0:55–1:00: Calls-to-action and next steps (Head of Ops) — 5 min: Assign follow-ups, link to action board.

Draft company-level OKRs to surface (TL;DR for slides)
- OKR 1: Reliability & uptime (platform)
  - KR1.1: Maintain 99.95% platform availability for all hosted APIs (measured by region).
  - KR1.2: Reduce P95 latency for standard token generation by 15% through batching/kernels.
  - Signals to share: Dedicated customer SLA tests completed, 2 regressed endpoints identified.

- OKR 2: Cost-per-inference optimization
  - KR2.1: Reduce average per-token infra cost by 18% on production traffic via batching and quantized variants.
  - KR2.2: Pilot Redwood Optimize recommendations on three high-volume routes with measurable savings.
  - Signals: Pilot design doc ready; initial sim shows ~12% savings—need infra time to implement.

- OKR 3: Enterprise/private deployments momentum
  - KR3.1: Close two Private VPC deals (pilot contract signed) by end of quarter.
  - KR3.2: Ship on-prem install docs and HSM integration blueprint.
  - Signals: Legal has two redlines; field engineering capacity limited.

Exec readout talking points (concise bullets for CEO/CTO slides)
- Where we are: strong growth in hosted API adoption, Dedicated renewals holding, Private deals long but strategic.
- What matters next 90 days: stabilize top customer SLAs, accelerate Model Variant fallback work, and clear infra backlog for Optimize pilots.
- Three asks to org: (1) Prioritize two infra stories (P95 kernel patches), (2) Cross-team support for Private pilot onboarding, (3) Bring one high-impact eval to production for quality regression detection.

Run-of-show operational checklist (pre-week)
- Week -2: Ask leads to submit 1 slide per segment + speaker notes in shared folder.
- Week -1: Rehearsal scheduling — dry run with MC and AV.
- 72 hours out: Publish pre-read (1 pager) with 3 OKR signal charts and explicit asks.
- 24 hours out: Final slides submitted, accessibility check (captioning, color contrast).
- Day-of: MC script printed, Zoom link tested, fallback dial-in ready.

Slide checklist (per speaker)
- One-slide slide: 1 headline, 2 supporting bullets, one visual (chart or screenshot).
- Minimize text: aim for 6 lines max per slide.
- Add owner footer: name + follow-up JIRA/owner tag.

Customer story prep (CS)
- Two-minute recorded clip option (preferred) to avoid live flub.
- Focus: measurable impact (latency/cost/regulatory win).
- Ask customer for a 1-sentence quote and permission to share.

Rapid-fire wins guidance (team leads)
- Each team: 2x bullets (one shipped win, one near-term ask/need).
- Keep it human (names, shout-outs).

Planned follow-up workflow (post-all-hands)
- Immediately after: Publish meeting notes + slide deck to Team Drive and company channel.
- Action board (Trello/JIRA/Asana): create tickets for all asks. Owner + due date required within 48 hours.
- 1-week check-in: company-ops to surface any unowned items; escalate to exec if still unassigned.

Contingencies and fallbacks
- If key slide not ready: swap with pre-approved backlog slide (talk track prepped).
- If CEO/CTO delayed: MC delivers pre-read highlights and extends Q&A via Slack AMA.
- If AV fails: shift to audio-only, record locally, and post to drive.

Open questions / decisions to resolve in planning sync:
- Who will maintain the live action board post-event? (ops or product program manager?)
- Approve the 48-hour ownership SLA? Execs prefer 72h; ops prefers 48h for momentum.
- Which private pilot customer gets the lead engineer allocation (capacity conflict with Dedicated renewals).

Next steps (owners and dates)
- Ava Chen: assemble pre-read + one-pager by 2026-03-01.
- Marcus Díaz: collect slides and coordinate rehearsal slots by 2026-03-05.
- Leah Kapoor: confirm customer clip permissions and source quote by 2026-03-03.
- Jonah Rivera: create action-board template and post-event workflow doc by 2026-03-12.

Notes to speakers (informal):
- Keep tone crisp and optimistic; avoid long technical digressions in the exec readout.
- Use the "what we did / why it matters / what we need" cadence for every slide.
- Mark any sensitive legal/regulatory content with "[CONFIDENTIAL]" in the slide footer — we'll gate distribution.

Draft rehearsal script snippet (MC + CEO):
- MC: "Welcome everyone — quick housekeeping — captions enabled — slides and recording will be posted — three primary asks at the end. Over to Sam."
- CEO: "Thanks — quick thank you, top signals, and three asks — then hand to Mei for OKR highlights."

Appendix (supporting links & references)
- Template deck: team-drive:/allhands/templates/pep-rally-2026-deck (placeholder)
- Action-board sample: JIRA:RDW-1289 (board template)
- Pre-read examples: confluence:RDW-OKR-2026

Status: draft — this doc intentionally concise. Iterate in planning sync (owners above).

Quick micro-retro (post-event questions to include in pulse survey)
- Did the all-hands clarify priorities for your team? Y/N + short why
- Was the cadence useful (length/timing)? 1–5
- Which of the three exec asks are you already aligned to? (checkboxes)

END OF DRAFT

It combines an exec-level readout, a curated OKR highlight section, and a pragmatic run-of-show for a 60-minute session. Use it as the baseline for speaker prep, slide checklist, and follow-up asks.

Primary goals:
- Re-center the company on our production-first thesis: serving reliability, predictable economics, and private deployment momentum.
- Signal concrete OKR progress and where we need cross-org lifts for the next quarter.
- Energize teams with quick wins, customer war stories, and an explicit list of executive asks.
- Reduce post-all-hands confusion by mapping owners to follow-ups.

Audience:
- All employees (engineers, product, go-to-market, SRE, support).
- Executive leadership will use the 15-minute readout to set strategic context.

Success metrics for this event (how we measure whether it worked):
- Survey pulse: >= 70% of respondents report "clear on priorities for next 90 days".

title: Company Commander Brief and Failover Protocol — Q2 Prep
preview:
Purpose:
This is a compact, actionable brief for execs and cross-functional leads to prepare for the Q2 all-hands and finalize company-level OKRs. Treat it as the single source for the pre-all-hands scrub, communication cadence, and immediate failover protocols if a major risk triggers in the week before the townline.

Intended audience:
- CEO, COO, Exec Leadership, VPs and Directors
- Owners of company-level OKRs (Primary + 2 backups)
- Communications and Ops partners managing the all-hands run-of-show

One-line summary for execs to read in <2 minutes:
We propose 3 company priorities for Q2 (Scale inference economics, Harden Private, Developer uptake). Draft OKRs attached below. Key risks: Dedicated capacity shortage, a critical regression in runtime perf, and a compliance gap in Private onboarding. For each we have a failover: model routing throttles, canary rollback and immediate OSS hotfix plan, and an opt-in delayed Private launch with extended support.

Draft Company Priorities (candidate language) — decisions required by Exec Scrub on 2026-03-15:
1) Scale inference economics: improve throughput-per-dollar across hosted API by 20% through batching, caching, and quantization guidance.
2) Harden Private: ship VPC onboarding with KMS integration and audit logs for GA customers, reduce configuration friction.
3) Developer uptake: reduce time-to-first-response for new teams (SDK + Quickstart) to <10 minutes and double the number of PLG signups that convert to live traffic.

Draft OKRs (top-level — metric placeholders to finalize):
- Objective A: Reduce per-token serving cost for common enterprise workloads
  - KR1: 20% lower median cost-per-token on top-100 customer prompts (benchmarked)
  - KR2: Deploy recommended quantization profiles for 3 model families and measure impact
  - KR3: Ship runtime prefix caching by end of Q2 (SLO: <5% tail latency impact)

- Objective B: Private readiness for regulated customers
  - KR1: Complete KMS + HSM integrations and 2 SOC mock audits
  - KR2: Onboard 2 pilot customers to private deployment (VPC or on-prem) with signed MOU
  - KR3: Provide compliance playbook + exportable audit logs feature

- Objective C: Developer-first activation and retention
  - KR1: SDK quickstarts for top-5 frameworks with sample apps (increase PLG conversions 2x)
  - KR2: Reduce onboarding-to-prod time to <72 hours for typical teams (tracked through intake)
  - KR3: Launch a set of 10 benchmarked eval suites for early regression detection

Notes / rationale (short):
- Cost/perf is our strongest product lever; shipping batching and KV-prefix caching is high ROI.
- Private is sales gating for several enterprise prospects; better to be conservative on GA wording if audit readiness lags.
- Developer activation drives inbound pipeline; focus on friction removal rather than aggressive marketing until we hit reliability SLOs.

All-hands run-of-show (high level):
- 0:00–0:05 Opening + company state (CEO) — include one-sentence on finances & health
- 0:05–0:12 Q2 priorities and top OKRs (Exec sponsor)
- 0:12–0:22 Product demo + live metric (quick) — show latency/cost chart for recent improvements
- 0:22–0:30 Customer story or pilot highlight
- 0:30–0:40 Org shoutouts + operational asks + Q&A triage

Communications plan (pre/post):
- Pre: Exec brief memo (this doc) distributed 48h before the dry run; heads submit hot topics by +36h
- Dry run: Exec scrub — 2026-03-17 10:00 PT (mandatory for speakers and owners)
- Post: 1-page recap + OKRs published to Confluence and Slack summary thread within 24h

Failover / risk playbook (if triggered between +7 days to all-hands):
- Risk: Dedicated capacity shortage (GPU quota hit)
  - Immediate: Pause new Dedicated provisioning for non-pilot accounts, shift high-latency traffic to hosted lower-cost regions, enable model-variant fallback (smaller quantized model)
  - Owner: Head of Dedicated (Miguel Soto)
  - Exec escalation: CPO + COO notified; choose either communicate limited capacity publicly or reroute traffic silently for 72h

- Risk: Runtime regression causing >5% increase in p95 latency or increased error rate
  - Immediate: Canary rollback to previous runtime build; open emergency PR for hotfix branch; promote patch after smoke tests
  - Owner: Serving runtime lead (Evelyn Chen)
  - Communication: Postmortem within 48h; brief to customers on remediation plan if customer-impacting

- Risk: Compliance gap discovered in Private onboarding (audit failure or missing controls)
  - Immediate: Freeze new Private pilot activations; switch new enterprise prospects to Supported Hosted mode with contractual protections and extended SLAs
  - Owner: Security & Compliance (Jordan Blake)
  - Exec note: Delay GA and be explicit in customer comms — better to push launch than to risk compliance event

Pre-all-hands exec scrub checklist (owners):
- OKR metrics: confirm owners and measurement methods (product-ops) — due 2026-03-14 (Asha)
- Slides: one-slide per priority + two data slides (engineering & go-to-market) — due 2026-03-16 (speakers)
- Demo: stable endpoint and canned dataset prepared; fallback recording in case live fails — due 2026-03-16 (platform-eng)
- Customer outreach: confirm one pilot customer willing to speak — due 2026-03-12 (customer-success)

Open questions / conversation items for scrub:
- Do we commit to the 20% cost target as a company KR or keep it an engineering metric? (trade-offs: customer-facing promise vs internal optimization runway)
- How aggressive should we be on Private GA messaging if only pilots are green? (options: soft-GA, pilot-limited GA, delay GA)
- Should we pre-announce a Developer activation challenge to drive signups at launch? pros/cons listed in playbook link.

Immediate next steps (this week):
- Distribute this brief to Exec + CC leads (Asha) — by EOD 2026-02-24
- Schedule 60m gov scrub (exec + speakers) for 2026-03-15 and dry run on 2026-03-17 (Ops)
- Engineering owners prepare 2 data slides and demo fallback (Miguel, Evelyn)
- Customer Success to confirm customer speaker and backup video (Jordan)

Notes to self / rough ideas (work-in-progress):
- Consider a short customer-facing one-pager that frames Q2 priorities as product benefits (latency/cost & Private security wins)
- Small tabletop exercise for execs about compliance failure scenario to rehearse messaging
- Keep the all-hands at 40 minutes; longer Q&A can be an optional drop-in after the main show.

Appendix: short links and artifacts
- Draft OKRs doc (team level link): https://confluence.redwood.ai/pages/OKR-Drafts-Q2
- Incidental tickets: JIRA-5487 (prefix caching), JIRA-5521 (KMS integration), JIRA-5574 (SDK quickstarts)
- Slack thread for prep: #allhands-q2-prep (pinned notes in thread)

-- end of brief --

(TODO: convert to a clean slide deck after exec scrub; pair down content to 1-side handout for leaders)

title: Summer midpoint all-hands playbook and contingency map
preview:
Purpose:
A lightweight, operational playbook to run the summer midpoint all-hands, present a consolidated midyear OKR draft to the company, and surface contingencies if capacity or quality targets slip. This is a working doc — expect edits from execs and product leads through the 1st March sync.

High level goals for the session (objective for the all-hands):
- Align on 3 company-level OKRs to carry into H2 with clear metrics and owners.
- Surface top 5 risks and mitigation commitments for the next 90 days.
- Communicate any experiential changes for Hosted API customers (pricing/SLAs/regions) and Dedicated ramp plan.
- Give teams a clear rollout cadence for rollouts, canaries, and fallback policies.

Proposed company OKRs (draft, numbers are draft targets — please flag feasibility)
OKR 1 — Reliable, predictable inference for production apps- KR 1.1: Reduce p95 generation latency for hosted API by 15% across standard workloads (baseline: Nov 2025 internal bench).- KR 1.2: Ship per-route tracing and token-level cost breakdown to Console GA (target: 80% routes instrumented).- KR 1.3: <15 incidents/month impacting SLA for paid Dedicated customers.
OKR 2 — Cost-conscious throughput improvements- KR 2.1: Reduce average tokens-per-USD by 20% via batching, prefix cache, and quantization recommendations for 3 top customer archetypes.- KR 2.2: Publish model-level cost playbook and automated suggestion engine prototype (internal Beta) to 10 pilot teams.
OKR 3 — Expand Private adoption for regulated customers- KR 3.1: Complete private VPC onboarding checklist automation and reduce setup time from 6w to 3w for typical deployments.- KR 3.2: Close 4 signed Private deals (with support + compliance add-on) by end of Q3.

Department callouts (short):
- Eng: need sign-off on canary rollback SLOs and runbook by 2026-03-01.- Product: finalize Console retention and billing mockups (Renee) by 2026-02-28.- Applied ML: deliver model-variant fallback matrix and calibration guidance for 5 high-risk models (Omar).

All-hands agenda (45 minutes, tight):
1) Opening (5m) — CEO framing: where we are vs. plan
2) Midyear OKR walkthrough (12m) — CFO + Head of Product present ask/targets
3) Reliability & capacity heartbeat (8m) — SRE/Serving runtime highlight mitigation for summer traffic
4) Customer signals (5m) — CS quick wins and escalations
5) Rollouts & playbook (8m) — What teams must do when an incident hits; live demo of per-route tracing
6) Q&A + sticky issues (7m) — collected in advance via #all-hands-q

Comms plan (pre/during/post):
- Pre (1 week): Exec email with 2-sentence summary + link to this doc; collect 3 anonymous questions. Owner: Aisha.
- Day of: Live Slack thread + short recording posted to All-Hands folder. Owner: Elena.
- Post (24–48h): Exec update digest with decisions and action owners. Owner: Marcus.

Contingency map — triggers and immediate actions (operational):
Trigger A: p95 latency increases > 25% for 24h for hosted API
Immediate actions:
- Auto-scale burst capacity to Dedicated pool (if available) and enable model-variant fallback to quantized variant. Owner: eng-infra oncall.
- Open incident channel #ops-incident and post customer notice template.
- Delay noncritical batch workloads and throttle non-production tokens.
Trigger owners: SRE -> escalate to CTO at 2h if no improvement.

Trigger B: Cost spikes > 30% vs rolling 7d baseline for top 10 customers
Immediate actions:
- Temporarily cap per-route qps and roll out cost-saver suggestion via Console (inform customer via CS).
- Run quick eval for output quality on quantized variant for affected routes.

Risk heatmap (top items):
- Capacity volatility in region eu-west-1 during cloud provider maintenance window (mitigation: pre-warm in one other region; communicate possible degraded throughput).
- Regression risk from newly onboarded open model: require eval set and canary on 1% traffic first.
- Sales/Legal exposure with Private customers assuming faster compliance timelines: call out explicit calendar and gating checklist.

Open questions / items to resolve before final OKR sign-off:
- Finance: acceptable downside to revenue if we pause non-critical beta customers for 2 weeks? (Finance input: Renee)
- Product: do we consolidate per-route tracing as an opt-in or default? (Ops cost vs. observability).
- Sales: what qualifies as a signed Private deal for KR counting? (Contract signed vs. MSA signed).

Decisions required this week (owners + target date):
- Finalize the three company OKRs and get exec approval (Owner: CEO, target: 2026-02-28).
- Approve canary rollback SLO and incident template (Owner: SRE lead, target: 2026-03-01).
- Approve customer comms language for potential rate-limiting scenarios (Owner: CS Head, target: 2026-03-03).

Quick checklist for team leads (to prepare for the all-hands):
- Submit one slide summarizing progress vs. OKR (top metric, top risk, request). Deadline: 2026-02-26.
- Pin 2 evaluation prompts for model-variant checks (Applied ML).
- Confirm per-route tracing feature flag behavior in staging by 2026-02-24 (Eng).

Notes to self / rough items (informal):
- Collect Qs via #all-hands-q channel; remind people to post before noon day-of.
- We should rehearse the latency mitigation demo with SRE (Marcus) — slice network to simulate region spike.
- TODO: add short customer-facing FAQ for potential rate limits (Elena).

Next steps:
- Iterate this doc with feedback from Marcus, Renee, Omar by 2026-02-25.
- Lock slides and send calendar invite for dress rehearsal 2026-03-02.

Appendix: Useful links and templates
- Incident customer notice template: https://confluence.redwood.ai/display/OPS/Customer+Notices
- Canary SLO draft: ENG-4921
- Private onboarding checklist (dev): https://confluence.redwood.ai/display/SEC/Private+Onboarding+Checklist

End of draft.
text:
Purpose:
A lightweight, operational playbook to run the summer midpoint all-hands, present a consolidated midyear OKR draft to the company, and surface contingencies if capacity or quality targets slip. This is a working doc — expect edits from execs and product leads through the 1st March sync.

High level goals for the session (objective for the all-hands):
- Align on 3 company-level OKRs to carry into H2 with clear metrics and owners.
- Surface top 5 risks and mitigation commitments for the next 90 days.
- Communicate any experiential changes for Hosted API customers (pricing/SLAs/regions) and Dedicated ramp plan.
- Give teams a clear rollout cadence for rollouts, canaries, and fallback policies.

Proposed company OKRs (draft, numbers are draft targets — please flag feasibility)
OKR 1 — Reliable, predictable inference for production apps- KR 1.1: Reduce p95 generation latency for hosted API by 15% across standard workloads (baseline: Nov 2025 internal bench).- KR 1.2: Ship per-route tracing and token-level cost breakdown to Console GA (target: 80% routes instrumented).- KR 1.3: <15 incidents/month impacting SLA for paid Dedicated customers.
OKR 2 — Cost-conscious throughput improvements- KR 2.1: Reduce average tokens-per-USD by 20% via batching, prefix cache, and quantization recommendations for 3 top customer archetypes.- KR 2.2: Publish model-level cost playbook and automated suggestion engine prototype (internal Beta) to 10 pilot teams.
OKR 3 — Expand Private adoption for regulated customers- KR 3.1: Complete private VPC onboarding checklist automation and reduce setup time from 6w to 3w for typical deployments.- KR 3.2: Close 4 signed Private deals (with support + compliance add-on) by end of Q3.

Department callouts (short):
- Eng: need sign-off on canary rollback SLOs and runbook by 2026-03-01.- Product: finalize Console retention and billing mockups (Renee) by 2026-02-28.- Applied ML: deliver model-variant fallback matrix and calibration guidance for 5 high-risk models (Omar).

All-hands agenda (45 minutes, tight):
1) Opening (5m) — CEO framing: where we are vs. plan
2) Midyear OKR walkthrough (12m) — CFO + Head of Product present ask/targets
3) Reliability & capacity heartbeat (8m) — SRE/Serving runtime highlight mitigation for summer traffic
4) Customer signals (5m) — CS quick wins and escalations
5) Rollouts & playbook (8m) — What teams must do when an incident hits; live demo of per-route tracing
6) Q&A + sticky issues (7m) — collected in advance via #all-hands-q

Comms plan (pre/during/post):
- Pre (1 week): Exec email with 2-sentence summary + link to this doc; collect 3 anonymous questions. Owner: Aisha.
- Day of: Live Slack thread + short recording posted to All-Hands folder. Owner: Elena.
- Post (24–48h): Exec update digest with decisions and action owners. Owner: Marcus.

Contingency map — triggers and immediate actions (operational):
Trigger A: p95 latency increases > 25% for 24h for hosted API
Immediate actions:
- Auto-scale burst capacity to Dedicated pool (if available) and enable model-variant fallback to quantized variant. Owner: eng-infra oncall.
- Open incident channel #ops-incident and post customer notice template.
- Delay noncritical batch workloads and throttle non-production tokens.
Trigger owners: SRE -> escalate to CTO at 2h if no improvement.

Trigger B: Cost spikes > 30% vs rolling 7d baseline for top 10 customers
Immediate actions:
- Temporarily cap per-route qps and roll out cost-saver suggestion via Console (inform customer via CS).
- Run quick eval for output quality on quantized variant for affected routes.

Risk heatmap (top items):
- Capacity volatility in region eu-west-1 during cloud provider maintenance window (mitigation: pre-warm in one other region; communicate possible degraded throughput).
- Regression risk from newly onboarded open model: require eval set and canary on 1% traffic first.
- Sales/Legal exposure with Private customers assuming faster compliance timelines: call out explicit calendar and gating checklist.

Open questions / items to resolve before final OKR sign-off:
- Finance: acceptable downside to revenue if we pause non-critical beta customers for 2 weeks? (Finance input: Renee)
- Product: do we consolidate per-route tracing as an opt-in or default? (Ops cost vs. observability).
- Sales: what qualifies as a signed Private deal for KR counting? (Contract signed vs. MSA signed).

Decisions required this week (owners + target date):
- Finalize the three company OKRs and get exec approval (Owner: CEO, target: 2026-02-28).
- Approve canary rollback SLO and incident template (Owner: SRE lead, target: 2026-03-01).
- Approve customer comms language for potential rate-limiting scenarios (Owner: CS Head, target: 2026-03-03).

Quick checklist for team leads (to prepare for the all-hands):
- Submit one slide summarizing progress vs. OKR (top metric, top risk, request). Deadline: 2026-02-26.
- Pin 2 evaluation prompts for model-variant checks (Applied ML).
- Confirm per-route tracing feature flag behavior in staging by 2026-02-24 (Eng).

Notes to self / rough items (informal):
- Collect Qs via #all-hands-q channel; remind people to post before noon day-of.
- We should rehearse the latency mitigation demo with SRE (Marcus) — slice network to simulate region spike.
- TODO: add short customer-facing FAQ for potential rate limits (Elena).

Next steps:
- Iterate this doc with feedback from Marcus, Renee, Omar by 2026-02-25.
- Lock slides and send calendar invite for dress rehearsal 2026-03-02.

Appendix: Useful links and templates
- Incident customer notice template: https://confluence.redwood.ai/display/OPS/Customer+Notices
- Canary SLO draft: ENG-4921
- Private onboarding checklist (dev): https://confluence.redwood.ai/display/SEC/Private+Onboarding+Checklist

End of draft.

title: Alignment Catalyst: Company Momentum Summary & Action Registry (Q2→Q3)
preview:
Purpose:
This working note is a condensed executive snapshot and action registry intended to drive company-wide alignment between Q2 close and Q3 kickoff. It synthesizes OKR candidates, a short all-hands run-of-show, prioritized cross-functional asks, and open decisions for the exec + program leads to resolve in the next two weeks.

Audience:
Exec Leadership, Product/Product Ops, Eng Leads, People Ops, Marketing (internal comms), Solutions/Customer Success.

High-level summary (TL;DR):
- Momentum: Host API adoption grew 28% QoQ; Dedicated capacity pilots converted 2 key enterprise accounts. We need to translate runway into operational reliability targets and a tighter product roadmap for Q3.
- Primary asks: finalize 3 company-level OKRs (one revenue/expansion, one reliability/ops, one developer adoption) + agree an all-hands narrative and a 20-minute leadership spotlight cadence.
- Timebox: decisions due by 2026-03-24 (leadership sync 03-25).

Proposed company-level OKRs (drafts — measurable KR examples included):
1) Objective: Deliver predictable inference economics for customers at scale
   KR1: Reduce median per-token cost for top-10 hosted workloads by 18% (measurement window: April→June)
   KR2: Ship 2 new quantization/engine profiles that reduce GPU usage per request by 25% for long-context models
   KR3: Sign 3 Dedicated capacity renewals (>$300k ARR each) or convert 70% of pilots to contracts
   Owner: Priya Nair (Revenue Operations)

2) Objective: Harden reliability and observability to enterprise SLAs
   KR1: Achieve 99.95% uptime for Dedicated clusters across regions for three consecutive 30-day windows
   KR2: Deploy token-level tracing in Console for 90% of production routes (opt-in customers)
   KR3: Reduce P95 tail-latency incidents by 40% (measured on critical routes)
   Owner: Miguel Reyes (Head of Eng)

3) Objective: Increase developer velocity and adoption of Redwood APIs
   KR1: Improve self-serve onboarding conversion rate from free trial to integration (webhook or API key usage) from 7% → 15%
   KR2: Publish 6 reference apps and 12 community snippets for common workloads (plugins, rerankers, embeddings pipelines)
   KR3: Reduce first-call latency (API cold-start) for hosted models by 25%
   Owner: Asha Patel (VP Product)

Notes on framing/why these:
- OKR #1 ties directly to revenue and customer discussions over the last quarter; reducing per-token cost is a unifying metrics lever for customers and product engineering.
- OKR #2 reflects increasing inbound enterprise asks for SLAs; we need a measurable reliability bar before ramping Dedicated sales.
- OKR #3 supports PLG motion to keep developer growth healthy while we scale enterprise.

Priority initiatives (cross-functional short list — Q3 candidate workstreams):
- Inference Cost Toolkit (Optimize): batching heuristics, KV cache hints surfaced in Console, recommendations engine. Lead: Ethan Park.
- Dedicated Runbook + Chaos Readiness: readiness checklist for pilot→contract customers, incident playbooks, smoke tests. Lead: SRE subteam (Kiran Gupta).
- Developer Quickstarts Sprint: 6 reference apps (chat app, summarizer, search-augmenter, embeddings-store, reranker, function-call sample). Lead: DevX (Nina Alvarez).
- Signal & Telemetry Sprint: token-level tracing rollout, route-level SLOs, alert fatigue reduction. Lead: Observability (Jordan Lee).

All-hands plan (compact, product-forward):
- Goal: Communicate Q3 north-star, celebrate hard wins from Q2, and give concrete asks to teams (no longer than 60 minutes).
- Proposed run-of-show (60 min):
   0–5m: Welcome + quick pulse (CEO) — one-slide KPIs (growth, revenue, uptime)
   5–15m: Customer story vignette (Solutions + Customer) — 2 min video clip + 3 min highlight
   15–30m: Leadership spotlight (product + eng) — 10-minute demo: \"How we saved X% per-token for a top workload\"
   30–40m: Ops & Reliability commit — SRE → clear SLA rollout calendar and what to expect
   40–50m: People & culture quick update (new hires, hiring focus areas)
   50–60m: Live AMA (pre-collected questions + 10 live minutes)
- Format notes: pre-reads circulated 72 hours before (1-pager), two rehearsals (exec + tech leads), closed-caption recording; quick feedback pulse (2-question survey) at end.

Communications & cadence (internal):
- Pre-read owners: Exec summary (Anika), OKR one-pager (Asha), Roadmap bullets (Miguel). Due to comms by 2026-03-20.
- Rehearsal schedule: 03-22 dry run, 03-24 tech-check with AV.
- Post-all-hands follow-up: publish action registry in Confluence, run a 30-day check-in for owners (owners listed below).

Action registry (initial — each entry is owner / decision / due):
- Priya Nair / Finalize commercial KR thresholds + renewals target definition / due 2026-03-24
- Miguel Reyes / Confirm Dedicated uptime SLOs and escalation policy / due 2026-03-20
- Asha Patel / Finalize developer onboarding funnel metric + instrumentation plan / due 2026-03-21
- Ethan Park / Deliver Optimize sprint scope doc (batching + cache hints) / due 2026-03-18
- Nina Alvarez / Ship first two reference apps (chat + embeddings-store) as beta / due 2026-04-08
- Jordan Lee / Telemetry rollout plan (token-trace pilot customers list) / due 2026-03-26
- Laura Chen / Headcount ask for Q3 (prioritized roles + hiring timeline) / due 2026-03-25

Risks & open questions (items we need to resolve before exec sync):
- Risk: If telemetry rollout is delayed, we cannot validate KR2 for reliability on schedule. Mitigation: scoped pilot with 2 enterprise customers and one internal workload.
- Question: What is the commercial threshold to label a Dedicated pilot as \"high probability to convert\"? (Finance needs a $ threshold for forecasting.)
- Question: Do we publicly commit to 99.95% for Dedicated in the next quarter, or make it a private SLA for pilot customers first? Trade-offs: market signal vs operational readiness.

Meeting artifacts & links (short list):
- Q2 retrospective notes (Confluence): https://confluence.redwood.com/display/STRAT/Q2+Retrospective
- Draft OKRs board (Miro): https://miro.com/app/board/uXj-EXAMPLE/
- Tickets referenced: COMP-1987, ENG-2144, OBS-3219

Prep checklist for owners (quick):
- [ ] Load one slide showing the direct customer impact of your KR (metric + example)
- [ ] Two talking points: 1) what success looks like by end of Q3; 2) one ask from other teams
- [ ] Confirm at least one rehearsal attendee (exec or tech lead)

Appendix: Suggested leader talking prompts (one-liners to keep narrative tight):
- CEO: \"We turn model performance into predictable economics — that is our unfair advantage this year.\"
- VP Product: \"These three KRs knit product, ops, and sales together — they move dollars and trust.\"
- Head of Eng: \"Our work this quarter is about removing tail risk: predictable latency and transparent costing.\"

Next steps (immediate):
1) Feedback on OKR wording by 2026-03-17 (reply inline in this doc)
2) Owners confirm action registry items by 2026-03-18
3) Exec sync to finalize on 2026-03-25 (primary decision gate)

Notes to reviewers: this is intentionally compact. Expect iterations — suggest changing only KRs with clear measurement definitions rather than objectives. If you disagree with an OKR, propose an alternate measurable KR in your reply.

-- End of working snapshot --

title: company-all-hands-facilitator-script-and-rapid-qna-v0-1
preview:
Purpose: A concise facilitator script, contingency playbook, and rapid Q&A format for the upcoming company all-hands. Use this as the single source for run-of-show, speaker prompts, timeboxing rules, and follow-ups. This doc is intentionally prescriptive so the MC can keep pace and protect speaker time while surfacing high-impact questions and OKR decisions.

Objectives (top-level):
- Surface 3 cross-company decisions that need exec alignment this quarter.
- Communicate high-level OKR progress and 2 notable pivots.
- Maintain a 60-minute total runtime with a structured 10-minute rapid Q&A at the end.
- Capture and triage every question to an owner within 24 hours.

Audience: All employees (engineering, product, GTM, SRE, CS, security). Expect ~140 attendees live + stream.

Pre-reads (send 48h before):
- 1-page OKR snapshot (what’s green/yellow/red) — link above.
- Short exec update deck (5 slides): priorities, capacity, customer signal.
- Two short ask artifacts for decisions (policy doc + proposal).

Run-of-show (60 minutes) — facilitator cues included:
00:00–03:00 — Welcome + housekeeping (MC: opening line)
  - Script: "Good afternoon — quick logistics: live captioning on, recording on, use the Q&A box for long questions and 'raise hand' for lightning asks. We'll capture everything and assign owners."
  - House rules: cameras optional, mute when not speaking, 1 follow-up per person in live Q&A.

03:00–12:00 — CEO state of the company (9 min)
  - Cue slide 1: vision -> 90 sec recap.
  - Cue slide 2: market momentum & customer signals (2 min).
  - Cue slide 3: three decisions requiring alignment (5 min) — each decision: one-sentence context, recommended option, ask for thumbs up.
  - MC prompt: "After each decision, we'll capture reactions in the Q&A and record a small decision note in the doc."

12:00–28:00 — Product + Platform update (16 min)
  - Owner: Head of Product (8 min) — product wins, one product pivot, ask for resource trade-off.
  - Owner: Head of Serving Runtime (8 min) — latency/cost metrics & Dedicated/Private rollout status.
  - Facilitator tag-lines: "Call out the metrics that map to customer SLAs" and "State mitigation steps for any red metrics."

28:00–38:00 — Customer & GTM highlight (10 min)
  - Customer story (3 min) + net-new pipeline signal (3 min) + go-to-market ask (4 min).

38:00–48:00 — Ops, Security & Reliability brief (10 min)
  - SRE quick wins (4 min), Security posture (3 min), Compliance/regulatory note for Private deployments (3 min).
  - If any item is high-risk, MC to call for a follow-up decision session (owner + deadline).

48:00–58:00 — Rapid Q&A (10 min) — structured format
  - Format: 6 lightning slots (90 seconds each) + 1 wildcard slot (2 minutes).
  - Selection: pre-filtered by moderators; priority to cross-team, decision-critical, customer-impacting questions.
  - MC script: "We'll take six lightning questions. Each speaker: 60 seconds to ask, 30 seconds for a direct answer. If the topic needs more, it goes to the parking lot with an owner."
  - Moderator instructions: tag each question with one of: [#decision, #bug, #followup, #context]. Assign owner in real time.

58:00–60:00 — Close & next steps (2 min)
  - Quick recap of decisions made, owners, and deadlines. Reminder: survey link + office hours for deeper dives.

Facilitator playbook (practical tips):
- Timeboxing: visible countdown in the presenter view. Stop speakers at the minute mark if overrunning (polite interjection script provided below).
- Polite interjection script: "Great signal — to keep us on time, let's park the deep dive and assign an owner. Can you capture this in the doc and lead a 30-min follow-up?"
- Tone: low-friction, upward-facing candor, remind people of safety to speak up.
- Decision framing: for each 'decision' slot, state context, options, recommended option, and 'ask' (approve/revisit/defer).

Rapid Q&A moderation guide:
- Moderator roles: 1 primary moderator (Priya Shah) to curate, 1 backchannel moderator (Slack) to route live reactions, 1 note-taker (Jon Park) to capture owners and action items.
- Filtering heuristics: prioritize questions that: affect more than one team; block delivery; concern a public customer; relate to safety/compliance.
- If >12 high-priority Qs, run a 30-min follow-up office hours and communicate time/window in chat.

Contingency & outage scripts:
- Streaming failure (common): immediate step — MC says "We're experiencing streaming issues; we'll continue the in-room session and post the recording. Please use the Q&A thread — we'll triage." Assign streaming owner and post status in #all-hands.
- Speaker no-show: have a 3-minute placeholder (short customer video or pre-recorded highlight). If the missing speaker is critical, CEO to offer a 2-minute bridge and reschedule a follow-up session.
- Major incident occurring same day: postpone the all-hands or move to a 'status-focused' format. Decision to postpone rests with CEO + Head of SRE. Notify company within 30 minutes of decision.

Decision capture template (to be filled live by note-taker):
- Decision ID (e.g., AH-2026-03-1)
- Short statement (one sentence)
- Options considered (bullet)
- Chosen option
- Owner (name + email)
- Deadline (YYYY-MM-DD)
- Follow-up meeting link (if scheduled)

Post-meeting follow-up timeline:
- Within 6 hours: publish recording + raw Q&A export + decision notes.
- Within 24 hours: owners assigned on each action and initial contact to requesters.
- Within 72 hours: schedule any post-decision working sessions.

Rehearsal checklist (48–24 hours prior):
- Confirm slides for all speakers (size & font).
- AV check (mic, camera, room mix, captions) — run once with CEO and Product lead.
- Moderator dry run (Priya + Jon + MC) for rapid Q&A flow.
- Confirm recording destination and SEO-friendly title for the published video.

Speaker-facing scripts (short snippets to reduce ad-libbing):
- Overrun nudge: "Wrap in 30s: key punchline + one action for the audience."
- Decision ask: "We are asking you to approve XYZ so we can move forward; this enables [benefit]."
- Vulnerability line (for red metrics): "We expect this to be uncomfortable — here is why, and here is how we are mitigating it."

Feedback and measurement:
- Survey (2 minutes) distributed after the event: 3 questions (useful? decisions clear? any blocking asks?).
- Metrics to monitor: live attendance, recording plays (7-day), % of questions closed within 72 hours, NPS-like item for all-hands utility.

Parking lot & follow-ups (live capture examples):
- "OKR cadence: deeper sync" -> Owner: Marcus Rivera -> Deadline: 2026-03-18
- "Dedicated capacity pricing clarity" -> Owner: Elena Morales -> Deadline: 2026-03-12
- "Security audit schedule for Private" -> Owner: Security Lead (TBD) -> Deadline: 2026-03-20

Open questions for planning team (to resolve in rehearsal):
- Do we want a live polling block to sense consent on the three exec decisions, or do thumbs-up in chat suffice?
- Confirm: who publishes the final decision note (CEO staff or Ops)?

Appendix: sequences to preserve sanity during the show
- If one segment runs long by >2 min, compress the product and GTM sections by 50/50.
- Keeper rule: never cut the CEO's decision block. Shorten narrative, not decisions.

Notes to self (facilitator): be prescriptive, show visible time, and protect the audience's time. This doc is intentionally concise — do not read it verbatim, use as cues.

End of doc.

  - Cue slide 3: three decisions requiring alignment (5 min) — each decision: one-sentence context, recommended option, ask for thumbs up.
  - MC prompt: "After each decision, we'll capture reactions in the Q&A and record a small decision note in the doc."

12:00–28:00 — Product + Platform update (16 min)
  - Owner: Head of Product (8 min) — product wins, one product pivot, ask for resource trade-off.
  - Owner: Head of Serving Runtime (8 min) — latency/cost metrics & Dedicated/Private rollout status.
  - Facilitator tag-lines: "Call out the metrics that map to customer SLAs" and "State mitigation steps for any red metrics."

28:00–38:00 — Customer & GTM highlight (10 min)
  - Customer story (3 min) + net-new pipeline signal (3 min) + go-to-market ask (4 min).

38:00–48:00 — Ops, Security & Reliability brief (10 min)
  - SRE quick wins (4 min), Security posture (3 min), Compliance/regulatory note for Private deployments (3 min).
  - If any item is high-risk, MC to call for a follow-up decision session (owner + deadline).

48:00–58:00 — Rapid Q&A (10 min) — structured format
  - Format: 6 lightning slots (90 seconds each) + 1 wildcard slot (2 minutes).
  - Selection: pre-filtered by moderators; priority to cross-team, decision-critical, customer-impacting questions.
  - MC script: "We'll take six lightning questions. Each speaker: 60 seconds to ask, 30 seconds for a direct answer. If the topic needs more, it goes to the parking lot with an owner."
  - Moderator instructions: tag each question with one of: [#decision, #bug, #followup, #context]. Assign owner in real time.

58:00–60:00 — Close & next steps (2 min)
  - Quick recap of decisions made, owners, and deadlines. Reminder: survey link + office hours for deeper dives.

Facilitator playbook (practical tips):
- Timeboxing: visible countdown in the presenter view. Stop speakers at the minute mark if overrunning (polite interjection script provided below).
- Polite interjection script: "Great signal — to keep us on time, let's park the deep dive and assign an owner. Can you capture this in the doc and lead a 30-min follow-up?"
- Tone: low-friction, upward-facing candor, remind people of safety to speak up.
- Decision framing: for each 'decision' slot, state context, options, recommended option, and 'ask' (approve/revisit/defer).

Rapid Q&A moderation guide:
- Moderator roles: 1 primary moderator (Priya Shah) to curate, 1 backchannel moderator (Slack) to route live reactions, 1 note-taker (Jon Park) to capture owners and action items.
- Filtering heuristics: prioritize questions that: affect more than one team; block delivery; concern a public customer; relate to safety/compliance.
- If >12 high-priority Qs, run a 30-min follow-up office hours and communicate time/window in chat.

Contingency & outage scripts:
- Streaming failure (common): immediate step — MC says "We're experiencing streaming issues; we'll continue the in-room session and post the recording. Please use the Q&A thread — we'll triage." Assign streaming owner and post status in #all-hands.
- Speaker no-show: have a 3-minute placeholder (short customer video or pre-recorded highlight). If the missing speaker is critical, CEO to offer a 2-minute bridge and reschedule a follow-up session.
- Major incident occurring same day: postpone the all-hands or move to a 'status-focused' format. Decision to postpone rests with CEO + Head of SRE. Notify company within 30 minutes of decision.

Decision capture template (to be filled live by note-taker):
- Decision ID (e.g., AH-2026-03-1)
- Short statement (one sentence)
- Options considered (bullet)
- Chosen option
- Owner (name + email)
- Deadline (YYYY-MM-DD)
- Follow-up meeting link (if scheduled)

Post-meeting follow-up timeline:
- Within 6 hours: publish recording + raw Q&A export + decision notes.
- Within 24 hours: owners assigned on each action and initial contact to requesters.
- Within 72 hours: schedule any post-decision working sessions.

Rehearsal checklist (48–24 hours prior):
- Confirm slides for all speakers (size & font).
- AV check (mic, camera, room mix, captions) — run once with CEO and Product lead.
- Moderator dry run (Priya + Jon + MC) for rapid Q&A flow.
- Confirm recording destination and SEO-friendly title for the published video.

Speaker-facing scripts (short snippets to reduce ad-libbing):
- Overrun nudge: "Wrap in 30s: key punchline + one action for the audience."
- Decision ask: "We are asking you to approve XYZ so we can move forward; this enables [benefit]."
- Vulnerability line (for red metrics): "We expect this to be uncomfortable — here is why, and here is how we are mitigating it."

Feedback and measurement:
- Survey (2 minutes) distributed after the event: 3 questions (useful? decisions clear? any blocking asks?).
- Metrics to monitor: live attendance, recording plays (7-day), % of questions closed within 72 hours, NPS-like item for all-hands utility.

Parking lot & follow-ups (live capture examples):
- "OKR cadence: deeper sync" -> Owner: Marcus Rivera -> Deadline: 2026-03-18
- "Dedicated capacity pricing clarity" -> Owner: Elena Morales -> Deadline: 2026-03-12
- "Security audit schedule for Private" -> Owner: Security Lead (TBD) -> Deadline: 2026-03-20

Open questions for planning team (to resolve in rehearsal):
- Do we want a live polling block to sense consent on the three exec decisions, or do thumbs-up in chat suffice?
- Confirm: who publishes the final decision note (CEO staff or Ops)?

Appendix: sequences to preserve sanity during the show
- If one segment runs long by >2 min, compress the product and GTM sections by 50/50.
- Keeper rule: never cut the CEO's decision block. Shorten narrative, not decisions.

Notes to self (facilitator): be prescriptive, show visible time, and protect the audience's time. This doc is intentionally concise — do not read it verbatim, use as cues.

End of doc.

title: Sourcing Calibration + 90-day Onboarding Playbook (WIP)
preview:
Purpose:
This is a working playbook to (1) align sourcers, hiring managers, and interviewers on candidate profile and evaluation, and (2) provide a repeatable 30/60/90 onboarding checklist for technical hires joining infra/serving-runtime teams. Focus role examples: Senior/Lead Technical Program Manager (Serving Runtime), Senior Software Engineer (Inference), Infra SRE.

Summary/Goals:
- Reduce time-to-offer for infra org roles from 42 days -> 28 days by standardizing scorecards and triage steps.
- Improve interview consistency: define 4 core evaluation buckets (technical depth, systems judgment, program execution, collaboration & communication).
- Ensure new hires hit meaningful milestones by week 4/8/12 with clear owner tasks and early feedback loops.

Sourcing Calibration (short):
- Ideal profile shorthand: 5+ years building/operating GPU-backed services or distributed CPU-heavy workloads; demonstrated cross-team program ownership; has shipped optimizations (cost/latency) not just features.
- Red flags: only ML research experience w/out production exposure, no quantifiable impact on latency/cost, vague descriptions of "ownership".
- Primary channels: internal referrals, specialized infra channels (HPC/ML infra Slack communities), alumni networks from scale infra orgs.
- Quick boolean checklist for sourcers:
  - Has experience with production model serving or distributed caching? (Y/N)
  - Example of measurable perf improvement? (Y/N)
  - Program/project across >2 teams? (Y/N)
  - Comfortable with ambiguity + on-call history? (Y/N)
- Sourcing note: prioritize candidates who can explain tradeoffs (latency vs cost, batching vs real-time) in concrete terms. Wildcards: strong SRE candidates who lack ML infra but have real-time systems experience.

Interview Rubric (calibration draft):
Score 1-4 for each dimension (1=miss, 2=basic, 3=good, 4=strong) with examples to anchor.
1) Technical Depth (architecture, system design) - 30%
  - 4: Designs a sharded KV + cache + fallback system, enumerates failure modes, can sketch KV cache eviction impacts on latency and correctness, suggests mitigations.
  - 2: Gives high-level system blocks but misses critical failure modes or cost implications.
2) Systems Judgment & Tradeoffs - 25%
  - 4: Discusses batching tradeoffs, memory vs throughput, quantization impacts, and tradeoffs for multi-tenant GPU pools.
3) Program Execution & Delivery - 25%
  - 4: Lays out clear rollout plan (canary, metrics, rollback), risk matrix, stakeholder map; examples of shipping cross-team rollout.
4) Communication & Collaboration - 15%
  - 4: Communicates clearly to both engineers and product stakeholders; shows examples of resolving ambiguous requirements.
5) Cultural / Redwoods Fit (bias for shipping, reliability focus) - 5% (pass/fail soft gate)

Interview loop template (for senior infra roles):
- Recruiter screen (20m): logistics, compensation, basic fit, red flags.
- Hiring manager technical screen (45m): architecture depth + recent project deep-dive.
- Systems design + tradeoffs (60m): cross-functional scenarios + on-call incidents.
- Program execution / rollout case (45m): sequence-of-events, metrics to observe, rollback plan.
- Peer sync / team fit (30m): culture, collaboration, multi-team experience.
- Debrief: all interviewers score independently -> 24-hour calibration meeting with HM + recruiter.

Rubric calibration notes (how to run):
- For each hire cohort, run a 1-hour calibration session where 3 past candidate recordings (or notes) are rescored by the panel to align scoring anchors.
- Keep a one-page doc with 3 exemplar hires (high, medium, low) for each role to reduce drift.
- Quarterly: review score distributions and flag any interviewer outliers.

Offer & Compensation checklist (short):
- Confirm band + comp range with finance (PMs: Band 5/6 ; Senior Eng: Band 5/6)
- Check equity pool availability, sign-off from VP People Ops.
- Pre-offer: hiring manager confirms role impact statement and 90-day milestones.

Pre-boarding tasks (owner tags):
- IT: laptop + GPU-capable accessories ordered (people-ops / IT) - due before day 0.
- Security: access request sent for internal infra repos, cloud (prod-read only until week 3) - (security).
- Manager: first-week schedule + intro docs + mentor assigned (manager).
- Slack: create private #onboard-[name] and invite team + buddy (recruiter).

30/60/90 Onboarding checklist (technical hire primer)
Week 0 (pre-boarding):
- Account set up: Okta, GH, internal CI, infra dashboards.
- Read: serving-runtime README, recent postmortem, current perf/latency dashboard link.
- Assign buddy and schedule 1:1s.

Week 1 (orientation + context):
- Goal: understand codebase boundaries, CI pipelines, main infra services.
- Tasks: clone repos, run local dev env, run a tiny integration that calls local model endpoint.
- Meetings: team demo, SRE runbook overview, product/pm alignment.
- Deliverable: small write-up: "how inference flow works at a high level" (shared with manager).

Week 2-4 (first project & visibility):
- Goal: complete a scoped, low-risk task (eg: add a metric, fix flaky test, improve a dev workflow).
- Tasks: pair with buddy on an infra change; submit PR and shepherd through review.
- Checkpoint: 1:1 with manager to review progress and adjust 30-day goals.

Week 5-8 (ownership & impact):
- Goal: lead a small rollout (canary) or own a bug class.
- Tasks: own post-deploy metrics for your change; write a short runbook or playbook.
- Deliverable: presentation in team sync: "my first impact and next steps".

Week 9-12 (drive outcomes):
- Goal: demonstrate measurable impact (eg: reduced p99 latency for a path, or reduced ops MTTR).
- Tasks: propose a 90-day improvement project and get buy-in.
- Checkpoint: formal 90-day review with manager + people-ops.

Manager and Buddy responsibilities (quick):
- Manager: weekly 1:1s, set clear priorities, remove blockers, bi-weekly feedback.
- Buddy: day-to-day context, codebase orientation, review first PRs.

Open questions / TODOs:
- Which infra onboarding labs should be required (we have a repo of "infra labs" but need to pick 3 for all infra hires). TODO: Aisha to propose labs by 2026-03-20.
- Decide standard ramp signals for hiring manager to use when recommending extension to probation vs confirm.
- Should we require "on-call shadow" during week 2 for all SRE/infra hires? Pros/cons noted in comments.

Quick links for new hires (placeholders):
- Serving runtime README: https://git.redwood/serving-runtime/README
- On-call runbooks: https://confluence.redwood/oncall/runbooks
- Observability dashboards (prod + staging): https://grafana.redwood/boards/inference

Notes to self (informal):
- Keep this doc lightweight — aim for a 1-pager cheat sheet that can be pasted into offer emails.
- Avoid heavy legal language in pre-boarding tasks; keep sensitive steps gated to security.
- Capture two exemplar scorecards (one strong hire, one marginal) to help calibrate interviewers.

Revision history (WIP):
- 2026-02-15: initial draft (Priya)
- 2026-02-22: added rubric anchors (Marco)
- 2026-03-05: added onboarding week breakdown (Benji)
- 2026-03-10: pulled in sourcing checklist + TODOs (Aisha)

title: Skill Shelf: Rapid Pool & 48hr Probe for Contingency Hires
preview:
Purpose:
This playbook describes a compact process to source, evaluate, and onboard contingency / emergency hires from a lightweight internal/external rapid pool. The core goal is to minimize time-to-deploy for critical gaps (eg. incident coverage, short-term feature push, leave coverage) while preserving signal on production readiness and team fit.

Scope:
- Roles: primarily engineering (platform, infra, serving-runtime), SRE, and solutions-engineering contractors; adapt for product or ops roles as needed.
- Timeline target: candidate vetted and accepted within 48 hours of probe start; minimal onboarding to productive contributor within 3–7 days.
- Not for full-time strategic hires unless converted through normal loop.

High-level flow:
1) Trigger (owner: hiring manager + people-ops): identify gap and select contingency path. Create a short requisition in HR system citing emergency reason (link HR ticket), priority level, and requested skill pillars.
2) Pull from Skill Shelf (owner: people-ops): query internal rapid-pool and approved vendor list. Notify 3–5 pre-vetted candidates for 48hr probe. If none available, open to external shortlist from partners.
3) 48hr probe (owner: hiring manager + tech lead): timeboxed practical assignment + 60-minute pair-review session. Decision after probe.
4) Rapid offer & onboarding (owner: people-ops + eng manager): standard short-form offer, access gating, 72hr lightweight onboarding checklist.

48hr Probe: structure and rubric
- Day 0: kickoff (30 min) — context, acceptance criteria, environment access notes, and example input/output. Probe must be scoped to 4–6 hours of focused work.
- Deliverable: small reproducible artifact (bug fix, microservice patch, performance tweak, infra runbook, or test harness) plus README describing approach and edge cases.
- Day 1: async work by candidate (4–6h).
- Day 2: 60-minute paired review session (30 min demo + 30 min deep questions).
- Outcome: pass / conditional / fail within 24 hours of demo.

Evaluation rubric (numeric signals used for pass decision):
- Functional correctness (0–4): does the deliverable meet stated acceptance criteria?
- Production awareness (0–4): CI, testing, rollout considerations, monitoring hooks, safety/fallback reasoning.
- Debuggability & observability (0–3): README clarity, logging, error handling, how to reproduce.
- Time and communication (0–2): met time expectations, clear async updates in PR and during demo.
- Team fit and collaboration (0–3): respectful communication during pairing, openness to feedback.
Pass threshold: total >= 9 with no single critical dimension (functional correctness or production awareness) below 2. Conditional offers allowed with a 7–14 day shadowing ramp.

Probe assignment examples (tailorable):
- Serving-runtime engineer: fix a performance regression on an internal inference benchmark, add a micro-benchmark and a regression guard in CI.
- SRE: write a small automation to rotate a flaky job, include metrics and an alert policy draft.
- Solutions engineer: implement a short reproducible demo integrating Redwood API with a mock customer flow and document expected costs.

Minimal onboarding checklist (first 72 hours):
- Pre-acceptance: security screening + short NDAs (handled by people-ops).
- Day 0 (pre-start): account provisioning request created (Slack, Google, repo read access).
- Day 1: orient (1 hour) with manager + 30m buddy pairing; join team channel; run the repo bootstrap steps; run the baseline test suite.
- Day 2: shadow rotation — handle a small bug or support ticket under supervision; checklist: run perf harness, run lint/test flow, confirm logging/metrics.
- Day 3: independent small deliverable with visibility meeting.

Handoff & knowledge capture:
- Every probe must produce a short Playbook Note (1–2 pages) stored in the team drive: goal, approach, artifacts, CI commands, known gaps, and on-call considerations.
- Tag playbook note with skill tags for future Skill Shelf indexing.

Risk controls and guardrails:
- No escalation of sensitive PII or customer data access without full security clearance. People-ops must verify required approvals before granting elevated access.
- Timebox and scope enforcement: probes should not expand beyond intended hours; use canary branches and short-lived credentials.
- Conversion to full-time: must pass a second-stage interview to evaluate long-term culture and growth fit.

Operational metrics to track (owner: people-ops + hiring manager):
- Time from trigger to probe start (target < 24 hours).
- Probe completion rate and pass rate.
- Time to first meaningful contribution (target median <= 7 days).
- Candidate satisfaction score (post-probe quick survey).

Communication templates (quick snippets):
- Probe invite: short subject + 3 bullet job context + estimated time commitment + sandbox link + start window.
- Offer note for contingency hires: short-form offer with conversion pathway and expected ramp checkpoints.

Open questions / follow-ups (notes to self):
- Should we maintain a 6-month revalidation cadence for Skill Shelf candidates?
- Integrate automated indexing of playbook notes into the People Ops dashboard.
- Consider a vendor panel for global coverage in off-hours (APAC).

Appendix: quick checklist (one-liner items)
- [ ] Trigger created in HR system (priority field set)
- [ ] People-ops alerted + pool queried
- [ ] Candidate invited with probe doc
- [ ] Probe kickoff scheduled within 24h
- [ ] Paired demo scheduled and feedback recorded
- [ ] Offer decision communicated within 24h of demo
- [ ] Onboarding checklist completed (72h)
- [ ] Playbook note added to team drive

Contact: Leah Brooks (people-ops) — for playbook edits, exceptions, and post-mortem reviews.
