- Probe invite: short subject + 3 bullet job context + estimated time commitment + sandbox link + start window.
- Offer note for contingency hires: short-form offer with conversion pathway and expected ramp checkpoints.

Open questions / follow-ups (notes to self):
- Should we maintain a 6-month revalidation cadence for Skill Shelf candidates?
- Integrate automated indexing of playbook notes into the People Ops dashboard.
- Consider a vendor panel for global coverage in off-hours (APAC).

Appendix: quick checklist (one-liner items)
- [ ] Trigger created in HR system (priority field set)
- [ ] People-ops alerted + pool queried
- [ ] Candidate invited with probe doc
- [ ] Probe kickoff scheduled within 24h
- [ ] Paired demo scheduled and feedback recorded
- [ ] Offer decision communicated within 24h of demo
- [ ] Onboarding checklist completed (72h)
- [ ] Playbook note added to team drive

Contact: Leah Brooks (people-ops) — for playbook edits, exceptions, and post-mortem reviews.

title: Objective mapping + fallthrough runbook (Q2 prep)
preview:
Purpose: lightweight operational mapping for the upcoming company alignment day (two-hour all-staff window + async prep). This is a working runbook — intent is to help execs and leads land a single objective surface, specify fallthrough behaviors if we need to compress scope, and provide a concise exec update template for the meeting.

Top-level constraints and context:
- Company thesis: prioritize inference serving reliability and predictable economics — keep product investments that materially reduce unit cost or improve latency SLOs.
- Timebox: 2 hours synchronized (all-staff) + 3 working days of team-level follow-ups.
- Audience: founders, execs, eng leads, product leads, solutions eng, growth reps.

Running agenda (executive run-of-show - sketch):
1) 0:00–0:05 — Welcome and framing (CEO) — 3 bullets: mission signal, 1 priority ask, what success looks like at the end of the day.
2) 0:05–0:20 — High-level metrics snapshot (headcount, revenue run rate, host/dedicated/private adoption mix, latency median and 99p) — slide + live callouts (CFO + Head of Infra).
3) 0:20–0:45 — Top 3 company objectives (each 8 min): brief owner statement, 1 exemplar KR, one dependency ask.
4) 0:45–1:10 — Cross-functional risk triage (panel) — identify top 6 risks; mark fallthrough triggers and owners.
5) 1:10–1:35 — Operational plays: dedicated/multi-region rollout plan and cost-savings bets (Platform + Infra).
6) 1:35–1:50 — People/Capacity: hiring hotspots, critical ICs, contractor windows (People Ops).
7) 1:50–2:00 — Commitments and immediate next steps: what teams do in next 72 hours.

Objective surface (proposed — short declarative statements):
A: Deliver 30% reduction in per-token inference cost on hosted API for typical PLG flows (baseline = Jan 2026).
B: Achieve 99.9% uptime for Dedicated pools with predictable p99 latency under committed traffic profiles.
C: Ship private deployment onboarding path for three regulated customers with audit logs and KMS integration.

Suggested Key Results (examples; teams should refine):
- A1: Reduce average token CPU/GPU time by 30% through batching, KV-cache affinity, and two model quantization profiles applied to top-10 routes. Measure: token-second per request.
- A2: Deploy auto-adaptive batching on the hosted API for small bursts; demonstrate 15% cost savings on sample workload.
- B1: End-to-end SLO for dedicated: 99.9% availability per month and p99 latency < 350ms on 512-token synthetic workload.
- C1: Deliver VPC private control plane template, HSM/KMS terraform modules, and onboarding playbook; onboard first customer in pilot region.

Fallthrough logic (how we compress scope if we need speed or risk grows):
- Trigger signals: sustained revenue miss >5% M/M at mid-quarter review; infra regression causing >0.5% failed requests; hiring lag for critical infra roles >8 weeks.
- Fallthrough chain: priority 1: narrow A to top-3 routes (reduce scope, preserve cost target for high-value traffic).
- Priority 2: shift non-customer-facing optimizations (experiments) to Optimize backlog; preserve Dedicated SLA work.
- Priority 3: postpone new Private deployments larger than pilot to next quarter.

Risk register (compact):
- R1: Quantization quality regressions — mitigation: gated A/B eval harness, rollback feature toggles, commit-level traceability. Owner: Applied ML.
- R2: Autoscaler instability under mixed workload — mitigation: synthetic canary, multi-pool throttles, fallback to single-model warm pool. Owner: Platform Eng.
- R3: Sales churn on slow Private onboarding — mitigation: pre-approved compliance checklist and dedicated onboarding engineer. Owner: Solutions Eng + Sales Eng.

Communications plan (internal + external):
- Internal: short exec update email + three TL;DR bullets after the alignment day; team-specific action cards published to relevant Slack channels and a follow-up doc in /shared_drives/company/misc (72-hr cadence).
- External: customers >SLA tier get an early heads-up note for changes impacting APIs; public blog only after pilot stability (target: end of Q2).

Templates to use (quick):
- Exec update template (one paragraph + 3 bullets):
  Subject: Q2 alignment update — top commit(s)
  Body: 1-sentence outcome + 3 bullets: owner + measure + 72-hr next step

Decision heuristics (guide for stretch choices):
- Prefer moves that improve unit economics with limited incremental ops burden.
- Defer initiatives that require >3 months of uninterrupted dedicated engineering time unless projected revenue/strategic impact >3x cost.

Immediate TODOs (72-hr list):
- Leads to submit refined KR metrics to the objective sheet (link above) — due EOD +2. Owner: team leads.
- Applied ML to prepare A/B harness and example regression criteria for quantization (due +4 days). Owner: Ethan Park.
- Platform Eng to draft autoscaler canary plan and identify safe rollback points (due +5). Owner: No a h Li.
- Comms: draft exec update email and short FAQ for all-staff (due +1). Owner: Claire O'Neill.

Open questions / notes to self:
- Do we want a separate micro-OKR for community/OSS contributions? pros: helps recruitment and trust; cons: distracts from core cost/latency bets.
- How aggressive should we be on shipping private audit logs vs. offering pilot with limited retention? Need legal input.

Appendix (quick links and references):
- Confluence OKR grid (linked above) — canonical single source.
- Example Jira tags for fallthrough items: ENG-xxx/PLAT-yyy/AM-zzz.

Style notes for presenters: short declarative slides, one metric per slide, explicit owner callouts, and a single ask at the end of each objective section (what you need from other teams or execs).

This is a living draft — add comments below and update the linked OKR grid. We want this doc to be thin, actionable, and tolerant of rough edges — it's a coordination artifact, not a polished comms.

---
Change log:
- 2026-02-10 (Maya): initial draft and agenda scaffold.
- 2026-02-18 (Ethan): added KR examples for A and notes on gated A/B harness.
- 2026-03-02 (Claire): updated run-of-show and 72-hr TODOs; minor clarifications.

title: Churn Retrospective Playbook — Template & Case Workbook
preview:
Purpose:
This is a working template and case workbook for running churn retrospectives (postmortems for lost renewals or early terminations). Use this to capture the timeline, root causes, counterfactuals, and a prioritized action plan that feeds product, onboarding, and GTM. Keep it concise — this lives next to the renewal tracker and should be completed within 7 business days of loss.

How to use this doc (quick):
- Owner: CS lead on the account (AE or CSM) opens the doc within 48h of confirmed loss.
- Fill the TL;DR, timeline, and evidence fields first.
- Run a 30–60 minute internal retro with CS, Solutions Eng, Product PM, and SDR/AE as required.
- Publish a 1-paragraph public summary to the Exec Weekly digest and link to the full workbook.

TL;DR (1–2 lines):
- Account name: [ACME Corp] 
- ARR at risk / lost: [USD 120k ARR] 
- Loss date: [2026-01-18] 
- Primary loss reason (short): [Product fit — latency impacted customer flow] 
- Quick recommendation: [Fix latency regression in v2.1 rollout + offer dedicated throughput for 90 days]

High-level impact scoring (fill one):
- Strategic: high | mid | low -> [mid]
- Revenue: >$250k / $100k–$250k / <$100k -> [< $100k]
- Likelihood to winback (estimate): high | mid | low -> [low]

Contributors (internal):
- Name, role, note on involvement
- Priya Natarajan — CSM, point owner (onboarding, renewal discussions)
- Marco Li — Solutions Eng (technical postmortem + logs)
- Asha Patel — Product PM (tracked feature backlog impact)

Customer context snapshot (short):
- Industry: fintech (example)
- Primary use: real-time customer support summarization on web chat
- Deployment: Redwood-hosted API (multi-region)
- Onboarding start / production go-live: 2024-09-10 -> 2024-11-15
- Renewal cadence: annual (renewal due: 2026-02-01)

Timeline (chronological evidence):
- 2024-09-10: kickoff (PS engagement SOW signed).
- 2024-11-15: production go-live, first 2 weeks normal.
- 2025-12-05: customer reported intermittent high tail latency for long chat sessions (support ticket #CS-2191).
- 2026-01-04: Customer escalated to AE; requested SLA for 95th percentile latency.
- 2026-01-09: Temporary mitigation (shortening max tokens) suggested; partial improvement.
- 2026-01-12: Product rollout v2.1 (kernel changes / batch scheduler updated) — we see a regression in 95p latency in EU region.
- 2026-01-16: Customer decision meeting; no commitment to renew without SLA commitment.
- 2026-01-18: Customer gives formal notice: non-renewal; cites reliability and cost variability.
- Evidence: attached logs, support tickets, SLA emails (links above).

Root cause analysis (structured):
- Symptom(s): 95p latency spikes after v2.1; customer-facing timeouts at peak hours.
- Immediate cause: batch scheduler change increased per-request queueing for very long sequences in EU.
- Contributing factors:
  - Lack of a canary test specifically exercising long chat sessions at scale in EU.
  - No alert configured for 95p latency beyond 500ms in customer region.
  - Commercial: no formal SLA offering to this customer tier; price-sensitive.
  - Communication: late visibility to customer about rollout schedule.
- Root cause (5-why summary):
  1) Why did customer see latency spikes? Because v2.1 changed batch scheduling behavior.
  2) Why did v2.1 change impact them? Because their load has a heavy tail of long sequences.
  3) Why wasn't this caught? Because our integration tests didn't include long-tail session profiles for EU region.
  4) Why are those tests missing? No test matrix required long-sequence distribution for multi-region canary.
  5) Why not added? No explicit ownership between platform and product for region-specific workload tests.

Loss reasons (ranked):
- 1 (primary): Operational reliability regression from product rollout (v2.1) — 60%
- 2: Pricing/cost variability concerns during peaks — 20%
- 3: Contract and SLA mismatch (no dedicated offering available at the time) — 12%
- 4: Onboarding gaps (missing documented mitigation playbook for session truncation) — 8%

Counterfactuals (what if we had done X?):
- If we had canaried v2.1 against long-session workloads in EU with 100k tokens/hour, the spike would likely have been caught (probable win).
- If we offered a temporary dedicated pool SLA at renewal discussions, customer may have renewed while we fixed runtime (plausible win).
- If we had an automated 95p latency alert tied to the account, escalation would have been faster and perception improved (possible win).
- Score counterfactuals by feasibility (low/mid/high) and impact (low/mid/high) in the action table below.

Action plan (owner / ETA / priority):
- Safety: Immediate customer stabilization 
  - Owner: Marco Li (SE) — Action: Revert batch scheduler change in EU for the customer’s traffic profile or pin legacy kernel for 90 days. ETA: 72 hours. Priority: P0.
- Short-term product patch
  - Owner: Product (Asha) — Action: Add targeted fix to batch scheduler logic to handle long sequences. ETA: 2 weeks. Priority: P1.
- Monitoring & Alerts
  - Owner: Platform SRE — Action: Add 95p latency monitor per-customer per-region with auto-notify to CS. ETA: 1 week. Priority: P1.
- Commercial makegood
  - Owner: Sales Ops / AE — Action: Offer one-time credit + temporary discounted dedicated capacity for 90 days. ETA: immediate negotiation. Priority: P1.
- Process changes (prevent recurrence)
  - Owner: Eng + Product 
  - Action: Expand canary test matrix to include long-tail session distributions by region; formalize ownership for region-specific test coverage. ETA: Q1 roadmap. Priority: P2.

Playbook: win-back steps (if applicable) — short checklist:
- Did we implement stabilization and confirm 95p restored? [ ]
- Has CS sent an interim status note to customer with timeline? [ ]
- Is a commercial concession approved and documented? [ ]
- Do we offer a technical walk-through and post-fix validation with live traffic? [ ]
- If customer declines initial outreach, schedule 60-day follow-up.

Communication guidance (internal and external):
- Internal: tag #cs-churn-retros, add short summary to exec weekly, include technical root cause and product owner actions. Keep sensitive pricing details limited to Sales Ops and legal.
- External (to customer): 1-paragraph apology + what we fixed + what we will do to prevent recurrence + commercial remediation offered. Send from AE+CS.

Metrics to track (postmortem KPIs):
- Time-to-stabilize (target <72 hours)
- 95p latency per-region per-account (baseline vs post-fix)
- Number of churn cases citing product regressions (quarterly)
- Win-back rate after makegood (target 20% for similar-sized accounts)

Knowledge transfer / artifacts to update:
- Add a canary scenario to repo: test/ci/canary-long-sessions-eu (owner: platform eng)
- Update onboarding doc: cs/playbooks/session-truncate-mitigation.md
- Add new SLA tier offering to commercial product catalog (Sales Ops owner)

Open questions / follow-up required:
- Was the cost variability complaint purely from usage spikes or from overprovisioning? Owner: AE to confirm with billing.
- Is there a broader population of customers with similar long-tail workloads? Owner: Data Science to run cohort query (query id: DS-1292).

Example closing paragraph to publish (public-friendly):
\"We regret ACME Corp experienced increased latency after a scheduled runtime update (v2.1). We identified a scheduling change that impacted long-session traffic in the EU region and rolled back the risky change for their workload while delivering a targeted fix. We are offering a remediation package to ACME and have put automated alerts and additional canary tests in place to prevent recurrence.\"

Notes to self / cheap wins:
- Consider a small playbook doc for AEs: \"What to offer at the first sign of latency complaints\" (one-pager).
- Track similar postmortems to build a 6-month trend deck for product leadership.

Appendix: Example evidence links and minimal artifacts (paste links below)
- Support ticket thread: link to thread (internal)
- Latency dashboard snapshot: link to Grafana panel
- Patch diff: link to PR #7891

(End of template — duplicate for each lost account, keep filename pattern: churn-<account>-postmortem-YYYYMMDD)

title: Account health deck variants - standard & enterprise
preview:
WORKING DRAFT - Account health deck templates (Standard + Enterprise)
Owner: Ava Chen (CS)  — collaborators: Marcus, Riley, Samira
Last updated: 2026-02-28
Status: draft — intended to be a reusable deck and playbook for CS teams running QBR-style reviews and renewal readiness checks.

PURPOSE
- Create two lightweight deck variants that scale across mid-market (Standard) and enterprise customers.
- Provide an accompanying renewal readiness tracker and playbook so AEs/CSMs have an operational runbook after the deck presentation.
- Keep slides prescriptive but editable per-account.

HOW TO USE THIS FILE
- Copy the appropriate slide outline into Google Slides and populate with account-specific data prior to the meeting.
- Use the renewal tracker tab (separate sheet linked below) to synchronize actions and owners.
- For Enterprise customers, include security, compliance, and ROI deep-dive slides from the enterprise appendix.

LINKED ARTIFACTS / DATA SOURCES (notes)
- Confluence: /CS/qbr-framework-2.0 (canonical metrics + definitions)
- Jira: CS-412 (QBR deck automation workstream)
- Analytics query: account_monthly_tokens.sql (product analytics team) — ask infra for 7-day rolling latency distribution by route
- Example PR: github.com/redwood/renewal-templates/pull/34

STANDARD DECK OUTLINE (recommended length 25–30 min)
1. Title slide — Account name, CSM, AE, date, renewal month
   - Note: keep single-line value prop for context (what feature usage matters)
2. Agenda + Objectives — what success looks like for this meeting
3. Executive snapshot (1 slide) — health score, renewal status, ARR impact, NPS change
   - Sidebar: highlight 1–2 risks and 1–2 wins
4. Product usage trends (2 slides) — MAU/DAU-like metrics for API calls, per-route token volume, percent of heavy inference jobs
   - Callout: latency and error trends for the primary route (last 90 days)
5. Value delivered (1 slide) — top 3 customer outcomes attributable to Redwood (reduction in infra ops, cost savings vs self-hosted, improved latency percentiles)
6. Adoption map (1 slide) — features/modules used vs. available 
7. Support & incidents (1 slide) — open P1/P2s, MTTR, major incidents since last QBR, actions taken
8. Roadmap alignment (1 slide) — what Redwood is shipping that aligns to customer's top priorities
9. Risks & mitigation (1 slide) — capacity, security, quality regressions, integration fragility
10. Renewal readiness and asks (1 slide) — suggested renewal term, recommended committed capacity (if applicable), open procurement blockers
11. Action plan & owners (1 slide) — 30/60/90 day actions, owners, due dates
12. Appendix pointer — security contacts, billing cadence, technical deep-dive links

SPEAKER NOTES / TALK TRACKS (Standard)
- Exec snapshot: keep it business-focused: quantify downtime avoided, features adopted, and dollarized impact where possible.
- Product usage: call out any anomalous spikes and link to support ticket if relevant.
- Renewal ask: recommend a clear next step (PO timeline or follow-up meeting with procurement).

ENTERPRISE DECK OUTLINE (recommended length 40–60 min + appendix materials)
- Use the Standard deck as base; expand with the following enterprise slides. Aim to send the deck 72 hours before the meeting.
1. Title + Legal/Procurement contact table
2. Agenda + Desired outcomes
3. Executive summary (2 slides) — business impact, retention risk score, recommended commercial motion (renew, expand, restructure)
4. Detailed usage and cost breakdown (3 slides) — token-level cost by product, batch vs streaming usage, caching effectiveness metrics
5. Performance SLA review (2 slides) — 95/99 latency percentiles vs. SLA, uplift since last review
6. Security & compliance posture (2 slides) — KMS/HSM usage status, SOC2/ISO attestation, VPC/private deployment notes, outstanding audit items
7. Integration health (1–2 slides) — model variants in use, model drift signals, eval regression alerts tied to customer prompt sets
8. Architecture snapshot (1 slide) — how the customer’s workloads are routed (hosted / dedicated / private) and fallback policies in place
9. Financial summary (1–2 slides) — committed spend, overages, forecast scenarios (12 months)
10. Renewal proposal (1 slide) — recommended term/committed capacity, discounts, SLAs, migration or upgrade paths
11. Procurement & legal timeline (1 slide) — sign-off steps, POC requirements, required contract addenda
12. Risk register + mitigations (1 slide) — prioritized list with probability/impact and owners
13. Action plan & governance cadence (1 slide) — steering committee, quarterly checkpoints, escalation path
14. Appendix: security pack, benchmark reports, model evaluation artifacts, sample SLO dashboards

ENTERPRISE SPEAKER NOTES / TALK TRACKS
- Financial summary: present three scenarios (status quo, conservative growth, adoption-driven expansion). Use clear ARR math and reference billing audits.
- Security: if customer is in regulated industry, surface the compliance gaps and the remediation timeline. 
- Procurement: have legal/contract playbook excerpts ready — common SLAs and indemnity language.

RENEWAL READINESS TRACKER (single-sheet template columns)
- Account name
- CSM owner
- AE owner
- Renewal date (mm/yyyy)
- Current ARR / MRR
- Recommended motion (renew / expand / restructure / churn risk)
- Proposed committed capacity (if applicable)
- Primary blockers (procurement / legal / technical / exec alignment)
- Risk score (1–10)
- Open action items (comma-separated or link to ticket)
- Owner (action-level owner)
- Due date
- Status (not started / in progress / blocked / done)
- Last touch date
- Notes (free text)

TRACKER USAGE NOTES
- Keep tracker as living single source of truth. Share weekly with AE for accounts within 90 days to renewal.
- Color-code rows: red (>=8 risk), amber (5–7), green (<=4).
- Link each "Open action items" entry to a Jira ticket when possible.

PLAYBOOK: 90-DAY RENEWAL RUNWAY (high-level cadence)
- Day 90–60: Exec alignment + usage review (send deck, schedule meeting), identify blockers, open procurement tickets.
- Day 59–30: Commercial negotiations + legal redlines, technical validation of capacity/SLAs, ramp plan for new commitments.
- Day 29–0: Final sign-off, invoice prep, onboarding for new capacity, post-renewal success plan.

ROLES & ESCALATION
- CSM: owner of health, product adoption, meeting facilitation.
- AE: commercial owner, negotiates price/term.
- Solutions Eng: technical validation, migration plan.
- Escalation path: CSM -> CS Manager -> Head of CS -> CRO.

MEASUREMENT: WHAT TO TRACK
- Health score composition (example): usage (40%), support load (20%), SLA compliance (15%), adoption breadth (15%), NPS/CSAT (10%).
- Renewal success metrics: renewal rate, time-to-sign, negotiation delta (discounts given vs list), churn reasons taxonomy.

TEMPLATES & SNIPPETS (copyable talk tracks)
- Opening exec summary snippet: "Over the last 90 days, your teams used Redwood for X inference hours which correlates to Y% faster response times and an estimated $Z saved vs self-hosted alternatives. Our recommendation is to move to a [12/24]-month committed plan to lock in preferred pricing and reserved capacity."
- Risk acknowledgment: "We see two high-priority items: procurement timeline and latency spikes on route /api/generate. Proposed next steps: procurement sync on [date], solutions eng runbook to validate caching improvements by [date]."

NOTES TO SELF (internal)
- For enterprise deck, prep a one-pager for legal with proposed SLA language to avoid surprises.
- Add an automated script to pull the last 90 days of token usage and 95/99 latencies into the first two slides — ask analytics for access. (Jira: CS-412)
- Consider building a slide library in Slides with placeholders and a one-click populate script for the CSM chrome extension (long-term).

UNRESOLVED QUESTIONS / DECISIONS NEEDED
- Should we always recommend committed capacity for accounts using >X tokens/day? Proposed threshold: 250k tokens/day — need ops input.
- Standardize risk scoring rubric across CS org or leave local calibration? (Ops prefers standardization)

ATTACHMENTS / APPENDIX (to include in final deck)
- Example SLA snapshot (enterprise appendix)
- Recent benchmarking report for model latency improvements (internal link)
- Customer-specific eval results if available (link to evaluation artifacts)

NEXT STEPS
- Review with CS leadership (Ava + Samira) by 2026-03-05 — incorporate feedback.
- Create Google Slides master and a Google Sheet tracker template, link both in Confluence.
- Add automation ticket to CS-automation backlog for pre-populating the executive snapshot.

END OF DRAFT

title: Executive Update (Draft): Pricing + Metering Integrity — Feb 2026
preview:
Audience: Exec staff (CFO/CPO/CRO/Eng), Finance/RevRec leadership, Legal (usage terms)
Owner: Talia Benson (PMO)
Last updated: 2026-02-26

TL;DR (what’s changed since last update)
- We have confirmed multiple meter integrity issues that can (a) create invoice disputes, (b) distort pricing experiment readouts, and (c) create RevRec/audit risk if not governed with change control.
- Engineering has fixes in flight (or merged) for the highest-risk issues (retry double-counting, missing region dimension, embeddings batch accounting). We are running “shadow billing compare” to validate deltas before any changes affect invoices.
- We need exec decisions this week on: (1) whether to issue proactive credits for a small set of impacted customers vs wait for disputes, (2) pricing experiment continuation rules given meter changes mid-flight, and (3) a firm month-end freeze window + late-event policy for RevRec.

1) Why this matters (risk statement)
Redwood’s usage-based model depends on high-integrity telemetry. Small metering errors (double-counting retries, inconsistent token accounting across endpoints, missing dimensions like region, late-arriving events) cascade into:
- Invoice disputes + customer churn risk (especially top accounts and procurement-heavy deals).
- Revenue recognition risk (audit trail gaps; uncertainty in completeness/cutoff).
- Bad pricing decisions (experiments misread due to measurement drift).

We are treating this as a cross-functional incident-style initiative (Finance/Legal/RevOps + Product + Eng), with explicit change control and a bias toward forward fixes (no retroactive invoice changes without Finance/Legal approval).

2) Current state (Feb 26 snapshot)
2.1 Meter integrity issues (confirmed / high confidence)
A) Double-counting in retry/fallback paths (Hosted API; streaming worst case)
- Symptom: Some requests that trigger server-side retry/fallback can be billed as if both attempts were successful, particularly when streaming disconnects or partial chunks occur.
- Impact: Over-billing risk on a subset of traffic; also skews experiment metrics (ARPU/usage) and cost attribution.
- Status: Fix implemented + regression tests added; rollout gated behind shadow billing compare.
- Artifacts: ENG-4430, PR-27463, perf-canary scenarios pending.

B) Missing/incorrect region dimension in usage events
- Symptom: Usage events missing region (or region mis-mapped) causing incorrect price application where pricing varies by region / margin attribution is region-dependent.
- Impact: Pricing correctness + gross margin attribution errors; impacts cohort analysis and sales margin claims.
- Status: Fix merged; backfill plan under review (risk-managed with compare mode).
- Artifacts: PR-27541; Slack eng-platform thread 1770069017.

C) Embeddings metering for batched requests
- Symptom: Batched embeddings calls (multiple inputs per request) were not consistently accounting units, causing under/over billing depending on batch composition.
- Impact: Meter correctness; also affects free tier/overage thresholds for embedding-heavy customers.
- Status: Fix + tests implemented; validating against sampled invoices/exports.
- Artifacts: PR-27569; Slack thread 1770164099.

D) Dedicated GPU-seconds definition ambiguity (v1)
- Symptom: Inconsistent accounting around autoscaling transitions and idle time; customer confusion on “why billed when idle,” and internal inconsistency between dashboards vs ledger aggregation.
- Impact: Disputes for Dedicated; contract language risk; renewal friction.
- Status: GPU-seconds v2 meter under implementation with explicit definition + examples; shadow compare in progress.
- Artifacts: PR-27492; Support ticket SUP-28505 context.

2.2 Data pipeline / operational gaps (systemic)
- Late-arriving events + backfills: we do not have a universally enforced cutoff/freeze window for month-end close. Finance has been handling exceptions manually, increasing audit burden.
- Idempotency/deduplication: improved keys are in progress; currently duplicates can spike during partial failures/backfills.
- “Source of truth” ambiguity: multiple tables used across RevRec, customer exports, and internal dashboards; we are standardizing naming/versioning and publishing a recon export.

3) What we’ve learned (key findings)
3.1 Meter bugs cluster in edge cases, not the mainline path
The majority of drift is concentrated in:
- streaming partials + disconnects
- server-side fallback routing
- client retries that collide with server retries
- batched embeddings
- backfill windows

3.2 Meter changes overlap with pricing experiments → analyses must be segmented
Some Q1 pricing experiments ran during periods where meter fixes were deployed or where missing dimensions were present. If we do not segment “pre-fix” vs “post-fix,” we will over-attribute changes in ARPU/conversion to pricing instead of measurement.
Proposed: all experiment readouts must include a meter-version / “meter integrity” annotation and exclude windows with known drift beyond threshold.

3.3 Customers mostly want predictability + explainability
From Sales/Support, the repeated theme is not just price level; it’s “how do you count usage?” and “can I reconcile this against my logs?” This means our product surface (usage API fields, exports, docs) is part of risk mitigation.

4) Customer impact (what we believe is happening)
- We have confirmed at least a small number of customer complaints tied to retry double-counting and usage export mismatches.
- Finance has a working sample set of invoices vs usage exports (Feb) showing mismatches by account/day/model/region; the top drivers map to the issues above.
- We expect the largest “perception impact” in:
  - high-volume streaming chat workloads
  - customers with aggressive retry logic
  - procurement/security heavy accounts that require audit logs + clear definitions

Open: We are still quantifying exposure ($) and materiality thresholds for proactive remediation.

5) Plan (6–8 week push; where we are)
Workstreams
A) Meter audit + specifications (Finance/RevRec + Eng + Product)
- Deliverable: formal meter specs (Hosted API + Dedicated) and a single “source of truth” dataset map.
- Status: Hosted API meter spec draft in progress; Dedicated spec being revised alongside GPU-seconds v2.

B) Engineering hardening + tests
- “Shadow billing compare” mode: compute old vs new meter results without affecting invoices.
- Add tests for streaming/tool calls/retries; add perf-canary metering invariants.
- Status: compare mode in; streaming retry fix in; embeddings batch fix in; idempotency PRs in progress.

C) Reconciliation playbook + exports
- Deliverable: repeatable recon steps (ledger vs usage events vs customer exports) + stable export table for Finance.
- Status: export pipeline work in progress; recon playbook draft is being updated with cutoff rules.

D) Dashboards, SLOs, and alerting
- Deliverable: console + Grafana metering sanity dashboard; data freshness SLOs; drift alerts with runbooks.
- Status: sanity check job + initial alerts in place; tuning thresholds to reduce noise while catching billing-impacting issues.

E) Pricing experiment analysis + guidance updates
- Deliverable: standard metrics/stop criteria; readouts with caveats; guidance for Sales and contracting.
- Status: analysis plan drafted; awaiting meter-version annotations and clean windows for final readouts.

Near-term timeline (proposed)
- Week of Feb 24 (current): finalize freeze window policy draft; complete first shadow compare results review; decide credit posture for top affected accounts.
- Week of Mar 3: ship recon export; turn on drift alerts to paging threshold after 1 week of tuning; publish initial internal Sales FAQ on billed vs raw tokens.
- Week of Mar 10–17: roll out meter fixes from shadow to production (gated), with Finance sign-off; begin updated experiment readouts.
- End of Mar: exec sign-off on pricing guidance + updated legal usage definitions; operational runbooks “on” for month-end close.

6) Decisions needed (exec asks)
Decision 1 — Customer credits/remediation posture for confirmed over-billing scenarios
Owner(s): Laura (CFO), Emily (RevRec), Michael (Legal), Jordan (CRO)
We need a consistent policy for when we issue proactive credits vs wait for disputes.
Proposal:
- Proactive credits if: (a) confirmed metering bug, (b) customer impact above threshold (to be set by Finance), (c) customer is in renewal/procurement motion or high reputational risk.
- Otherwise: fix forward + offer customer-facing reconciliation/export improvements; credits case-by-case.
Inputs needed:
- Finance to set materiality threshold and accounting handling (contra revenue vs credit memo timing).

Decision 2 — Pricing experiments: continue/stop rules in the presence of meter fixes
Owner(s): Evelyn (CPO), Chris (Product Ops), Rishi (FP&A)
We need alignment that experiments will not be “called” on noisy windows.
Proposal:
- Any experiment readout must be segmented into pre/post meter fix.
- If segmentation reduces effective sample size below power threshold, extend experiment duration or re-run.
- Freeze variant exposure changes while core meter fixes are rolling out (avoid confounding).

Decision 3 — Month-end close usage freeze window + late event/backfill policy
Owner(s): Emily (RevRec), Logan (Eng Telemetry), Mei (Recon datasets)
We need a hard cutoff for RevRec with a documented exception process.
Proposal:
- Establish a “usage freeze” timestamp for month-end (e.g., T+2 business days), after which late events require (a) logged incident/change request, (b) Finance approval, (c) documented delta and whether it rolls forward.
- Publish as part of the month-end close checklist and recon playbook.

Decision 4 — External/customer messaging timeline
Owner(s): Jordan (CRO), Soojin (PMM), Michael (Legal), Nikhil (PM Hosted API)
We need to decide if/when we proactively update customer-facing docs and/or reach out to affected accounts.
Proposal:
- Update docs/FAQs and usage export fields (billed_tokens, cache_hit attribution) first; then targeted outreach for accounts with confirmed discrepancies.

7) Key risks / watchouts
- Shadow compare deltas: some differences are expected (definition clarifications) vs true bugs. We need explicit sign-off criteria before enabling new meters for billing.
- Backfills can create customer confusion if their historical exports change. Preferred approach: no retroactive invoice changes without approval; if exports are updated, provide “as_of” timestamps + versioning.
- Sales enablement must be synchronized with Legal usage language; inconsistent explanations increase procurement friction.

8) Owners + accountability (who is doing what)
- Program management + executive cadence: Talia Benson
- Hosted API meter definitions + attribution: Nadia Rahman / Nikhil Sharma
- Telemetry pipeline hardening + idempotency: Logan Wright / Cole Summers
- Recon datasets + drift checks: Mei Lin (with Finance)
- Data quality SLOs + alerting: Aisha Bello
- Experiment analysis plan + readouts: Chris Osei / Sana Farid
- RevRec policy + month-end close process: Emily Stone
- Margin model updates: Rishi Malhotra
- Contract language + clause library: Michael Grant / Sofia Mendes

9) Appendix: proposed sign-off criteria for enabling meter changes (draft)
Before a meter change can affect invoices:
1) Shadow billing compare runs for >= 7 days (or enough volume) with deltas explained.
2) Drift thresholds met by key dimensions (account, model, region, endpoint): within agreed tolerance.
3) Perf-canary metering invariant scenarios passing (streaming, retries, fallback, tool calls).
4) Finance/RevRec approval recorded (who/when/what changed) + audit trail link.
5) Customer-facing impacts assessed (usage exports, docs) + comms plan if needed.

Notes / open questions (tracked)
- What is the materiality threshold for proactive credits (and for RevRec adjustments) across self-serve vs enterprise?
- Should we expose a “meter_version” field in usage exports to simplify reconciliation during changes?
- For cache-hit attribution: do we bill differently, or only report it? (Impacts perceived fairness and Sales narrative.)

Next exec checkpoint
- Proposed: 30 min in exec staff meeting week of Mar 3
- Pre-reads: updated shadow compare summary, top customer list with recommended remediation posture, and draft freeze window policy for sign-off.

- Artifacts: ENG-4430, PR-27463, perf-canary scenarios pending.

- Symptom: Usage events missing region (or region mis-mapped) causing incorrect price application where pricing varies by region / margin attribution is region-dependent.
- Impact: Pricing correctness + gross margin attribution errors; impacts cohort analysis and sales margin claims.
- Status: Fix merged; backfill plan under review (risk-managed with compare mode).
- Artifacts: PR-27541; Slack eng-platform thread 1770069017.

C) Embeddings metering for batched requests
- Symptom: Batched embeddings calls (multiple inputs per request) were not consistently accounting units, causing under/over billing depending on batch composition.
- Impact: Meter correctness; also affects free tier/overage thresholds for embedding-heavy customers.
- Status: Fix + tests implemented; validating against sampled invoices/exports.
- Artifacts: PR-27569; Slack thread 1770164099.

D) Dedicated GPU-seconds definition ambiguity (v1)
- Symptom: Inconsistent accounting around autoscaling transitions and idle time; customer confusion on “why billed when idle,” and internal inconsistency between dashboards vs ledger aggregation.
- Impact: Disputes for Dedicated; contract language risk; renewal friction.
- Status: GPU-seconds v2 meter under implementation with explicit definition + examples; shadow compare in progress.
- Artifacts: PR-27492; Support ticket SUP-28505 context.

2.2 Data pipeline / operational gaps (systemic)
- Late-arriving events + backfills: we do not have a universally enforced cutoff/freeze window for month-end close. Finance has been handling exceptions manually, increasing audit burden.
- Idempotency/deduplication: improved keys are in progress; currently duplicates can spike during partial failures/backfills.
- “Source of truth” ambiguity: multiple tables used across RevRec, customer exports, and internal dashboards; we are standardizing naming/versioning and publishing a recon export.

3) What we’ve learned (key findings)
3.1 Meter bugs cluster in edge cases, not the mainline path
The majority of drift is concentrated in:
- streaming partials + disconnects
- server-side fallback routing
- client retries that collide with server retries
- batched embeddings
- backfill windows

3.2 Meter changes overlap with pricing experiments → analyses must be segmented
Some Q1 pricing experiments ran during periods where meter fixes were deployed or where missing dimensions were present. If we do not segment “pre-fix” vs “post-fix,” we will over-attribute changes in ARPU/conversion to pricing instead of measurement.
Proposed: all experiment readouts must include a meter-version / “meter integrity” annotation and exclude windows with known drift beyond threshold.

3.3 Customers mostly want predictability + explainability
From Sales/Support, the repeated theme is not just price level; it’s “how do you count usage?” and “can I reconcile this against my logs?” This means our product surface (usage API fields, exports, docs) is part of risk mitigation.

4) Customer impact (what we believe is happening)
- We have confirmed at least a small number of customer complaints tied to retry double-counting and usage export mismatches.
- Finance has a working sample set of invoices vs usage exports (Feb) showing mismatches by account/day/model/region; the top drivers map to the issues above.
- We expect the largest “perception impact” in:
  - high-volume streaming chat workloads
  - customers with aggressive retry logic
  - procurement/security heavy accounts that require audit logs + clear definitions

Open: We are still quantifying exposure ($) and materiality thresholds for proactive remediation.

5) Plan (6–8 week push; where we are)
Workstreams
A) Meter audit + specifications (Finance/RevRec + Eng + Product)
- Deliverable: formal meter specs (Hosted API + Dedicated) and a single “source of truth” dataset map.
- Status: Hosted API meter spec draft in progress; Dedicated spec being revised alongside GPU-seconds v2.

B) Engineering hardening + tests
- “Shadow billing compare” mode: compute old vs new meter results without affecting invoices.
- Add tests for streaming/tool calls/retries; add perf-canary metering invariants.
- Status: compare mode in; streaming retry fix in; embeddings batch fix in; idempotency PRs in progress.

C) Reconciliation playbook + exports
- Deliverable: repeatable recon steps (ledger vs usage events vs customer exports) + stable export table for Finance.
- Status: export pipeline work in progress; recon playbook draft is being updated with cutoff rules.

D) Dashboards, SLOs, and alerting
- Deliverable: console + Grafana metering sanity dashboard; data freshness SLOs; drift alerts with runbooks.
- Status: sanity check job + initial alerts in place; tuning thresholds to reduce noise while catching billing-impacting issues.

E) Pricing experiment analysis + guidance updates
- Deliverable: standard metrics/stop criteria; readouts with caveats; guidance for Sales and contracting.
- Status: analysis plan drafted; awaiting meter-version annotations and clean windows for final readouts.

Near-term timeline (proposed)
- Week of Feb 24 (current): finalize freeze window policy draft; complete first shadow compare results review; decide credit posture for top affected accounts.
- Week of Mar 3: ship recon export; turn on drift alerts to paging threshold after 1 week of tuning; publish initial internal Sales FAQ on billed vs raw tokens.
- Week of Mar 10–17: roll out meter fixes from shadow to production (gated), with Finance sign-off; begin updated experiment readouts.
- End of Mar: exec sign-off on pricing guidance + updated legal usage definitions; operational runbooks “on” for month-end close.

6) Decisions needed (exec asks)
Decision 1 — Customer credits/remediation posture for confirmed over-billing scenarios
Owner(s): Laura (CFO), Emily (RevRec), Michael (Legal), Jordan (CRO)
We need a consistent policy for when we issue proactive credits vs wait for disputes.
Proposal:
- Proactive credits if: (a) confirmed metering bug, (b) customer impact above threshold (to be set by Finance), (c) customer is in renewal/procurement motion or high reputational risk.
- Otherwise: fix forward + offer customer-facing reconciliation/export improvements; credits case-by-case.
Inputs needed:
- Finance to set materiality threshold and accounting handling (contra revenue vs credit memo timing).

Decision 2 — Pricing experiments: continue/stop rules in the presence of meter fixes
Owner(s): Evelyn (CPO), Chris (Product Ops), Rishi (FP&A)
We need alignment that experiments will not be “called” on noisy windows.
Proposal:
- Any experiment readout must be segmented into pre/post meter fix.
- If segmentation reduces effective sample size below power threshold, extend experiment duration or re-run.
- Freeze variant exposure changes while core meter fixes are rolling out (avoid confounding).

Decision 3 — Month-end close usage freeze window + late event/backfill policy
Owner(s): Emily (RevRec), Logan (Eng Telemetry), Mei (Recon datasets)
We need a hard cutoff for RevRec with a documented exception process.
Proposal:
- Establish a “usage freeze” timestamp for month-end (e.g., T+2 business days), after which late events require (a) logged incident/change request, (b) Finance approval, (c) documented delta and whether it rolls forward.
- Publish as part of the month-end close checklist and recon playbook.

Decision 4 — External/customer messaging timeline
Owner(s): Jordan (CRO), Soojin (PMM), Michael (Legal), Nikhil (PM Hosted API)
We need to decide if/when we proactively update customer-facing docs and/or reach out to affected accounts.
Proposal:
- Update docs/FAQs and usage export fields (billed_tokens, cache_hit attribution) first; then targeted outreach for accounts with confirmed discrepancies.

7) Key risks / watchouts
- Shadow compare deltas: some differences are expected (definition clarifications) vs true bugs. We need explicit sign-off criteria before enabling new meters for billing.
- Backfills can create customer confusion if their historical exports change. Preferred approach: no retroactive invoice changes without approval; if exports are updated, provide “as_of” timestamps + versioning.
- Sales enablement must be synchronized with Legal usage language; inconsistent explanations increase procurement friction.

8) Owners + accountability (who is doing what)
- Program management + executive cadence: Talia Benson
- Hosted API meter definitions + attribution: Nadia Rahman / Nikhil Sharma
- Telemetry pipeline hardening + idempotency: Logan Wright / Cole Summers
- Recon datasets + drift checks: Mei Lin (with Finance)
- Data quality SLOs + alerting: Aisha Bello
- Experiment analysis plan + readouts: Chris Osei / Sana Farid
- RevRec policy + month-end close process: Emily Stone
- Margin model updates: Rishi Malhotra
- Contract language + clause library: Michael Grant / Sofia Mendes

9) Appendix: proposed sign-off criteria for enabling meter changes (draft)
Before a meter change can affect invoices:
1) Shadow billing compare runs for >= 7 days (or enough volume) with deltas explained.
2) Drift thresholds met by key dimensions (account, model, region, endpoint): within agreed tolerance.
3) Perf-canary metering invariant scenarios passing (streaming, retries, fallback, tool calls).
4) Finance/RevRec approval recorded (who/when/what changed) + audit trail link.
5) Customer-facing impacts assessed (usage exports, docs) + comms plan if needed.

Notes / open questions (tracked)
- What is the materiality threshold for proactive credits (and for RevRec adjustments) across self-serve vs enterprise?
- Should we expose a “meter_version” field in usage exports to simplify reconciliation during changes?
- For cache-hit attribution: do we bill differently, or only report it? (Impacts perceived fairness and Sales narrative.)

Next exec checkpoint
- Proposed: 30 min in exec staff meeting week of Mar 3
- Pre-reads: updated shadow compare summary, top customer list with recommended remediation posture, and draft freeze window policy for sign-off.

title: vital-signals-integration-plan-q2-bridge-v0-1
preview:
Purpose
---
This doc collects the top-level "vital signals" we want to surface to execs and the company for the upcoming Q2 bridge period, and proposes integrated OKRs + an all-hands agenda. Treat as a working captain's log: half synthesis, half checklist. Not final; aim to ship a 1-page exec brief and an all-hands slide pack from this.

Snapshot: 6-month signal view (high-level)
- Ingestion / usage: hosted API active keys up 22% month-over-month; median requests/day per customer flat. Top 1% of customers account for 48% of tokens.
- Latency & errors: P50 generation latency steady at 190ms; P95 increased from 420ms -> 560ms during peak weekday windows (12:00–15:00 PT) last 3 weeks. Two related incidents: slow KV-cache priming (INC-2026-02A), intermittent model fallbacks (OPS-1428).
- Cost / unit economics: average cost-per-token suppressed by 8% via batching changes, but overall spend increased due to higher traffic from PLG motion; dedicated contract churn risk from three mid-market accounts citing latency spikes.
- Quality/regressions: Eval alerts triggered on prompt-set 'search-rewrite' (regression score -3.1pts) after a quantized variant rollout; revert under consideration.
- Reliability: Dedicated capacity SLA met at 99.95% last 30 days; hosted API regional failovers exercised twice.

High-level narrative for execs
- What to celebrate: steady product-led growth + successful cost wins from Optimize recommendations.
- What keeps us up: latency P95 regressions during midday traffic and an eval regression in quantized model that may affect search customers.
- Immediate ask: prioritize a 2-week reliability sprint (infra + runtime + model rollbacks) to restore P95 targets and hold churn at bay.

Proposed Company OKRs (bridge, 8–12 weeks)
Objective A: Restore predictable latency and reduce customer churn risk
  - KR1: P95 generation latency <= 450ms across all hosted regions (measured over rolling 7-day windows).
  - KR2: Reduce latency incident count impacting >1% of customers from 3 -> 0.
  - KR3: Convert 2 at-risk mid-market customers to dedicated previews + SLA commitments.
Objective B: Protect output quality while preserving cost gains
  - KR1: Re-run failing prompt-set evals across 3 model variants and produce regression root-cause notes within 10 days.
  - KR2: Ship a safe-rollback pathway + canary gating in Console for quantization rollouts (docs + UI) with 95% automated gating on evals.
Objective C: Tighten company-wide signal visibility and response
  - KR1: Build a 1-page 'vital signals' dashboard for execs (token cost, P95, eval regressions, at-risk customers) with 1-minute refresh.
  - KR2: Define and staff a 2-week on-call triage rotation for the sprint (owners + backups).

Draft Exec Update (3–4 minute speaking points)
- Quick win: cost per token down 8% due to Optimize batching; thanks to infra and Optimize teams.
- Concern: P95 latency elevated during peak hours and an eval regression after quantized rollout — immediate sprint to fix and safe-rollout steps.
- Ask: Leadership to approve a 2-week reliability + quality sprint and grant temporary reallocation of 3 FTEs from feature teams (2 infra/runtime, 1 applied-ml).

All-hands: proposed 45-minute format (slim, action-oriented)
- 00:00–05:00: CEO framing — where we are vs. plan (high-level narrative).
- 05:00–12:00: CTO deep-dive — P95 story, incident summary, plan to fix + expected timelines.
- 12:00–22:00: Product — customer signals and at-risk accounts (ask: volunteer outreach owners).
- 22:00–30:00: Ops/demo — new Console gating workflow for rollouts + mini demo of one-page signals dashboard.
- 30:00–40:00: Live Q&A (moderated), capture unresolveds.
- 40:00–45:00: Closing + clear asks for each team + where to sign up for sprint triage rotation.

Comms plan (timeline)
- Day 0 (pre-sprint): Silent prep with leadership; finalize exec brief (1-pager) and all-hands slide (x2 deck owners: ProductOps + EngComms).
- Day 1: Exec brief out to SLT + sprint kickoff email to org (ask: opt-in rota).
- Mid-sprint (day 7): Checkpoint note + short retro on progress; updated 1-pager with interim metrics.
- Post-sprint (day 14–16): All-hands + public postmortem-ish note (what changed, customer impacts, next steps).

Risks & mitigations (rapid)
- Risk: Rolling back quantized model reduces cost benefit -> mitigations: canary traffic split on dedicated customers, offer temporary billing credits to impacted accounts.
- Risk: Reallocating FTEs delays roadmap -> mitigation: timebox reprioritization to 2 weeks; emphasize no permanent headcount moves.
- Risk: Communication noise -> mitigation: single source-of-truth page + triage rotation + regular short updates.

Leadership asks (concrete)
- Approve 2-week reliability & quality sprint budget (temporary reassignments + $0 emergency cloud budget up to $75k).
- Approve outreach plan to 3 at-risk customers (assign AEs and Solutions Eng).
- Fast-track Console gating PR (eng + product sign-off) to enable safe rollouts.

Open questions (to resolve in next 72 hours)
- Do we accept short-term cost increase (credits) to preserve customer trust? (Ops + Finance decision)
- Which 3 FTEs are least disruptive to product velocity if moved? (Leads to coordinate)
- Are there immediate blockers in the quantized rollback path (infra edge cases)? (SRE + runtime)

Next steps & owners (quick)
- Priya: finalize 1-page exec brief + distribute to SLT by EOD Thu (owner).
- Marcus: draft sprint scope + list of infra/runtime tasks, start on-call rota draft (owner).
- Lina: assemble all-hands slide deck skeleton + reach out to QA for demo materials (owner).
- Ethan: outreach plan for at-risk accounts; coordinate with Solutions Eng and AE (owner).
- All: add any missing signals to confluence:COMPANY-STRAT/vital-signals-collection-2026 (collab).

Notes to self / rough TODOs
- Pull precise token-cost numbers from finance (Q2 month-to-date).
- Get incident timelines and L3 notes for the two recent incidents to include in the exec appendix.
- Decide whether to schedule a separate 'customer-facing' town hall if we escalate credits.

Appendix (short data pointers)
- Quick metrics to attach: 30d active keys, top traffic windows histogram, P95 by region, eval-regression delta table (per prompt-set).
- Relevant tickets: OPS-1428 (fallbacks), INC-2026-02A (KV priming), ML-881 (eval regression tracking).

---
Draft ends. Feedback welcome — especially on KR thresholds and the all-hands cadence. This is intentionally actionable: finalize owners + timeline and we can convert to a polished one-pager for the SLT.

title: Account Sentinel: Health Audit & Executive Escalation Playbook
preview:
Purpose:
This working playbook is a single-page, operational guide for rapid assessment and escalation when an account is showing signs of churn risk. Focus is on a repeatable health audit, quantifiable risk scoring, executive escalation criteria, and modular "save plays" the CSM team can mix-and-match.

Status note:
- Drafted by Customer Success Ops (Sophie Martinez).
- Intended to be used for mid-size and enterprise accounts (>$50k ARR).
- This is a working doc: expect TODOs and links to specific tickets.

Quick summary checklist (TL;DR):
- Run health audit (15–30m) -> compute Risk Score.
- If Risk Score >= 65 -> start Tier-1 escalation (CSM + AM + SE).
- If Risk Score >= 85 OR customer verbally signals intent to leave OR legal/billing dispute -> Exec escalation within 48h.
- Apply 1–2 save plays within 72h and track progress in the Escalation Tracker.

1) Health Audit (15–30 min) — data pulls + CSM interview
- Inputs to gather: monthly usage by product feature (last 90d), support ticket volume & severity, NPS/CSAT trends, renewal date + contract terms, recent roadmap asks, billing disputes, active P0/P1 incidents, number of distinct users, recent executive engagement, recent onboarding/adoption activities, expansion pipeline (if any).
- Source queries / where to get each item:
  - Usage: platform-telemetry > customer_usage_report (last 90d)
  - Tickets: Support > filter by customer ID, last 180d
  - NPS: CS-Surveys dashboard
  - Billing: Finance > Billing Disputes queue
  - Recent PRs/bugs impacting customer: GH repo with customer label

2) Risk Score rubric (0–100) — simple weighted sum (CSM runs in worksheet)
- Feature adoption (max 25): 0–25 (<=10% adoption -> 25 points)
- Usage trend (max 20): negative >20% quarter-over-quarter -> 20 points
- Support burden (max 15): >3 P0/P1 in 90d -> 15 points
- Business fit & spend (max 10): single-product low ACV -> 10 points
- Executive engagement (max 10): no exec touch in 9+ months -> 10 points
- Contract & billing risk (max 10): active dispute or recent delayed payments -> 10 points
- Product blockers (max 10): critical unmet roadmap ask or blocker -> 10 points
- Interpretation: 0–34 low risk, 35–64 medium risk, 65–84 high risk, 85–100 crisis/at-risk.

3) Escalation path & responsibilities
- Tier 0 (CSM only): Score < 35 -> CSM remediation plan; track in CRM.
- Tier 1 (CSM + AM + SE): Score 35–64 -> 72h action plan: CSM owns action items; AM sets commercial touchpoint; SE triages technical issues.
- Tier 2 (Full escalation + Exec briefing): Score >= 65 OR legal/billing -> 48h SLAs: assemble Exec Brief, schedule 30m exec sync (VP CS or CRO), propose Save Play(s).
- Tier 3 (Immediate Exec + Legal + Finance): Score >= 85 OR expressed intent to leave OR unresolved P0 for >48h -> emergency war room, daily standups, CEO-level readout if requested.

Role matrix (quick)
- CSM: owner of health audit, communications, playbook execution, ticket tracker.
- AM (Account Manager): commercial concessions, discount approvals, contract renegotiation lead.
- SE (Solutions Engineer): root-cause analysis, hotfix coordination, timeline for delivery.
- Support Ops: triage and priority routing, SLA enforcement.
- Engineering Manager: commit to ship timelines for fixes/features.
- VP CS / CRO: executive sponsor, decisions on concessions and renewal posture.

4) Modularity: Save Plays (pick 1–3, combine as needed)
A. White-Glove Technical Remediation
  - When: product is unstable or blocker exists.
  - Steps: 1) SE-led RCA + temporary mitigation within 48h, 2) Engineering patch in planned sprint or hotfix, 3) Weekly technical status update to customer.
  - Metrics to restore: reduce error rate by 80% or re-enable blocked workflow.

B. Commercial Reset / Concessions
  - When: customer cites price/value mismatch or procurement concerns.
  - Steps: AM proposes limited concession (credits, short-term discount, success milestone credits). Any concession >10% requires VP approval.
  - Goal: buy time + tie to specific outcomes (adoption metric or module usage).

C. Exec Alignment & Roadmap Commitment
  - When: strategic customer asking for roadmap prioritization.
  - Steps: prepare 1-page exec brief, map requested features to product roadmap, propose timeboxed commitment, offer quarterly roadmap sync.

D. Adoption Sprint (2–4 weeks)
  - When: lack of feature adoption.
  - Steps: focused onboarding for power users, sandbox environment, run list of 3 high-impact workflows, success metrics (DAU increase, task completion).

E. Financial Transition (short-term contract restructure)
  - When: procurement/legal barriers.
  - Steps: legal and finance looped; propose amendment with clear exit criteria and review dates.

5) Escalation tracker fields (minimum)
- customer_name, account_id, renewal_date, ARR, risk_score, primary_contact, escalation_tier, summary_of_issue, actions (list), owner(s), due_dates, status, linked_tickets, exec_readout_date.
- Keep this as a single-row Google Sheet template we populate per account.

6) QBR addendum template (to be used when a QBR coincides with an active escalation)
- 15–20m executive summary (health score, ask)
- 20–30m technical status (SE deep dive)
- 10m commercial options and proposed concession
- Decisions & next steps (owner + due date)

7) Communication templates (placeholders)
- Slack (to internal execs):
  "Heads-up: Account {{ACCT}} ({{ARR}}) — Risk Score {{SCORE}} = {{tier}}. Key issue: {{short summary}}. CSM: {{name}}. Recommend: Tier {{tier}} escalation, exec brief within 48h. See tracker: {{sheet link}}"

- Customer email (to CFO/Head of Prod):
  Subject: Proposal to address {{short issue}} — next 30 days
  Body: Short summary, proposed save plays + timeline, ask for a 30m exec sync, clear acceptance criteria, contact info.

- Exec readout one-pager (single page):
  - Top line: account, ARR, renewal, risk score, ask
  - Facts: usage delta, tickets, product blockers, exec engagement
  - Options: 2–3 concrete options + cost (commercial or engineering effort)
  - Recommendation & next steps

8) Timeboxes & SLAs (operational)
- Health audit: complete within 24h after risk signal identified.
- Tier 1 response plan: initial outreach within 48h; remediation plan within 72h.
- Escalation to execs: brief prepared within 24–48h of Tier 2 trigger; exec sync within 48h.
- Resolution commitments: capture commit dates for patches/features; update weekly until closed.

9) Evidence & measurement (how we know it worked)
- Primary leading indicators: 30-day DAU/MAU change, activation of critical workflow, reduction in P0/P1 incidents, CSAT change.
- Secondary: renewal signed or explicit renewal deferral, expanded scope, reduced open bugs.

10) Known gaps / open questions (TODOs)
- Add automated query to compute risk score in BI (TODO: JIRA CS-4900).
- Define approval matrix for concessions >10% (owner: AM leads).
- Build single-row Escalation Tracker template and link to this doc (TODO: support ops).

Appendix: sample escalation tracker link (placeholder): https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/placeholder

Notes / provenance:
- Inspired by past 'save' plays used with three customers in 2024 Q3.
- Cross-check against legal for any template concession language before sending.
- Keep all customer-sensitive notes in CRM (do not post in public channels).

End of doc.
text:
Purpose:
This working playbook is a single-page, operational guide for rapid assessment and escalation when an account is showing signs of churn risk. Focus is on a repeatable health audit, quantifiable risk scoring, executive escalation criteria, and modular "save plays" the CSM team can mix-and-match.

Status note:
- Drafted by Customer Success Ops (Sophie Martinez).
- Intended to be used for mid-size and enterprise accounts (>$50k ARR).
- This is a working doc: expect TODOs and links to specific tickets.

Quick summary checklist (TL;DR):
- Run health audit (15–30m) -> compute Risk Score.
- If Risk Score >= 65 -> start Tier-1 escalation (CSM + AM + SE).
- If Risk Score >= 85 OR customer verbally signals intent to leave OR legal/billing dispute -> Exec escalation within 48h.
- Apply 1–2 save plays within 72h and track progress in the Escalation Tracker.

1) Health Audit (15–30 min) — data pulls + CSM interview
- Inputs to gather: monthly usage by product feature (last 90d), support ticket volume & severity, NPS/CSAT trends, renewal date + contract terms, recent roadmap asks, billing disputes, active P0/P1 incidents, number of distinct users, recent executive engagement, recent onboarding/adoption activities, expansion pipeline (if any).
- Source queries / where to get each item:
  - Usage: platform-telemetry > customer_usage_report (last 90d)
  - Tickets: Support > filter by customer ID, last 180d
  - NPS: CS-Surveys dashboard
  - Billing: Finance > Billing Disputes queue
  - Recent PRs/bugs impacting customer: GH repo with customer label

2) Risk Score rubric (0–100) — simple weighted sum (CSM runs in worksheet)
- Feature adoption (max 25): 0–25 (<=10% adoption -> 25 points)
- Usage trend (max 20): negative >20% quarter-over-quarter -> 20 points
- Support burden (max 15): >3 P0/P1 in 90d -> 15 points
- Business fit & spend (max 10): single-product low ACV -> 10 points
- Executive engagement (max 10): no exec touch in 9+ months -> 10 points
- Contract & billing risk (max 10): active dispute or recent delayed payments -> 10 points
- Product blockers (max 10): critical unmet roadmap ask or blocker -> 10 points
- Interpretation: 0–34 low risk, 35–64 medium risk, 65–84 high risk, 85–100 crisis/at-risk.

3) Escalation path & responsibilities
- Tier 0 (CSM only): Score < 35 -> CSM remediation plan; track in CRM.
- Tier 1 (CSM + AM + SE): Score 35–64 -> 72h action plan: CSM owns action items; AM sets commercial touchpoint; SE triages technical issues.
- Tier 2 (Full escalation + Exec briefing): Score >= 65 OR legal/billing -> 48h SLAs: assemble Exec Brief, schedule 30m exec sync (VP CS or CRO), propose Save Play(s).
- Tier 3 (Immediate Exec + Legal + Finance): Score >= 85 OR expressed intent to leave OR unresolved P0 for >48h -> emergency war room, daily standups, CEO-level readout if requested.

Role matrix (quick)
- CSM: owner of health audit, communications, playbook execution, ticket tracker.
- AM (Account Manager): commercial concessions, discount approvals, contract renegotiation lead.
- SE (Solutions Engineer): root-cause analysis, hotfix coordination, timeline for delivery.
- Support Ops: triage and priority routing, SLA enforcement.
- Engineering Manager: commit to ship timelines for fixes/features.
- VP CS / CRO: executive sponsor, decisions on concessions and renewal posture.

4) Modularity: Save Plays (pick 1–3, combine as needed)
A. White-Glove Technical Remediation
  - When: product is unstable or blocker exists.
  - Steps: 1) SE-led RCA + temporary mitigation within 48h, 2) Engineering patch in planned sprint or hotfix, 3) Weekly technical status update to customer.
  - Metrics to restore: reduce error rate by 80% or re-enable blocked workflow.

B. Commercial Reset / Concessions
  - When: customer cites price/value mismatch or procurement concerns.
  - Steps: AM proposes limited concession (credits, short-term discount, success milestone credits). Any concession >10% requires VP approval.
  - Goal: buy time + tie to specific outcomes (adoption metric or module usage).

C. Exec Alignment & Roadmap Commitment
  - When: strategic customer asking for roadmap prioritization.
  - Steps: prepare 1-page exec brief, map requested features to product roadmap, propose timeboxed commitment, offer quarterly roadmap sync.

D. Adoption Sprint (2–4 weeks)
  - When: lack of feature adoption.
  - Steps: focused onboarding for power users, sandbox environment, run list of 3 high-impact workflows, success metrics (DAU increase, task completion).

E. Financial Transition (short-term contract restructure)
  - When: procurement/legal barriers.
  - Steps: legal and finance looped; propose amendment with clear exit criteria and review dates.

5) Escalation tracker fields (minimum)
- customer_name, account_id, renewal_date, ARR, risk_score, primary_contact, escalation_tier, summary_of_issue, actions (list), owner(s), due_dates, status, linked_tickets, exec_readout_date.
- Keep this as a single-row Google Sheet template we populate per account.

6) QBR addendum template (to be used when a QBR coincides with an active escalation)
- 15–20m executive summary (health score, ask)
- 20–30m technical status (SE deep dive)
- 10m commercial options and proposed concession
- Decisions & next steps (owner + due date)

7) Communication templates (placeholders)
- Slack (to internal execs):
  "Heads-up: Account {{ACCT}} ({{ARR}}) — Risk Score {{SCORE}} = {{tier}}. Key issue: {{short summary}}. CSM: {{name}}. Recommend: Tier {{tier}} escalation, exec brief within 48h. See tracker: {{sheet link}}"

- Customer email (to CFO/Head of Prod):
  Subject: Proposal to address {{short issue}} — next 30 days
  Body: Short summary, proposed save plays + timeline, ask for a 30m exec sync, clear acceptance criteria, contact info.

- Exec readout one-pager (single page):
  - Top line: account, ARR, renewal, risk score, ask
  - Facts: usage delta, tickets, product blockers, exec engagement
  - Options: 2–3 concrete options + cost (commercial or engineering effort)
  - Recommendation & next steps

8) Timeboxes & SLAs (operational)
- Health audit: complete within 24h after risk signal identified.
- Tier 1 response plan: initial outreach within 48h; remediation plan within 72h.
- Escalation to execs: brief prepared within 24–48h of Tier 2 trigger; exec sync within 48h.
- Resolution commitments: capture commit dates for patches/features; update weekly until closed.

9) Evidence & measurement (how we know it worked)
- Primary leading indicators: 30-day DAU/MAU change, activation of critical workflow, reduction in P0/P1 incidents, CSAT change.
- Secondary: renewal signed or explicit renewal deferral, expanded scope, reduced open bugs.

10) Known gaps / open questions (TODOs)
- Add automated query to compute risk score in BI (TODO: JIRA CS-4900).
- Define approval matrix for concessions >10% (owner: AM leads).
- Build single-row Escalation Tracker template and link to this doc (TODO: support ops).

Appendix: sample escalation tracker link (placeholder): https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/placeholder

Notes / provenance:
- Inspired by past 'save' plays used with three customers in 2024 Q3.
- Cross-check against legal for any template concession language before sending.
- Keep all customer-sensitive notes in CRM (do not post in public channels).

End of doc.

title: All-hands format experimentation proposal — Q3 pilot
preview:
Purpose:

Quick proposal for running a small-scale experiment on all-hands format and cadence for Q3. This doc bundles: an OKR-focused agenda draft, proposed exec update script, facilitator checklist, measurement plan, and a lightweight rollback/fallback plan. Goal: reduce meeting time by 25% while increasing perceived alignment score in the 30/60 day pulse.

Context and hypothesis:
- Context: Feedback from the last two town halls shows noise around length, heavy exec monologue, and limited time for cross-team Q&A. Engagement metrics: 38% watch-through in recording, 46% survey response stating "too long".
- Hypothesis: Shorter, modular all-hands (45 minutes) with pre-shared micro-briefs + live highlight panel improves cross-team clarity and enables deeper follow-ups via office-hours.

Proposed pilot structure (1 quarter) — 3 all-hands total (months 1, 2, 3):
- Format: 45 minutes total, structured pace:
  - 0–5m: Welcome + one-sentence company highlight (CEO)
  - 5–15m: Two 5-minute OKR health flashes (hosted by product-ops) covering top 6 company OKRs (each flash is 60–90s per OKR)
  - 15–30m: Customer impact stories (1 short customer video + 6-minute customer-success highlight + 4-minute solutions-engineering rapid demo)
  - 30–40m: Exec decisions & risks (each exec has a 90s slot to call out decisions, blockers, or ask teams for help)
  - 40–45m: Live curated Q&A (questions collected ahead; 3 chosen by facilitator)
- Deliverables pre-meeting: 1-page micro-brief to be distributed 48 hours ahead (OKR snapshot, top asks, links for deep dives)
- Post-meeting: 1) Recording trimmed to highlights (<=10m highlight clip). 2) Follow-up office-hours calendar with slots for deep dives.

Draft OKR narrative (for the OKR health flashes):
- Company Objective A: Make inference predictable and economical for customers.
  - Key Result 1: Reduce hosted per-token median latency by 15% for 95th percentile requests — current baseline 220ms.
  - Key Result 2: 10% unit-cost reduction via batching & cache improvements on production workloads.
- Company Objective B: Enterprise expansion — Dedicated & Private traction.
  - Key Result 1: 6 new Dedicated contracts signed (>$100k ARR)
  - Key Result 2: Ship Private deployment readiness checklist and 2 customer pilots.
- Company Objective C: Reliability & SLAs
  - Key Result 1: Achieve 99.95% uptime on Dedicated clusters across two regions.
  - Key Result 2: Release rollback-safe routing policies for model variants.

Exec update script (short bullets for each exec — each gets 90s):
- Engineering lead (runtime): "We shipped kernel selector v2; early signal: 9% p50 latency improvement on small models; watch for memory pressure on long-context workloads. Need SRE support for curated canary next week (PLAT-1428)."
- Product lead (hosted): "Launched model catalog additions and streaming improvements; usage uptick on embeddings. Ask: Product marketing to publish quickstart w/ new SDK samples by month end."
- Sales/CS lead: "1 strategic win in EMEA; pipeline healthy but procurement cycles longer; need legal to prioritize two-term NDA customizations for Private pilots."
- Security/Compliance: "KMS integration is in validation; no blockers but ask infra for testing window and audit log retention decisions."

Facilitator checklist (pre, during, post):
- Pre (5 days out): Confirm micro-brief content, record CEO 1-sentence highlight, collect exec 90s scripts, gather 2 customer assets.
- Day before: Publish micro-brief; QA recording/audio; choose curated questions; confirm office-hours slots.
- During: Keep clock, mute unused feeds, run the 45m timer, nudge execs at 60s, collect unanswered Qs as follow-ups.
- Post: Trim highlight clip, update Confluence summary, assign follow-up owners for each open ask, schedule office-hours.

Measurement plan (how we’ll judge success):
- Quantitative: watch-through rate of highlight clip (target +15% over baseline), survey alignment score (NPS-like question about clarity, target +10 points), mean meeting length (target 45m), number of follow-up deep-dive signups (target 20% of attendees).
- Qualitative: selected open-text signals in pulse survey; feedback from 2 pilot SRE/product teams.

Risk and mitigation:
- Risk: Important material doesn't fit 90s slots -> Mitigation: require pre-shared micro-briefs and link to office-hours.
- Risk: Execs feel constrained -> Mitigation: run 1 rehearsal and share rationale + time-savings impacts; optional "exec breakout" 15m before meeting.
- Risk: Lower live engagement because of shorter live Q&A -> Mitigation: promote office-hours and collect questions in advance via Slack thread; rotate live Q&A selection.

Communication and rollout timeline:
- Week 0 (alignment): Execs sign off on pilot plan, product-ops creates micro-brief template.
- Week 1: Confirm customers and assets; schedule pilot dates and office-hours.
- Week 2: Distribute first micro-brief; run rehearsal with execs.
- Week 3: Pilot #1. Immediately collect pulse feedback; debrief within 48 hrs.
- Months 2–3: Iterate on structure and communications per measurement signals.

Open questions / decision points for execs:
- Are we comfortable shifting to 45 minutes for the pilot quarter? (Yes/No)
- Who is the canonical owner for trimming highlight clips and publishing them? (suggestion: product-ops)
- Approval for legal to expedite two NDA variants for Private pilot customers? (Yes/No)

Appendix: sample micro-brief template (to be copy/pasted into Confluence):
- Subject: All-hands micro-brief — [Date]
- TL;DR in one line: [CEO one-sentence highlight]
- Top OKR bullets (3 lines, include metric and trend)
- Decisions/asks for teams (3 items max)
- Links: Confluence OKR page, JIRA top blockers, Office-hours calendar

Notes to self / working items (raw):
- Need to sync with comms on clip-hosting and newsletter feature.
- Follow-up: confirm recording trimming SLA with engineering media infra.
- Make a short internal survey (5 Qs) for pilot metrics collection; ask insights team for sampling plan.

If this seems reasonable, ask execs to reply with answers to the three decision points above by COB Friday.

-- Maya

(Working draft: expect edits. This doc intentionally light on formal tone — will move to Confluence canonical doc after exec sign-off.)
