title: Clarity Week — Decision Grid and All-Staff Orientation
preview:
Purpose:
This doc captures the working decisions, asks, and run-of-show for the upcoming Clarity Week (company-wide alignment and planning). Primary goal: converge on the set of cross-company commitments for the next 90 days and produce a single, actionable OKR slate for exec review + an all-staff orientation that surfaces risks and handoffs.

Audience:
- Company execs (founders, VC partners optional)
- Org leads (ENG, PRODUCT, SRE, SALES, CS)
- People ops / comms

Desired outcomes by EOD Friday:
1) Finalized top-3 company OKRs with metrics and owners.
2) Run-of-show for all-staff orientation + 1-pager for ICs.
3) Decision grid resolved for 6 cross-org tradeoffs (staffing, infra spend, launch scope).
4) List of unblock actions (owner + ETA) to feed into next sprint planning.

Context / constraints (working notes):
- Q2 budget flexibility: modest runway for Dedicated capacity pilots, but hiring must be prioritized vs critical infra spend.
- Customer commitments: two enterprise pilots in May require Private deployment support + SOC2 artifacts.
- Product roadmap: Hosted API feature parity for streaming/tool-calling expected by mid-Q2; Optimize toolkit MVP slated for beta.

Draft company-level OKRs (working — numbers are baseline + targets):
OKR 1 (Reliability & SLA):
- Objective: Deliver consistent latency & uptime for production inference customers.
- KR1: 95th percentile latency (hosted text generation, 1k-token distribution) <= 120ms (baseline 160ms).
- KR2: Platform uptime >= 99.9% for hosted API (baseline 99.7%).
- KR3: Onboard 2 enterprise customers onto Dedicated (baseline 0).
Owner: Riley Chen (eng-sre)

OKR 2 (Unit Economics):
- Objective: Reduce per-token inference cost for typical PLG workloads.
- KR1: Reduce per-token cost by 20% on high-volume routes via batching/caching (baseline $0.0009/token).
- KR2: Deploy two optimized quantization profiles covering 70% of tail traffic.
Owner: Sofia Martinez (apply-ml / optimize)

OKR 3 (Developer Growth / Product):
- Objective: Make Redwood the easiest inference platform for engineering teams to adopt.
- KR1: Improve developer onboarding NPS from 6.0 -> 7.8 (survey baseline 6.0).
- KR2: Reduce median time-to-first-successful-call (new orgs) from 45min -> 20min.
Owner: Miguel Torres (devexp)

Notes: these OKRs are intentionally compact — we may move a couple of KRs under product/dedicated teams depending on readiness. Need CFO signoff on cost-reduction targets.

Decision grid (top-of-mind cross-company tradeoffs — matrix summarized as bullets):
1) Dedicated capacity expansion vs. hiring ML infra engineers
- Decision criteria: customer commitments, time to revenue, one-time infra cost vs OPEX.
- Recommendation: postpone large Dedicated expansion; prioritize two-month contract to satisfy pilot customers + hire 1 infra engineer (contract-to-hire) to stabilize rollout.
- Open question: CFO threshold for commit > $Xk requires exec review (see JIRA RDW-987).

2) Aggressive quantization rollout vs. quality guardrails
- Tradeoff: cost savings vs. potential quality regression for prompt-heavy customers.
- Recommendation: phased rollout with canary on 10% traffic + automatic rollback triggers tied to eval prompt set.

3) All-staff orientation length (90m deep-dive vs 60m high-level)
- Recommendation: 60m company-wide, follow-up discipline-specific deep dives (30m) next week.

4) Feature freeze window during platform migration
- Recommendation: soft freeze on non-critical product launches for two weeks around migration; emergency exceptions allowed via triage ticket.

Run-of-show — All-staff orientation (60 minutes):
- 00:00–05:00: Welcome + housekeeping (CEO)
- 05:00–20:00: Company priorities & top-3 OKRs (Exec sponsor for each gives 3 slides + 2 min)
- 20:00–35:00: Reliability & incident-sharing highlight (SRE: short postmortem + wins)
- 35:00–45:00: Product momentum demo: hosted API streaming + Optimize quick wins
- 45:00–55:00: Commitments & what success looks like (Owners call out first two weeks)
- 55:00–60:00: Live Q&A (moderated, burning questions only)

Prep notes for presenters:
- Slides: max 3 slides per Exec sponsor. Single slide: what we commit, why it matters, how will we measure.
- Submit slides to /confluence/clarity-week-decks by Tue 03/03 EOD. (CONFLUENCE-2345)
- Speakers: mic check 10 min before (Zoom host: Jordan Blake).

Oriented one-pager for ICs (to publish post-allstaff):
- What we decided (top-3 OKRs)
- Immediate asks (list of 1–3 per team)
- How to escalate blockers (triage steps + Slack channel)
- Where to find the artifacts (links)

Roles + owners (quick):
- Program lead / convener: Asha Patel (company-ops) — overall ship timeline, decision synth.
- Run-of-show host / comms: Jordan Blake — logistics, slide collation.
- OKR steward: Miguel Torres — collate KR metrics and dashboards.
- SRE / Reliability owner: Riley Chen — latency / uptime KR runs.
- Evals / quality owner: Sofia Martinez — define prompt sets and regression gates.

Timeline (working):
- Mon 03/01: Leadership finalize draft OKRs (this doc -> exec slack review).
- Tue 03/02: Team leads submit slide drafts.
- Wed 03/03: Dry run with execs (30m) + final slide upload.
- Thu 03/04: All-staff orientation (live).
- Fri 03/05: Publish IC one-pager + OKR tracker in Confluence.

Risks & mitigations (brief):
- Risk: Missing telemetry for KR measurement (some routes not instrumented). Mitigation: Miguel to prioritize event+metric backlog — aim to instrument top 6 routes by Wed.
- Risk: Quality regressions from quantization. Mitigation: Sofia to own canary prompt set and run daily gating tests.
- Risk: Enterprise pilot delays (SOC2 docs). Mitigation: Legal + Solutions Eng to run sweep and provide prioritized checklist; escalate to COO if > 1 week delay.

Open questions (to resolve in exec sync):
- Q1: CFO approval threshold for Dedicated expansion spend — what exact number triggers formal approval? (Action: Asha to get clear by Tue).
- Q2: Are we comfortable with a public-facing developer NPS survey in early Q2 or should it stay internal for another cycle? (Action: Miguel to propose sample cadence).
- Q3: Does the sales team commit to two enterprise pilots landing this quarter? (Action: Sales lead to confirm by Wed).

Unresolved ops items / notes to self:
- Create short feedback form for orientation (post-event) — keep it 3 questions.
- Build visibility dashboard for KR progress (simple table in Confluence).
- Re-check timezone-friendly timing for APAC participants; consider recording.

Next steps (owner + ETA):
- Asha: finalize decision grid + escalate CFO spend threshold (Tue 03/02).
- Miguel: instrument top routes + draft developer NPS cadence (Wed 03/03).
- Riley: run p99 latency deep-dive and draft mitigation steps (Thu 03/04).
- Sofia: prepare canary eval prompt sets and rollout checklist (Wed 03/03).
- Jordan: collect slides, set up Zoom + recording + Q&A moderation (Wed 03/03).

Appendix: links & quick references
- Confluence deck stub: CONFLUENCE-2345
- Triage ticket: JIRA-RDW-987
- PR for dashboard: https://github.com/redwood-inference/platform/pull/412
- Slack for event coordination: #company-ops (seed reminders 1h / 10m)

Notes about tone and distribution:
- Keep orientation tight and optimistic — highlight measurable wins, be explicit about tradeoffs.
- Avoid overpromising timelines (we will call out contingencies).

Raw brainstorming fragments (keepsake of working doc):
- \"What if\" mini-sessions for teams who want to propose new bets — capture in 2-pager and route to exec review.
- Consider one-off office hours for migrate-to-dedicated questions post orientation.
- Would like a single shared spreadsheet that lists KR owners and status; avoid duplicated trackers.

-- end --

1) Dedicated capacity expansion vs. hiring ML infra engineers
- Decision criteria: customer commitments, time to revenue, one-time infra cost vs OPEX.
- Recommendation: postpone large Dedicated expansion; prioritize two-month contract to satisfy pilot customers + hire 1 infra engineer (contract-to-hire) to stabilize rollout.
- Open question: CFO threshold for commit > $Xk requires exec review (see JIRA RDW-987).

2) Aggressive quantization rollout vs. quality guardrails
- Tradeoff: cost savings vs. potential quality regression for prompt-heavy customers.
- Recommendation: phased rollout with canary on 10% traffic + automatic rollback triggers tied to eval prompt set.

3) All-staff orientation length (90m deep-dive vs 60m high-level)
- Recommendation: 60m company-wide, follow-up discipline-specific deep dives (30m) next week.

4) Feature freeze window during platform migration
- Recommendation: soft freeze on non-critical product launches for two weeks around migration; emergency exceptions allowed via triage ticket.

Run-of-show — All-staff orientation (60 minutes):
- 00:00–05:00: Welcome + housekeeping (CEO)
- 05:00–20:00: Company priorities & top-3 OKRs (Exec sponsor for each gives 3 slides + 2 min)
- 20:00–35:00: Reliability & incident-sharing highlight (SRE: short postmortem + wins)
- 35:00–45:00: Product momentum demo: hosted API streaming + Optimize quick wins
- 45:00–55:00: Commitments & what success looks like (Owners call out first two weeks)
- 55:00–60:00: Live Q&A (moderated, burning questions only)

Prep notes for presenters:
- Slides: max 3 slides per Exec sponsor. Single slide: what we commit, why it matters, how will we measure.
- Submit slides to /confluence/clarity-week-decks by Tue 03/03 EOD. (CONFLUENCE-2345)
- Speakers: mic check 10 min before (Zoom host: Jordan Blake).

Oriented one-pager for ICs (to publish post-allstaff):
- What we decided (top-3 OKRs)
- Immediate asks (list of 1–3 per team)
- How to escalate blockers (triage steps + Slack channel)
- Where to find the artifacts (links)

Roles + owners (quick):
- Program lead / convener: Asha Patel (company-ops) — overall ship timeline, decision synth.
- Run-of-show host / comms: Jordan Blake — logistics, slide collation.
- OKR steward: Miguel Torres — collate KR metrics and dashboards.
- SRE / Reliability owner: Riley Chen — latency / uptime KR runs.
- Evals / quality owner: Sofia Martinez — define prompt sets and regression gates.

Timeline (working):
- Mon 03/01: Leadership finalize draft OKRs (this doc -> exec slack review).
- Tue 03/02: Team leads submit slide drafts.
- Wed 03/03: Dry run with execs (30m) + final slide upload.
- Thu 03/04: All-staff orientation (live).
- Fri 03/05: Publish IC one-pager + OKR tracker in Confluence.

Risks & mitigations (brief):
- Risk: Missing telemetry for KR measurement (some routes not instrumented). Mitigation: Miguel to prioritize event+metric backlog — aim to instrument top 6 routes by Wed.
- Risk: Quality regressions from quantization. Mitigation: Sofia to own canary prompt set and run daily gating tests.
- Risk: Enterprise pilot delays (SOC2 docs). Mitigation: Legal + Solutions Eng to run sweep and provide prioritized checklist; escalate to COO if > 1 week delay.

Open questions (to resolve in exec sync):
- Q1: CFO approval threshold for Dedicated expansion spend — what exact number triggers formal approval? (Action: Asha to get clear by Tue).
- Q2: Are we comfortable with a public-facing developer NPS survey in early Q2 or should it stay internal for another cycle? (Action: Miguel to propose sample cadence).
- Q3: Does the sales team commit to two enterprise pilots landing this quarter? (Action: Sales lead to confirm by Wed).

Unresolved ops items / notes to self:
- Create short feedback form for orientation (post-event) — keep it 3 questions.
- Build visibility dashboard for KR progress (simple table in Confluence).
- Re-check timezone-friendly timing for APAC participants; consider recording.

Next steps (owner + ETA):
- Asha: finalize decision grid + escalate CFO spend threshold (Tue 03/02).
- Miguel: instrument top routes + draft developer NPS cadence (Wed 03/03).
- Riley: run p99 latency deep-dive and draft mitigation steps (Thu 03/04).
- Sofia: prepare canary eval prompt sets and rollout checklist (Wed 03/03).
- Jordan: collect slides, set up Zoom + recording + Q&A moderation (Wed 03/03).

Appendix: links & quick references
- Confluence deck stub: CONFLUENCE-2345
- Triage ticket: JIRA-RDW-987
- PR for dashboard: https://github.com/redwood-inference/platform/pull/412
- Slack for event coordination: #company-ops (seed reminders 1h / 10m)

Notes about tone and distribution:
- Keep orientation tight and optimistic — highlight measurable wins, be explicit about tradeoffs.
- Avoid overpromising timelines (we will call out contingencies).

Raw brainstorming fragments (keepsake of working doc):
- \"What if\" mini-sessions for teams who want to propose new bets — capture in 2-pager and route to exec review.
- Consider one-off office hours for migrate-to-dedicated questions post orientation.
- Would like a single shared spreadsheet that lists KR owners and status; avoid duplicated trackers.

-- end --

title: Alignment Loom: 90-day Synoptic & Town Ticker
preview:
Purpose:
This doc is a working scratchpad for the next 90-day alignment cycle and the associated all-hands (Town Ticker). Aim: surface a concise set of company-level objectives, the minimal staffing shifts to enable them, and a repeatable town run-of-show that helps execs and ICs stay synchronized.

Context and constraints (brief):
- Q1 shipped large infra changes (autoscaling, batching improvements) -> short-term latency gains but some flakiness in edge cases.
- We have a hiring cadence mismatch: more infra/headcount growth in infra than product/solutions.
- Customer signals: several mid-market prospects expect Dedicated SLA/latency numbers by end of next quarter.
- Budget guardrails: conservative hiring for org beyond headcount freeze lift timeline on April 1.

High-level 90-day focus (narrative):
- Stabilize the recent infra roll and convert it into predictable SLOs we can advertise.
- Move cost/perf levers from experimental to suggested defaults in Redwood Optimize.
- Prepare Dedicated capacity & Private deployment playbook for 3 target enterprise pilots.

Draft company-level OKRs (working):
O1: Make inference performance predictable for customers
KR1.1: 95th percentile latency for hosted text-generation under standard load <= 150ms average across regions
KR1.2: Release postmortem + mitigation for last three infra flakiness incidents; reduce repeat failure rate by 80%
KR1.3: Public-facing performance page with per-model SLOs and historical trend (beta) by end of month 2

O2: Increase unit economics transparency & defaults
KR2.1: Ship Optimize default quantization profile recommendations for the top 5 workload archetypes
KR2.2: Reduce per-token cost (median) for existing beta customers by 15% via batching/caching tuning suggestions and one automated policy
KR2.3: Add token-level cost breakdown to Console billing flow (experimental widget)

O3: Convert three enterprise trials to Dedicated or Private pilots
KR3.1: Pilot readiness checklist completed and validated with Solutions Eng for 3 named accounts
KR3.2: Enterprise onboarding time reduced from signed contract to pilot runbook execution by 30%
KR3.3: Security/compliance gap list for Private deployment closed for 2 pilot customers

Staffing & resource shifts (minimal viable moves):
- Short term reallocation: move 0.5 FTE from internal infra tooling to pilot integration support for 8 weeks (contact: Diego).
- Apply a temporary focus window for Solutions Eng: 2 engineers (Ava + contractor) to ship the dedicated pilot runbook artifacts by week 6.
- Cross-train 3 SREs on Dedicated capacity night-run procedures to reduce reliance on a single on-call SME.
- Keep hiring freeze for new non-customer-facing roles until budget review; exceptions for security/compliance hires with exec approval.

All-hands (Town Ticker) plan — concise run-of-show (30–40m):
- 0–5m: Framing note (CEO) — why this 90-day cycle matters and one metric to watch.
- 5–12m: Ops status (SRE lead) — stability wins, open incidents, and mitigations. Slide: three charts (p95 latency, incident rate, rollouts cadence).
- 12–20m: Product priorities (Product lead) — top 3 product commits and customer-facing expectations.
- 20–28m: Sales/customer signals (GTM rep) — funnel highlights for Dedicated/Private interest + ask for support.
- 28–34m: Quick wins & shoutouts (rapid fire): teams that moved the needle.
- 34–40m: Focus ops & concrete asks (what to do this sprint) + Q&A (typed, filtered).
Notes to facilitator: keep slides to one visual + 1–2 bullets each; require slide deck submissions 48h prior; limit exec speak time to 3 mins each.

Communications cadence (recommended):
- Week 0 (launch): short email from CEO with the one metric and 3 asks (share, align, volunteer).
- Weekly: Town Ticker newsletter (one page) with wins/risks/action owners; distributed Mondays.
- Biweekly: Leadership sync (30m) to surface cross-team blockers; use a rotating gatekeeper to collect signals via Slack thread #90-day-loom.
- Mid-cycle pulse: 10-question async survey (owner: People Ops) to gauge overload & clarity.

Decision points (executive/OWG):
- Approve the 0.5 FTE reallocation to pilot support for 8 weeks (deadline: Feb 22).
- Approve exceptions to hiring freeze for 2 security roles (deadline: Feb 28).
- Decide on public performance page beta scope (internal decision owner: Product + Eng infra) by March 10.

Risks and mitigations (quick list):
- Risk: reallocation causes delay in non-critical infra work -> Mitigation: freeze non-critical infra changes for 8 weeks, maintain hotfix budget.
- Risk: pilot customers need more integration engineering than estimated -> Mitigation: reserve contractor hours and prioritize onboarding backlog.
- Risk: communication fatigue -> Mitigation: keep Town Ticker short, use data-first updates and one ask per newsletter.

Open questions / unresolved items (notes-to-self):
- Do we commit to the public SLO language or keep it internal for now? (Stakeholders: Legal, Security, Product)
- Which 3 customer accounts are highest ROI to prioritize for Dedicated pilots? (Ask Sales to nominate by Feb 16).
- Should we allow manager-level exceptions to hiring freeze for one-off skills? (HR + CFO discussion)

Immediate next steps (owners):
- Diego: draft pilot runbook template and post to Confluence (due Feb 19).
- Ava: scope Optimize default profiles for top workloads; quick spike this week (due Feb 25).
- Maya (owner): finalize Town Ticker run-of-show and request slide submissions (due Feb 16).
- People Ops (Sofia): prep mid-cycle pulse survey draft (due Feb 20).

Appendix (working links & shorthand):
- Confluence canvas: https://confluence.redwood.company/display/OPS/90-Day+Alignment+Canvas
- Pilot readiness ticket: ENG-4812
- Hiring freeze exception thread: HR-124
- Slack channels: #ops-90-day-loom, #town-ticker-submissions

Quick facilitator checklist (one-pager):
- Collect slides 48h prior -> confirm 2 slides max per speaker
- Prepare metrics snapshot (SRE) -> p95 latency, incident count, error budget burn
- Post 2 action items at the end of the Town Ticker with owners & due dates
- Publish newsletter + recording within 24h

Notes to self (informal):
- Keep language simple for the company-wide comms; execs like crisp numbers + one big ask.
- Avoid overloading the all-hands with tactical engineering detail; surface those in the leadership sync.
- This doc will serve as the single source of truth for the 90-day ops window until replaced.

-- end of working draft --

(Version history: v0-1 initial draft, raw; expect edits from Diego/Ava/Sofia. Heavy on action owners; will prune to a one-pager for the all-hands email.)

title: KMS implementation decision log and evidence pipeline
preview:
Purpose and scope:
This working doc captures decisions and operational notes for KMS/HSM/BYOK adoption, key rotation procedures, and how we produce tamper-evident evidence for audits and SIEM ingestion. Audience: security eng, infra, SRE, legal/compliance. Status: working draft, expect iterative updates and small experiments in the next 60 days.

High-level choices (decision log):
- Decision 2025-11-10: Use envelope encryption for all customer data at rest. Root (KEK) stored in managed HSM (cloud provider or dedicated HSM) and used only to wrap/unwrap data encryption keys (DEKs). Rationale: limits exposure of KEKs and allows lightweight rewrap on rotation.
- Decision 2025-11-18: Support BYOK for enterprise customers as a first-class flow (customer uploads key material into a dedicated HSM tenant or uses a wrapped KEK). Rationale: compliance requests and regulated customers required BYOK. Implementation notes in subsequent sections.
- Decision 2025-12-02: KEKs rotate annually by default; DEKs rotate on a 90-day cadence or when triggered by key compromise. Non-destructive rotation adopted (re-wrap DEKs rather than re-encrypt all objects immediately).

Threat & tradeoff summary:
- Self-managed HSM: most control, higher ops burden, slower onboarding.
- Provider-managed HSM: lower ops, integrated audit logs, potential exposure to provider operations.
- BYOK: increases compliance coverage but complicates key lifecycle (customer escrow, rotation coordination, and revocation flows).

Implementation notes: KMS / HSM architecture
- Key hierarchy: account-KEK (master key per tenant group) -> application KEK (per-product) -> DEKs (per-dataset/object).
- DEKs stored as metadata in object storage encrypted with application KEK. No plaintext keys persisted.
- For caches/redis: encrypt values with local DEKs; memcached payloads include key id for lookup.
- Backups: backup blobs contain rewrapped DEKs with their version metadata. Restore process consults KMS to unwrap.

RBAC v2 and separation of duties (ops model):
- Roles: kms-admin, kms-operator, kms-auditor, application-service, svc-account.
- kms-admin: can create KEKs, configure HSM cluster, assign roles. Limited to 2 individuals by policy and key ceremony.
- kms-operator: can rotate and rewrap DEKs, and trigger emergency rotation. No key export rights.
- kms-auditor: read-only access to KMS audit logs and evidence buckets, cannot perform crypto ops.
- application-service: runtime ability to request DEK unwrap for specific object ids; access limited by scope and policy conditions (time-limited tokens, IP constraints).
- Enforcement: IAM policies + RBAC v2 service that maps Logical Roles to cloud service accounts. All role grants require automated approval ticket in Jira and a retention record.

Audit log schema (authoritative internal mapping):
- We will emit structured audit events to stdout/cloud logging, then forward to SIEM after enrichment.
- Canonical fields we must include (one-line per field):
  timestamp: ISO8601 UTC
  event_id: unique UUID
  principal: principal id (svc-account or user)
  principal_type: human | service | external (BYOK)
  action: key.create | key.rotate | key.unwrap | key.wrap | key.delete | key.policy.change | key.access_granted | key.access_revoked
  resource_type: kms_key | key_version | policy | hsm_cluster
  resource_id: logical key id (tenant/app/key-id)
  key_version: v123 (if applicable)
  request_origin: aws_region / ip / hostname
  correlation_id: request tracing id from routing layer
  outcome: success | failure | partial
  outcome_code: numeric code for quick filtering
  actor_metadata: role=...;ticket=JIRA-...;approval=auto/manual
  raw_payload_digest: sha256 hex of request payload (for evidence linking)
  evidence_ref: s3://... or gs://... pointing to rotation manifest or signed artifact
  note: free text for human ops notes

Sample audit event (log text form, not machine-parseable here but follows the schema above):
  timestamp: 2026-01-12T18:23:44Z
  event_id: b6f3e2a4-7c1d-4f9a-9ea2-1f3d2c7a6d55
  principal: svc-account-data-processor
  principal_type: service
  action: key.unwrap
  resource_type: key_version
  resource_id: tenant-42/app-embed/key-a
  key_version: v2026-01-12-1
  request_origin: us-west2
  correlation_id: req-0a1b2c3d
  outcome: success
  outcome_code: 0
  actor_metadata: role=kms-operator;ticket=SEC-9123;approval=auto
  raw_payload_digest: da39a3ee5e6b4b0d3255bfef95601890afd80709
  evidence_ref: s3://redwood-compliance/kms-evidence/tenant-42/rotation-2026-01-12.json

Evidence automation: what we collect and how
- For every rotation event (automatic or manual) we produce a signed rotation manifest (json) that contains: event_id, key_id, old_version, new_version, principal, signatures (operator pubkeys), timestamp, rewrap_count, verification_checks (list). The manifest is signed by the rotation pipeline's private key and uploaded to the compliance bucket.
- Rotation pipeline steps (automated):
  1) Validate rotation schedule and preconditions (no pending critical incidents).
  2) Fetch keys and perform rewrap via HSM API.
  3) Run verification jobs that try to unwrap sample DEKs and perform checksum on selected objects.
  4) Generate manifest and sign with pipeline key.
  5) Push manifest and logs to compliance bucket and emit audit event referencing artifact.
  6) Trigger SIEM alert if any verification steps fail.
- Evidence retention: manifests and signed logs kept for 7 years in immutable storage with WORM enabled for regulated customers.

SIEM export & mapping notes:
- Forwarder will ship canonical audit events to SIEM (Splunk/ELK). We will use an enrichment step to add tenant context and risk-scoring.
- Sensitive fields redaction: principal metadata may contain emails; strip or hash PII before forwarding to lower-retention indexes. Full raw logs (with PII) live only in locked compliance bucket with strict ACL.
- Key SIEM alerts to implement: unauthorized key access attempt, repeated unwrap failures, key deletion without approved ticket, unapproved policy changes, HSM health anomalies.
- Example Splunk query (plain-text intent): count by outcome where action=key.unwrap and outcome=failure and timewindow=1h -> trigger pager if count > 5 for a single principal.

Operational playbooks (short):
- Normal rotation: ops pipeline does automatic rewrap, manifest created, QA verification green, ticket closed with evidence refs.
- Emergency rotation (compromise suspected):
  1) Create emergency ticket, annotate with incident id.
  2) Freeze all new unwrap requests by toggling policy to deny-list.
  3) Promote a backup KEK, rewrap DEKs, publish manifest with 'emergency' flag.
  4) Notify legal and customers as required by contract.
  5) Post-incident: forensic job to collect HSM attestation, audit logs, and signed manifests for evidence package.

Operational metrics to emit to Console and Compliance dashboards:
- rotations_per_day, rotations_per_tenant, rotation_success_rate, average_time_to_rotate, unwrap_latency, unauthorized_access_rate, audit_log_delivery_lag.

Compliance checklist (initial):
- HSM attestation records captured and stored.
- All key operations produce an audit event that contains evidence_ref.
- BYOK onboarding process includes escrow options and revocation guidance.
- RBAC v2 mapped to cloud IAM, with automated ticketing for any role grant.
- SIEM pipeline configured with redaction rules and retention tiers.

Implementation tasks and owners:
- Infra: implement rotation pipeline and signing keys (owner: Marco, due: 2026-01-31).
- SRE: integrate audit-forwarder into SIEM and test full flow (owner: Priya, due: 2026-02-14).
- Legal/Compliance: finalize retention policy and BYOK contract appendix (owner: Daniella, due: 2026-02-01).
- Product: define tenant UX for BYOK and emergency rotation notices (owner: Sasha, due: 2026-02-20).

Open questions / risks:
- How do we handle HSM vendor change mid-contract? Need cross-wrapped KEKs or a migration plan that maintains auditability.
- BYOK customer-supplied keys with unsupported algorithms: define minimal supported algorithms and fail-fast during onboarding.
- Long-tail restores: rewrap-on-restore vs on-access re-encryption cost analysis.

Next steps (short term):
- Prototype evidence manifest signer and upload flow (week 1).
- Add canonical audit emitter library to all KMS clients (week 2).
- Run tabletop for emergency rotation and evidence collection (week 3).

Notes to reviewers:
- This is intentionally prescriptive but pragmatic; feedback requested on rotation cadence defaults, retention windows, and BYOK escrow language.
- If you are an SRE or infra engineer and want to test the manifest signature verifier, see github:infra/kms-config#142 for the testing harness.

End of document.

- kms-operator: can rotate and rewrap DEKs, and trigger emergency rotation. No key export rights.
- kms-auditor: read-only access to KMS audit logs and evidence buckets, cannot perform crypto ops.
- application-service: runtime ability to request DEK unwrap for specific object ids; access limited by scope and policy conditions (time-limited tokens, IP constraints).
- Enforcement: IAM policies + RBAC v2 service that maps Logical Roles to cloud service accounts. All role grants require automated approval ticket in Jira and a retention record.

Audit log schema (authoritative internal mapping):
- We will emit structured audit events to stdout/cloud logging, then forward to SIEM after enrichment.
- Canonical fields we must include (one-line per field):
  timestamp: ISO8601 UTC
  event_id: unique UUID
  principal: principal id (svc-account or user)
  principal_type: human | service | external (BYOK)
  action: key.create | key.rotate | key.unwrap | key.wrap | key.delete | key.policy.change | key.access_granted | key.access_revoked
  resource_type: kms_key | key_version | policy | hsm_cluster
  resource_id: logical key id (tenant/app/key-id)
  key_version: v123 (if applicable)
  request_origin: aws_region / ip / hostname
  correlation_id: request tracing id from routing layer
  outcome: success | failure | partial
  outcome_code: numeric code for quick filtering
  actor_metadata: role=...;ticket=JIRA-...;approval=auto/manual
  raw_payload_digest: sha256 hex of request payload (for evidence linking)
  evidence_ref: s3://... or gs://... pointing to rotation manifest or signed artifact
  note: free text for human ops notes

Sample audit event (log text form, not machine-parseable here but follows the schema above):
  timestamp: 2026-01-12T18:23:44Z
  event_id: b6f3e2a4-7c1d-4f9a-9ea2-1f3d2c7a6d55
  principal: svc-account-data-processor
  principal_type: service
  action: key.unwrap
  resource_type: key_version
  resource_id: tenant-42/app-embed/key-a
  key_version: v2026-01-12-1
  request_origin: us-west2
  correlation_id: req-0a1b2c3d
  outcome: success
  outcome_code: 0
  actor_metadata: role=kms-operator;ticket=SEC-9123;approval=auto
  raw_payload_digest: da39a3ee5e6b4b0d3255bfef95601890afd80709
  evidence_ref: s3://redwood-compliance/kms-evidence/tenant-42/rotation-2026-01-12.json

Evidence automation: what we collect and how
- For every rotation event (automatic or manual) we produce a signed rotation manifest (json) that contains: event_id, key_id, old_version, new_version, principal, signatures (operator pubkeys), timestamp, rewrap_count, verification_checks (list). The manifest is signed by the rotation pipeline's private key and uploaded to the compliance bucket.
- Rotation pipeline steps (automated):
  1) Validate rotation schedule and preconditions (no pending critical incidents).
  2) Fetch keys and perform rewrap via HSM API.
  3) Run verification jobs that try to unwrap sample DEKs and perform checksum on selected objects.
  4) Generate manifest and sign with pipeline key.
  5) Push manifest and logs to compliance bucket and emit audit event referencing artifact.
  6) Trigger SIEM alert if any verification steps fail.
- Evidence retention: manifests and signed logs kept for 7 years in immutable storage with WORM enabled for regulated customers.

SIEM export & mapping notes:
- Forwarder will ship canonical audit events to SIEM (Splunk/ELK). We will use an enrichment step to add tenant context and risk-scoring.
- Sensitive fields redaction: principal metadata may contain emails; strip or hash PII before forwarding to lower-retention indexes. Full raw logs (with PII) live only in locked compliance bucket with strict ACL.
- Key SIEM alerts to implement: unauthorized key access attempt, repeated unwrap failures, key deletion without approved ticket, unapproved policy changes, HSM health anomalies.
- Example Splunk query (plain-text intent): count by outcome where action=key.unwrap and outcome=failure and timewindow=1h -> trigger pager if count > 5 for a single principal.

Operational playbooks (short):
- Normal rotation: ops pipeline does automatic rewrap, manifest created, QA verification green, ticket closed with evidence refs.
- Emergency rotation (compromise suspected):
  1) Create emergency ticket, annotate with incident id.
  2) Freeze all new unwrap requests by toggling policy to deny-list.
  3) Promote a backup KEK, rewrap DEKs, publish manifest with 'emergency' flag.
  4) Notify legal and customers as required by contract.
  5) Post-incident: forensic job to collect HSM attestation, audit logs, and signed manifests for evidence package.

Operational metrics to emit to Console and Compliance dashboards:
- rotations_per_day, rotations_per_tenant, rotation_success_rate, average_time_to_rotate, unwrap_latency, unauthorized_access_rate, audit_log_delivery_lag.

Compliance checklist (initial):
- HSM attestation records captured and stored.
- All key operations produce an audit event that contains evidence_ref.
- BYOK onboarding process includes escrow options and revocation guidance.
- RBAC v2 mapped to cloud IAM, with automated ticketing for any role grant.
- SIEM pipeline configured with redaction rules and retention tiers.

Implementation tasks and owners:
- Infra: implement rotation pipeline and signing keys (owner: Marco, due: 2026-01-31).
- SRE: integrate audit-forwarder into SIEM and test full flow (owner: Priya, due: 2026-02-14).
- Legal/Compliance: finalize retention policy and BYOK contract appendix (owner: Daniella, due: 2026-02-01).
- Product: define tenant UX for BYOK and emergency rotation notices (owner: Sasha, due: 2026-02-20).

Open questions / risks:
- How do we handle HSM vendor change mid-contract? Need cross-wrapped KEKs or a migration plan that maintains auditability.
- BYOK customer-supplied keys with unsupported algorithms: define minimal supported algorithms and fail-fast during onboarding.
- Long-tail restores: rewrap-on-restore vs on-access re-encryption cost analysis.

Next steps (short term):
- Prototype evidence manifest signer and upload flow (week 1).
- Add canonical audit emitter library to all KMS clients (week 2).
- Run tabletop for emergency rotation and evidence collection (week 3).

Notes to reviewers:
- This is intentionally prescriptive but pragmatic; feedback requested on rotation cadence defaults, retention windows, and BYOK escrow language.
- If you are an SRE or infra engineer and want to test the manifest signature verifier, see github:infra/kms-config#142 for the testing harness.

End of document.

title: Adoption Symphony — QBR Agenda, Facilitation Script & Prep Checklist
preview:
Purpose:
A compact, facilitator-first QBR script and prep checklist that centers adoption signals and the renewal ask in a single 45–60 minute conversation. Use when the customer is in a typical renewal window (90–180 days out) and we need to align execs, surface risks, and secure next-steps without forcing a hard commercial negotiation in the meeting itself.

Desired outcomes (pick 2–3):
- Shared view of adoption health and top 3 blockers
- Clear, timeboxed plan toward renewal milestones (PO issued, SOW signed, exec alignment)
- Commit to a follow-up cadence and owner for commercial ask

Pre-meeting checklist (owner: AM) — quick pass (48–72 hours before):
- Confirm attendee list and roles (exec sponsor, AM, product SME, technical lead). Flag any missing decision-makers.
- Attach one-pager: current usage + delta vs. target; highlight 3 wins and 2 regressions.
- Export: daily active user trend (30/90 day), key API latency P95, errors by route, and a 30-day cost delta (sheet snapshot).
- Bring latest NPS/CSAT snippets and top 5 support tickets by impact.
- If renewal within 120 days: have preliminary commercial options (term, seats, committed throughput) in notes; do NOT lead with pricing in the first 10 minutes.

Participants & Roles:
- Facilitator (AM) — own meeting flow, timebox, read signals, ask for decisions.
- Co-pilot (Customer Success Ops) — has metrics, can pull dashboard live, capture action items.
- Product SME — answers technical feasibility and roadmaps.
- Executive sponsor (internal or customer) — visible alignment, able to escalate.

45-minute template agenda (timings flexible):
1) 0:00–0:05 — 1-line opener + mutual context (Facilitator)
   Script: "Thanks all — quick round: who’s joining and the one thing you need from this session? I’ll timebox us to 45 minutes and share next steps at the end."
2) 0:05–0:12 — Adoption highlights + one-pager walk (Co-pilot)
   Script prompts: "Top 3 wins since last QBR, plus two areas where adoption is below expectations. Why does the second regression matter to your business?"
3) 0:12–0:24 — Customer use-case spotlight (Customer or AM)
   Facilitation cue: "Can you walk us through a recent customer-facing flow that depends on our inference? What changed compared to your success criteria?"
4) 0:24–0:34 — Risk triage & unblock conversation (Facilitator + Product SME)
   Structure: list the top 3 risk signals (usage drop, latency regressions, missing integrations). For each: root cause hypothesis, short-term mitigation, owner, ETA.
5) 0:34–0:40 — Renewal-readiness check (Facilitator)
   Script: "We’re X days from the renewal window. Based on adoption and blockers discussed, here are 2 proposed paths: A — 12 month renewal with committed throughput; B — 6 month extension + focused adoption sprint. Which aligns with your priorities?"
6) 0:40–0:45 — Decisions & immediate next steps (Facilitator)
   Deliverables: action owners, deadlines, who will own the commercial follow-up.

Facilitation tips (shortcuts for running tight calls):
- Use the whiteboard to capture 1-line risks; convert each risk into a single owner + next action during the meeting.
- If conversation drifts into pricing, acknowledge and park: "Good topic — can we table specifics and agree who from your team will take the follow-up?"
- Call out the energy: if quiet execs, ask: "Is there a blocker we haven’t surfaced? If not, do we have executive alignment for the proposed path?"

Risk signals cheat sheet (visible in meeting):
- Usage: 15% QoQ DAU decline or churn of top 3 API keys
- Reliability: P95 latency +30% vs last Q; repeated timeouts in the last 30 days
- Product fit: >2 stalled integration projects or feature toggles off for >14 days

Renewal conversation templates (do not read verbatim; adapt):
- Soft framing: "We want to make this simple and aligned to your roadmap. If the adoption trajectory looks like X, recommended term is Y with these commitments. We’ll follow up within 3 business days with the formal options."
- Harder ask (when execs aligned): "Can we agree to a commercial path and a kick-off for the adoption sprint by EOD next Tuesday so we can secure capacity?"

Post-meeting follow-up checklist (Co-pilot & AM — within 48 hours):
- Send meeting notes and 1-page owner/action table.
- Attach export of the one-pager metrics and the short-term mitigation plan.
- Book the commercial follow-up with the right sales/ops reps (include deadlines).
- Create/update renewal tracker row: renewal date, decision owner, top 3 risks, proposed path, next milestone.

Renewal tracker row (text template to paste into tracker):
- Customer: <name>
- Renewal date: <YYYY-MM-DD>
- Current ARR: <value>
- Recommended path: <12m/6m/adoption-sprint>
- Owner: <AM>
- Top risks (1 line each): <risk1>; <risk2>; <risk3>
- Next milestone & ETA: <deliverable> by <date>

Quick operational notes to self (notes-to-self section — brief):
- Slack snippets to pull live charts: /dash usage <customer-id> /dash latency <customer-id>
- If product SME is late: start with highlights and call them in at 12 min for the risk triage.
- Keep a running list of support tickets referenced; attach ticket ids in the follow-up email.

Unresolved questions / TBDs (for the working doc):
- Is there a standard commercial matrix for customers with seasonal usage that reduces term length but includes throughput burst credits? (Action: CS Ops to propose options)
- Should we include a short customer-facing one-click renewal link for small accounts? (Action: Product Ops evaluation)

Appendix (short snippets):
- Example subject line for the invite: "QBR prep: adoption review + renewal-readiness — 45m"
- Example follow-up subject: "[Action required] QBR notes & next steps — renewal milestone"

End of doc.

Version notes: initial draft 2025-09-28; updated for condensed 45-minute format and renewal templates on 2026-02-15 by Aisha K. Sharma.

title: Half-year executive briefing — staff-readying checklist
preview:
Purpose:
This doc is a practical, itemized checklist for aligning leadership and staff before the half-year exec briefing. Treat as a living working doc: fill in owners, slide numbers, and rehearsal status. Goal: reduce last-minute churn and ensure consistent messages across teams.

Audience: all presenters, product leads, eng managers, SRE leads, comms, and people ops.

High-level timeline (week-of):
- D-14: confirm high-level story arc and required metrics (owner: Aisha)
- D-10: draft exec deck skeleton in GH/Drive (owner: Product Ops)
- D-7: finalize slides and hand off to comms for tone/edit (owner: Marissa)
- D-5: dry-run with leadership only (30–45m) (owner: CEO EA)
- D-3: full rehearsal w/ panel + backup messaging (owner: Ethan)
- D-1: slide lock + production checks (owner: Slide Gatekeeper)
- Day-of: on-call support starts 90 minutes before show (owner: SRE lead)

Run-of-show (45 minutes total):
- 0:00–0:05 — Welcome, safety, and context (CEO)
- 0:05–0:12 — Company health & key metrics (CFO/Product Ops)
- 0:12–0:25 — Product updates and live demo (Product + Demo owner)
- 0:25–0:33 — Roadmap/OKR refresh and launches (Product PM)
- 0:33–0:42 — Customer stories & GTM wins (GTM lead)
- 0:42–0:45 — Close + immediate next steps and CTAs (CEO)
- 0:45–1:15 — Extended cross-company AMA (optional; panel)

Pre-flight Checklist (required before D-3):
- Slides: final deck in drive, slide numbers pinned, speaker notes reviewed by comms. (owner, status)
- Metrics: verified numbers and query links (owner: Data Eng). Attach query IDs.
- Demos: smoke-tested on network/air-gapped env, minimal token use, fallback recording available. (owner: Demo owner)
- Accessibility: captions enabled for livestream, transcript plan in place. (owner: People Ops)
- Recording: recording and public excerpt policy confirmed. (owner: Legal)
- Backup slides: one-slide fallback per presenter with key takeaways (owner: each presenter)
- Spokes Q&A: consolidated Q&A prep doc with possible tricky questions + suggested answers (owner: Comms)

Decision Matrix (what needs exec sign-off before D-7):
- Any postponement of feature announcements — Exec sign-off required (owner: Product VP)
- PR/corporate comms embargo timing — Legal + Comms sign-off
- Customer names to publish — Sales/CS approval

Roles & POCs (quick list):
- Overall lead: Aisha Kapoor
- Slide gatekeeper: Marissa Gomez (final deck collator)
- Rehearsal coordinator: Ethan Park (schedules, tech checks)
- SRE On-call: Luca Bianchi (studio connectivity, livestream)
- Comms Q&A owner: Priya Nair (prepares messaging + FAQ)
- Data verifier: Data Eng SME (linked_artifacts should include query IDs)

Rehearsal checklist (D-3 full rehearsal):
- Tech check 30m before: mic, camera, slide advance handoff, streaming token tested
- Order run-through: each presenter times talk to +/- 15s
- Q&A practice: panel handles 4 predetermined hard questions
- Fallback drill: simulate demo failure -> swap to backup slide -> resume show
- Recording test: playback a 5m clip and verify audio/video quality

Comms templates (to adapt):
- Internal announcement (pre-read): short summary, why it matters, 3 asks for staff (read, RSVP, share questions)
- External blog teaser (post-brief): highlight 2 customer wins + 1 new capability (draft by Comms)
- Slack pulse message for engineering: 1–2 lines + link to summary deck and Q&A doc

Customer-facing considerations (check before public release):
- Any content with identifiable customers needs explicit opt-in from named customers (owner: Customer Success)
- Pricing/availability language must be cleared with Sales Ops and Legal

Risk register (quick hits + mitigations):
- Live demo failure -> mitigation: pre-recorded demo + short demo script (owner: Demo)
- Streaming outage -> mitigation: switch to recorded video and post summary + transcript (owner: SRE)
- Data metric dispute -> mitigation: show query source and timestamp; keep raw results accessible (owner: Data Eng)

Open questions (to resolve by D-7):
- Which 2 customer stories do we feature? (owner: CS)
- Final decision on cadence change announcement (tentative/firm?) (owner: Product)
- Are we including any new pricing detail in this briefing? (owner: Finance)

Post-brief actions (owners + SLA):
- Publish distilled 1-pager for org within 24h (owner: Comms)
- Update OKR tracking board with any mid-course corrections within 48h (owner: Product Ops)
- Customer follow-ups for featured accounts within 72h (owner: CS)

Appendix: suggested slide assignments (draft):
- Slide 1: Title + agenda (CEO)
- Slide 2: Company metrics snapshot (CFO)
- Slide 3–5: Product highlights and short demo (Product + Demo owner)
- Slide 6: OKR adjustments (Product Ops)
- Slide 7: Roadmap bullets (Product PM)
- Slide 8: GTM customer stories (GTM)
- Slide 9: Risks & ask list for teams (CEO + People Ops)

Quick hacks for lower friction rehearsal: (notes to rehearsal coordinator)
- Use a shared timing doc with each speaker’s target and a red/amber/green column
- Ask presenters to limit text on slides; use 1–2 bullets and a backup slide for details
- Run the show at 80% speed in the first full rehearsal to catch overrun hazards

Notes to self / things to track in this doc (working):
- Add query IDs for each metric (Data Eng) — currently missing for revenue by ARR %
- Demo pre-record location: drive:/demos/halfyear-v1.mp4 (confirm access)
- Confirm captioning vendor and budget (People Ops / Comms)

Immediate next steps (today):
- Aisha: confirm final story arc and top 3 messages by EOD
- Product Ops: seed slide skeleton in drive and tag presenters
- Comms: draft Slack + internal announcement templates for review

Owner checklist tickbox (copy this into each owner’s worksheet):
- [ ] I have viewed the final deck and confirmed my slide(s)
- [ ] I have a 2-line backup message in case demo fails
- [ ] I have done 1 timed run of my section and submitted timing to rehearsal coordinator

End of working checklist.

Revision history: v0-1 initial draft (Aisha); v0-2 will incorporate data queries and rehearsal slots.

- Slide 6: OKR adjustments (Product Ops)
- Slide 7: Roadmap bullets (Product PM)
- Slide 8: GTM customer stories (GTM)
- Slide 9: Risks & ask list for teams (CEO + People Ops)

Quick hacks for lower friction rehearsal: (notes to rehearsal coordinator)
- Use a shared timing doc with each speaker’s target and a red/amber/green column
- Ask presenters to limit text on slides; use 1–2 bullets and a backup slide for details
- Run the show at 80% speed in the first full rehearsal to catch overrun hazards

Notes to self / things to track in this doc (working):
- Add query IDs for each metric (Data Eng) — currently missing for revenue by ARR %
- Demo pre-record location: drive:/demos/halfyear-v1.mp4 (confirm access)
- Confirm captioning vendor and budget (People Ops / Comms)

Immediate next steps (today):
- Aisha: confirm final story arc and top 3 messages by EOD
- Product Ops: seed slide skeleton in drive and tag presenters
- Comms: draft Slack + internal announcement templates for review

Owner checklist tickbox (copy this into each owner’s worksheet):
- [ ] I have viewed the final deck and confirmed my slide(s)
- [ ] I have a 2-line backup message in case demo fails
- [ ] I have done 1 timed run of my section and submitted timing to rehearsal coordinator

End of working checklist.

Revision history: v0-1 initial draft (Aisha); v0-2 will incorporate data queries and rehearsal slots.

title: Early Embed Hiring Rubric and Shadowing Checklist
preview:
Purpose:
This doc is a compact, interviewer-friendly rubric + new-hire early-embed shadowing checklist intended for roles with cross-functional impact (infra, platform, SRE, devtools). Goal: reduce decision friction while preserving qualitative evidence capture and give new hires a 2-week shadowing plan that speeds role activation and alignment.

How to use this doc (quick):
- Interviewers: use the 5-dimension rubric below. Score 1-4, add 2–3 sentences of evidence per dimension shown.
- Hiring manager / TA: aggregate scores, read evidence, apply decision matrix. If split, schedule follow-up pair interview focused on the weakest dimension.
- Onboarding partner: once offer accepted, convert evidence notes into a personalized shadow plan.

Compact interview rubric (5 dimensions, weights in parentheses):
1) Problem-solving + system design (30%) -> Can the candidate decompose a real-world production inference problem? Look for: constraint tradeoffs, latency/cost thought, fallback strategies.
2) Execution & ownership (25%) -> Past examples of shipping, incident ownership, cross-team coordination. Signal: concrete timeline + outcome.
3) Technical depth (20%) -> Depth on one stack area: GPU infra, model serving, caching, or orchestration. Look for: concrete debugging examples, bottleneck analysis.
4) Communication & partnership (15%) -> Ability to explain tradeoffs to engineers and PMs; clarity in async docs. Signal: clear stepwise explanation, stakeholder-aware decisions.
5) Culture fit & learning orientation (10%) -> Growth mindset, feedback examples, pair work.

Scoring guidance (per dimension):
4 = Exceptional: concrete metrics/outcomes, led cross-team change; demo-level depth.
3 = Strong: solid examples, pragmatic tradeoffs, independent delivery.
2 = Mixed: some gaps in generalization or depth; requires coaching.
1 = Missed: lacks core signals for role.

Decision matrix (example):
- Average weighted score >= 3.25 -> strong hire.
- 2.6 <= score < 3.25 -> hire with conditions (mentoring plan & specific ramp milestones).
- score < 2.6 -> pass / re-run if specific gap can be validated.
Tie-breakers: evidence quality beats numeric tie; prioritize candidates who demonstrated cross-team communication and measurable outcomes.

Required interviewer notes (behavioral template):
- One-sentence summary of candidate (role-relevant).
- Top 2 strengths with evidence.
- Top 1 risk and suggested verification step.
- Recommendation: hire / hire-with-conditions / pass.

Fast shadowing checklist (first 10 business days):
Day 0 (before start): access check (GitHub, Slack, VPN, workspace), basic infra diagram + \"where to ask\" list, intro 1-pager from manager.
Day 1: orientation + team kickoff, buddy intro, read two product PRDs and the serving-runtime README.
Day 2: paired shadow on triage rotation (observe on-call notes, playbook entries), 30m with SRE lead about incident postmortem process.
Day 3: run local stack (dev env), reproduce a simple regression test, log issues.
Day 4: attend planning sync, demo how a request flows through routing and caching; write 1 short doc paragraph summarizing it.
Day 5: 1:1 with mentor to convert interview evidence into a prioritized 30-day plan.
Day 6-7: focused pairing on a small bugfix or safe improvement (low-risk triage), submit PR for review.
Day 8: shadow customer-support ticket triage to learn common user pain points.
Day 9: lead a short async post on Slack summarizing learning and next steps.
Day 10: alignment sync with manager, review of first PR & feedback, agree on 30-day milestones.

30-day ramp milestones (examples to turn into OKRs):
- Ship one non-user-facing improvement (perf, tests, infra).
- Lead one incident drill or playbook update.
- Complete pair rotations across two partner teams.

Hiring manager checklist (pre-offer):
- Confirm scoring alignment across interviewers, document a 2–3 line rationale for hire.
- Identify top 2 risks and mitigation plan (mentorship, training courses, initial tasks).
- Set preliminary 30-day milestones in offer package.

Calibration notes for People Ops/TA:
- Use this rubric as preferred for cross-functional infra roles until a formal discipline-specific rubric is adopted.
- Archive interviewer notes as single-line evidence for future calibration sessions.
- Run quarterly light calibration: pick 3 hires, compare rubric evidence vs. 90-day outcomes.

Appendix: quick templates (copyable)
Interviewer one-liner: \"(Name) — strong system thinker, deep in X, needs coaching on cross-team delivery; recommend hire-with-mentor. Evidence: led quantized rollout that improved p95 by 30% while reducing cost by 12%.\"
Risk mitigation example: \"If candidate lacks service-level scale experience, pair with SRE buddy, enroll in internal \"prod runbook\" onboarding, and set a 30-day incident drill completion milestone.\"

Open questions / TODOs:
- Decide whether to weight "technical depth" differently for non-engineering partner roles.
- Create a short interviewer training slide deck (2–3 slides) with examples.

Notes to self (Aisha): check in with Marco for calibration deck, schedule pilot with infra hires in Q1.

title: All-Staff 30/60/90 slate + rapid deck notes
preview:
Context and intent:

This is a working prep doc for the upcoming all-staff (target date: Mar 4) and the compact 30/60/90 slate that execs and managers can use for follow-ups. The goal: align the company on a tight set of priorities for the next 90 days, provide the exec update narrative, and surface risks + contingency plays. Use this for the quick deck (10 slides) and the run-of-show notes we hand to hosts.

High-level narrative (one-liner):
- Keep product momentum while investing in predictability: deliver Dedicated runway + Private onboarding, reduce per-token operating cost by 18% through batching/quantization playbooks, and raise SLOs for hosted API reliability to 99.95%.

Topline metrics to show (latest):
- Hosted API 7-day p50 latency (completion): 190ms (target 170ms)
- Token rate (hosted): +42% QoQ; unit cost trending down 8% QoQ after early batching changes
- Dedicated committed utilization: 68% (goal 80% after capacity reshuffle)
- Customer NPS (inference product): 46 (benchmark 50)

Draft OKRs (compact; these are the ones we push in the all-staff):
Objective A: Make Redwood the predictable inference platform customers can trust
- KR A1: Raise hosted API uptime SLO to 99.95% measured across 30 days (currently 99.88%)
- KR A2: Ship first-phase Dedicated autoscaling policy and burst capacity feature (GA in E2E test) - rollout 50% of customers
- KR A3: Release Private onboarding checklist and automated preflight (remove manual step X) to reduce onboarding time 30%

Objective B: Lower real per-token cost for production workloads while preserving quality
- KR B1: Ship two optimized quantization profiles and recommend flow in Redwood Optimize that reduce median cost/token by 12% for seq<=512 workloads
- KR B2: Improve caching effectiveness: increase prefix reuse hit rate from 11% to 26% on top-10 customers
- KR B3: Publish cost transparency report and per-route token cost breakdown in Console (beta)

Objective C: Increase product-led growth velocity with better dev experience
- KR C1: Shorten time-to-first-successful-request (signup -> first inference) from 22m to 8m (docs + SDK sample improvements)
- KR C2: Grow self-serve paid conversion 35% QoQ across targeted use-cases (chat + embeddings combo)
- KR C3: Ship 3 reference app templates for search, summarization, and personalization with full starter prompts and evals

All-hands run-of-show (10:00 - 11:00) - 60 minutes compact
- 10:00 - 10:05 Welcome + quick scoreboard (CEO) - 3 bullets: growth, uptime, cost improvement
- 10:05 - 10:15 Product & Roadmap snapshot (Head of Product) - top 3 launches: Dedicated autoscaler, Console cost-beta, Private preflight
- 10:15 - 10:25 Customer spotlight (CS lead) - 5 minute clip + 5 minute QA
- 10:25 - 10:35 Engineering deep-dive (serving-runtime lead) - where latency wins came from (kernels, batching), remaining hotspots
- 10:35 - 10:45 Ops & Reliability (SRE) - SLOs, incident response improvements, runbook adoption
- 10:45 - 10:55 Org priorities and 30/60/90 ask (Exec synthesis) - hiring hotspots, resourcing asks, one comms/ask per team
- 10:55 - 11:00 Rapid Q&A / close (CEO + COO)

Slides: quick deck notes (each slide 30-90s)
- Slide 1: Cover + all-hands theme: "Predictability > unpredictability"
- Slide 2: Scoreboard (metrics above) with small trend charts
- Slide 3: Customer story highlight (one quote + thumb video still)
- Slide 4: Product roadmap bullets (hosted + dedicated + private) - 3 rows: What, Who, Timing
- Slide 5: Reliability story: recent incident remediation + SLO progress
- Slide 6: Cost and Optimize: headline numbers + next actions for teams
- Slide 7: Org asks (hiring, shifts) - list of 6 fast asks with owners
- Slide 8: 30/60/90 compact plan: top 3 priorities per function (ENG, PRODUCT, CS, SRE, G2M)
- Slide 9: Risk scenarios + fallback ladder (capacity shortage, model-quality regression, major outage)
- Slide 10: Closing: what to do after (team huddles, post-all-hands doc + feedback form link)

30/60/90 compact slate (what each timeslice means, for managers to cascade)
- 30-day focus (stabilize): finish Dedicated autoscaler MVP, close two highest-severity infra bugs, ship Console cost-beta to beta customers, complete Private preflight automation spec
- 60-day focus (scale): onboard first wave Dedicated customers with autoscale, roll cost-beta to GA customers, run two cross-team load tests, finish reference app templates
- 90-day focus (optimize): measure and report unit economics improvements, iterate on quantization flows, bake SLOs into routing policy and fallback ladder

Decision points we need before the all-staff (owner and deadline):
- Do we show the real hosted API p99 latency numbers (sensitive) or p95 plus note? (Owner: Priya, due: 2026-02-18)
- Finalize customer clip and permissions for the all-staff (Owner: Maya, due: 2026-02-19)
- Approve the 6 org asks and who will own comms (Owner: Carlos, due: 2026-02-20)

Open risks and contingency plays (quick list):
- Risk: Dedicated capacity hit unexpectedly high (holiday burst) -> Play: enable short-term burst pool + auto-fallback to hosted variant with explicit consumer cost note
- Risk: Quantization causes generation quality regressions -> Play: gated rollout with eval hooks, automatic rollback on regressions detected via prompt test-suite
- Risk: Console beta surfaces incorrect cost allocation -> Play: revert to previous cost breakdown and push hotfix to fix token attribution

Communications checklist (pre and post all-hands):
- Pre: short teaser email (Exec comms) + RSVP form + invite top 100 customers to watch recording (CS)
- During: Slack #company-townline live thread + pinned transcript + ops channel standby
- Post: publish deck in Confluence, create short FAQ, request team huddles to document 30/60/90 commitments in team spaces

Follow-up actions (first pass):
- Ethan: finalize draft deck + hand to CEO for edits (due 2026-02-22)
- Priya: run p99 sensitivity review and prepare wording options (due 2026-02-18)
- Maya: final customer consent and clip ready (due 2026-02-19)
- Carlos: consolidate org asks and owners (due 2026-02-20)
- Lena: QA Console cost-beta demo and record video (due 2026-02-24)

Notes to self / rough ideas (informal):
- Keep slides VERY visual; ops loves numbers but front-load them to avoid long monologue
- Avoid jargon: "quantization" needs a one-line plain-English explanation + link to deeper doc for folks who want it
- Consider a 2-question pulse at end of all-hands: "Do you have enough clarity to act?" and "What single roadblock should execs triage?"

Appendix: short draft exec script bullets (CEO)
- Opening: thanks, quick score (growth + reliability + cost), one story about a customer who switched because of reliability
- Middle: product momentum (top 3 wins), engineering note on where latency came down, what remains
- Close: concrete asks (6 asks), where to find follow-ups and how we will measure impact, 2-week check-in cadence on all-hands commitments

Quick links (internal):
- Confluence draft deck: https://confluence.redwood.ai/display/ALL/All-Staff+Mar+2026
- Ops ticket for autoscale: https://jira.redwood.ai/browse/PD-919
- Cost-beta checklist: https://docs.redwood.ai/cost-beta/checklist

Unresolved questions (log):
- Should we surface org-level hiring numbers in the all-staff or keep it to teams? (Decision pending from People Ops)
- Which customers should be in the beta cohort list? (CS to propose top 12)

If you edit this doc: leave short notes at the top with initials and date. This is intended to be the single source of truth for the compact all-staff run-of-show and the 30/60/90 asks that teams will cascade.

End.

title: Reliability governance crosswalk + ops cadence (draft)
preview:
Purpose:
This working doc defines the cross-functional governance and sprint cadence for the Reliability Program (service-level objectives, monitoring, escalation, and runbook ownership). It focuses on aligning product, hosting, dedicated/private deployment teams, and SREs so SLOs become operational controls rather than just measurement artifacts.

High-level objectives:
- Codify who owns what for SLO lifecycle: definition, implementation, alerting, remediation, and post-incident improvements.
- Establish a repeatable monthly + quarterly ops cadence for SLO reviews, rollouts, and regressions.
- Create a lightweight governance matrix to authorize SLO changes (threshold shifts, new services, measurement changes) with minimal friction.
- Drive measurable reduction in sev2/3 recurrence through targeted SLO-linked playbooks.

Scope (what's in/out):
- In scope: API latency/error SLOs for hosted API, Dedicated ingress/throughput SLOs, critical control-plane SLOs for Private deployments (auth, provisioning), alert routing and ownership.
- Out of scope: non-production test-only metrics, infra capacity planning unrelated to SLO targets, customer billing metrics.

Key stakeholders and roles:
- SRE lead (Maya Thompson) — program owner, cadence chair, and escalation owner.
- Product-hosted-api (Priya Nair) — product SLO steward for hosted models, defines customer-facing objectives.
- Platform-engineering (Ethan Park) — implements measurement instrumentation and dashboards.
- Dedicated/private PM (Riley Gomez) — rollout owner for Dedicated and Private SLO variants.
- Solutions Eng / SE (Samir Patel) — customer impact liaison and runbook reviewer.

Governance matrix (decision flow):
- Minor SLO tuning (<=10% threshold change, no new alerts): Product + SRE lead sign-off via email + doc update.
- Major SLO change (new metric, >10% threshold change, affects SLAs): Product, SRE, and Legal contact + 2-week review window + stakeholder meeting.
- Emergency rollback: SRE lead authority to revert measurement logic or thresholds, notify stakeholders within 4 hours.

Cadence and rituals:
- Weekly (30m): SLO triage sync. Quick status from owners on active regressions, new alerts, and short-lived experiments.
- Monthly (1h): SLO health review. Present monthly SLI trends, incidents impacting SLOs, remediation backlog. Action items assigned.
- Quarterly (2h): SLO strategy and rollouts. Discuss new SLOs, deprecations, and policy changes. Reconcile to OKRs.
- On-call rotation review (bi-weekly): Ensure runbook coverage and alert noise reduction efforts.

Milestones (next 3 months):
- 2026-02-20: Baseline inventory complete for top-20 services and current SLOs (owner: Ethan).
- 2026-03-05: Instrumentation gaps triage finished and PR backlog created (owner: Platform Eng).
- 2026-03-22: First monthly SLO health review published, with prioritized remediation list and owners.
- 2026-04-15: Governance template and approval checklist added to Confluence; pilot SLO change review for Hosted API.

Sprint-level plan (sample 2-week sprint):
- Sprint goal: Reduce false-positive alerts on hosted-api 95th-latency SLO by 30%.
- Tasks:
  - Investigate top alert signatures and add histogram bucketing (Ethan) — 3d.
  - Implement adaptive alert suppression for maintenance windows (SRE) — 2d.
  - Add customer-impact annotation to events (Samir) — 2d.
  - QA dashboards & sanity checks (Priya) — 1d.
  - Post-deploy monitoring and rollback plan (Maya) — ongoing.

Runbook snippets (to be expanded):
- SLO breach initial triage:
  1) Confirm measurement accuracy vs KPI dashboards (Platform Eng).
  2) Check recent deploys/rollouts for correlated changes (Product & Release Eng).
  3) If customer-impacting, open incident, escalate to on-call and notify CS and SE.
  4) Apply mitigation: traffic reroute, scale-up, or temporary threshold loosening (SRE lead).
  5) Post-incident: assign RCA and SLO regression task, track under RELINF JIRA epic.

Telemetry and measurement notes:
- Prefer percentiles (p50/p95/p99) plus error-rate windows over raw averages.
- Use rolling windows aligned with customer-facing SLAs (1m/5m/1h) and store both raw and aggregated values.
- All SLO definitions must include measurement source, query, and owner in Confluence.

Open questions / action items (todo list):
- [ ] Decide canonical SLI for model-serving (latency vs tokens/time) — Action: Priya by 2026-02-19.
- [ ] Finalize alert severity mapping to on-call playbooks — Action: Maya + SRE on-call by 2026-02-26.
- [ ] Author a short customer-facing SLO FAQ for Sales and CS — Action: Samir by 2026-03-01.

Risks and mitigations:
- Risk: Measurement inconsistencies across Dedicated vs Hosted variants. Mitigation: enforce shared query templates and a compatibility shim library maintained in platform repo.
- Risk: Governance slowness causing missed improvements. Mitigation: keep minor changes lightweight and codify emergency rollback path.

Communication plan:
- Weekly notes posted to #reliability-program slack channel.
- Monthly digest emailed to product leads, infra, and CS.
- Quarterly readout in all-hands for engineering leadership with slides and trend snapshots.

Decision log (working):
- 2025-11-10: Agreed to use p95 for user-perceived latency baseline for Hosted API. (owner: Priya).
- 2025-12-02: Adopted governance matrix with 2-week review minimum for major changes. (owner: Maya).

Next steps (immediate):
- Complete baseline inventory (Ethan, due 2026-02-20).
- Create RELINF epic and move instrumentation tasks into sprint board (Maya).
- Draft customer-facing FAQ and share with Samir for review (Priya).

Notes to self / rough ideas:
- Consider a light-weight SLO dashboard template per product vertical so each PM can onboard in <1 day.
- Explore automated SLO-change testing using shadow traffic in staging before production rollouts.
- Keep a small "guardrail" SLA for Dedicated customers that maps to burst capacity policies.

References and links (in-doc):
- Confluence page: INF-RELIABILITY-CROSSWALK (linked).
- GitHub: reliability instrumentation helpers (PR #256).
- JIRA epic: RELINF-4123 (program tracking).

text:
Purpose:
This working doc defines the cross-functional governance and sprint cadence for the Reliability Program (service-level objectives, monitoring, escalation, and runbook ownership). It focuses on aligning product, hosting, dedicated/private deployment teams, and SREs so SLOs become operational controls rather than just measurement artifacts.

High-level objectives:
- Codify who owns what for SLO lifecycle: definition, implementation, alerting, remediation, and post-incident improvements.
- Establish a repeatable monthly + quarterly ops cadence for SLO reviews, rollouts, and regressions.
- Create a lightweight governance matrix to authorize SLO changes (threshold shifts, new services, measurement changes) with minimal friction.
- Drive measurable reduction in sev2/3 recurrence through targeted SLO-linked playbooks.

Scope (what's in/out):
- In scope: API latency/error SLOs for hosted API, Dedicated ingress/throughput SLOs, critical control-plane SLOs for Private deployments (auth, provisioning), alert routing and ownership.
- Out of scope: non-production test-only metrics, infra capacity planning unrelated to SLO targets, customer billing metrics.

Key stakeholders and roles:
- SRE lead (Maya Thompson) — program owner, cadence chair, and escalation owner.
- Product-hosted-api (Priya Nair) — product SLO steward for hosted models, defines customer-facing objectives.
- Platform-engineering (Ethan Park) — implements measurement instrumentation and dashboards.
- Dedicated/private PM (Riley Gomez) — rollout owner for Dedicated and Private SLO variants.
- Solutions Eng / SE (Samir Patel) — customer impact liaison and runbook reviewer.

Governance matrix (decision flow):
- Minor SLO tuning (<=10% threshold change, no new alerts): Product + SRE lead sign-off via email + doc update.
- Major SLO change (new metric, >10% threshold change, affects SLAs): Product, SRE, and Legal contact + 2-week review window + stakeholder meeting.
- Emergency rollback: SRE lead authority to revert measurement logic or thresholds, notify stakeholders within 4 hours.

Cadence and rituals:
- Weekly (30m): SLO triage sync. Quick status from owners on active regressions, new alerts, and short-lived experiments.
- Monthly (1h): SLO health review. Present monthly SLI trends, incidents impacting SLOs, remediation backlog. Action items assigned.
- Quarterly (2h): SLO strategy and rollouts. Discuss new SLOs, deprecations, and policy changes. Reconcile to OKRs.
- On-call rotation review (bi-weekly): Ensure runbook coverage and alert noise reduction efforts.

Milestones (next 3 months):
- 2026-02-20: Baseline inventory complete for top-20 services and current SLOs (owner: Ethan).

- Tasks:

title: Collective Velocity: 30/60/90 Compact and All-Hands Runbook (draft)
preview:
PURPOSE

A compact planning artifact to align the company on the next 90 days of work (30/60/90 cadence), the set of proposed company-level OKRs for exec review, and the all-hands agenda + presenter notes for the town. This is intentionally lightweight — a single doc for execs and facilitators to run from during the next staging and rehearsal cycle.

TL;DR
- Proposed company OKRs (draft) — 3 objectives, each with 2–4 measurable KRs.
- All-hands format: 40m exec updates + 20m Q&A + 20m breakout demos/pulse checks.
- Communication timeline and rehearsal plan for week of Feb 16.
- Primary risks and mitigations + clear owner list for follow-ups.

CONTEXT / WHY NOW
- We need a tighter cadence between product bet alignment, infra capacity prep (Dedicated & Private lanes), and customer-facing SLAs.
- Recent spikes in hosted usage during experiments highlighted routing fallback gaps (see JIRA-1873).
- Execs want a short, decisive set of OKRs to cascade into Q2 planning and resource commitments.

PROPOSED COMPANY-LEVEL OKRs (DRAFT)
Objective A: Make inference predictable and cost-efficient for production workloads
- KR A1: 20% reduction in median per-token CPU/GPU cost across top 10 customer workloads (benchmarked).
- KR A2: Decrease 95th percentile serving latency by 15% for our hosted API across p50-p95 windows.
- KR A3: Ship two new Optimize recommendations (auto-batching/caching presets) and measure adoption in 8 pilot customers.

Objective B: Expand enterprise readiness and Private deployment adoption
- KR B1: Close three Paid Private pilots (VPC/on-prem) with documented deployment runbooks and SLAs.
- KR B2: Audit and remediate top 10 compliance items for Private (KMS, audit logs) — evidence in compliance tracker.
- KR B3: Reduce onboarding time for Dedicated customers from signup to first successful inference job by 30%.

Objective C: Developer experience and ecosystem velocity
- KR C1: Ship language SDK parity (feature gap < 5%) across Python/TS/Go with updated examples.
- KR C2: Cut average time to reproduce a customer incident (first repro in SLO timeframe) by 40%.
- KR C3: Publish two reference apps that demonstrate cost-effective serving patterns, with benchmarks.

NOTES ON METRICS & ASSUMPTIONS
- Cost/KR measurements will use the billing baseline from Jan 2026 and the infra telemetry bucket tagged \"okrs-bench-v1\".
- Latency baseline gathered from hosted-api/perf-dashboard; small sample variance expected across regions.
- Adoption pilot = >3 customers integrating optimize presets into a production route for >= 7 days.

ROLL-OUT & COMMUNICATION TIMELINE
- Feb 12: Finalize draft OKRs with exec edits (this doc).
- Feb 15: Pre-brief managers + leads; send pre-read to all staff. (Owners: Ava / Marco)
- Feb 17: Exec rehearsal (30m) + Town run-through (facilitators). (Owners: Priya / Samir)
- Feb 19: Company all-hands + public blog mini-post summarizing top-level OKRs and asks. (Owner: Elena)
